structures.desc: Describing ongoing past actions with was/were + -ing, including contrast with past simple for interrupted or simultaneous actions.

rules.t: Form the past continuous with was/were + verb-ing. Use 'was' with I/he/she/it and 'were' with you/we/they.
rules.td: Man bildet die Verlaufsform der Vergangenheit mit was/were + Verb-ing. 'was' steht bei I/he/she/it und 'were' bei you/we/they.

ex.en: I was watching TV at 8 o'clock.
ex.de: Ich sah um 8 Uhr fern.

ex.en: They were playing football in the park.
ex.de: Sie spielten im Park Fußball.

rules.t: Negative: wasn't/weren't + -ing. Questions: Was/Were + subject + -ing?
rules.td: Verneinung: wasn't/weren't + -ing. Fragen: Was/Were + Subjekt + -ing?

ex.en: She wasn't sleeping when I called.
ex.de: Sie schlief nicht, als ich anrief.

ex.en: Were you listening to music?
ex.de: Hast du Musik gehört?

rules.t: Use the past continuous for a longer background action and the past simple for a short interrupting action. Connect them with 'when' or 'while'.
rules.td: Man verwendet die Verlaufsform für die längere Hintergrundhandlung und das Simple Past für die kurze, unterbrechende Handlung. Verbunden mit 'when' oder 'while'.

ex.en: I was walking to school when I saw a cat.
ex.de: Ich ging gerade zur Schule, als ich eine Katze sah.

ex.en: While she was sleeping, the phone rang.
ex.de: Während sie schlief, klingelte das Telefon.

affirmative: I was watching TV.
They were playing football.
She was reading a book.
negative: I wasn't sleeping.
They weren't listening.
She wasn't working.
questions: Were you watching TV?
Was she reading?
What were they doing?

errs.d: Using the wrong form of 'be' (was/were mismatch)
errs.w: I were walking to school.
errs.c: I was walking to school.

errs.d: Missing the -ing ending on the main verb
errs.w: She was walk down the street.
errs.c: She was walking down the street.

errs.d: Using past continuous for habitual past actions instead of past simple
errs.w: I was going to school every day last year.
errs.c: I went to school every day last year.

errs.d: Using past continuous for both the background action and the interrupting event
errs.w: I was walking when I was seeing a cat.
errs.c: I was walking when I saw a cat.

structures.desc: Using when, before, after, while, during, until, and by the time to connect clauses and express time relationships in narratives.

rules.t: 'While' is followed by a clause (subject + verb): While I was sleeping. 'During' is followed by a noun phrase: During the storm. They are NOT interchangeable.
rules.td: 'While' steht vor einem Satz (Subjekt + Verb): While I was sleeping. 'During' steht vor einer Nominalphrase: During the storm. Sie sind NICHT austauschbar.

ex.en: While they were climbing, it started to rain.
ex.de: Während sie kletterten, begann es zu regnen.

ex.en: During the storm, they stayed inside.
ex.de: Während des Sturms blieben sie drinnen.

rules.t: 'When' introduces a time clause. 'Before' means earlier than. 'After' means later than. All are followed by a clause.
rules.td: 'When' leitet einen Zeitsatz ein. 'Before' bedeutet früher als. 'After' bedeutet später als. Alle stehen vor einem Satz.

ex.en: When the storm started, we ran inside.
ex.de: Als der Sturm begann, liefen wir hinein.

ex.en: Before she left, she packed her bags.
ex.de: Bevor sie ging, packte sie ihre Taschen.

ex.en: After the rain stopped, they continued walking.
ex.de: Nachdem der Regen aufhörte, gingen sie weiter.

rules.t: 'Until' means up to the point when. 'By the time' means before or when (often implying something was too late).
rules.td: 'Until' bedeutet bis zu dem Zeitpunkt. 'By the time' bedeutet bis/als (oft mit der Bedeutung, dass etwas zu spät war).

ex.en: They waited until the rain stopped.
ex.de: Sie warteten, bis der Regen aufhörte.

ex.en: By the time they arrived, it was dark.
ex.de: Als sie ankamen, war es dunkel.

affirmative: When the storm started, we ran inside.
While they were climbing, it rained.
During the storm, they stayed inside.
negative: They didn't leave until the rain stopped.
Before she left, she didn't forget her bag.
questions: What happened when the storm started?
What were you doing during the break?

errs.d: Using 'during' with a clause or 'while' with a noun phrase
errs.w: During I was sleeping, the phone rang.
errs.c: While I was sleeping, the phone rang.

errs.d: Using 'while' before a noun phrase instead of 'during'
errs.w: While the storm, we stayed inside.
errs.c: During the storm, we stayed inside.

errs.d: Dropping parts of 'by the time' (writing just 'by' or 'by time')
errs.w: By they arrived, it was dark.
errs.c: By the time they arrived, it was dark.

structures.desc: Expressing how long something takes using the construction It + take(s) + person + time + to + infinitive.

rules.t: Present: It takes + person + time + to + base verb. The subject is always 'it'.
rules.td: Präsens: It takes + Person + Zeit + to + Grundform. Das Subjekt ist immer 'it'.

ex.en: It takes me twenty minutes to walk to school.
ex.de: Ich brauche zwanzig Minuten, um zur Schule zu gehen.

ex.en: It takes her an hour to do her homework.
ex.de: Sie braucht eine Stunde für ihre Hausaufgaben.

rules.t: Past: It took + person + time + to + base verb. Questions: How long does it take (you) to...? / How long did it take to...?
rules.td: Vergangenheit: It took + Person + Zeit + to + Grundform. Fragen: How long does it take (you) to...? / How long did it take to...?

ex.en: It took us two hours to drive there.
ex.de: Wir brauchten zwei Stunden, um dorthin zu fahren.

ex.en: How long does it take you to get to school?
ex.de: Wie lange brauchst du zur Schule?

affirmative: It takes me 20 minutes to walk to school.
It took us two hours to drive there.
negative: It doesn't take long to get there.
It didn't take me long to finish.
questions: How long does it take you to get to school?
How long did it take to fly there?

errs.d: Missing 'to' before the infinitive
errs.w: It takes me 20 minutes walk to school.
errs.c: It takes me 20 minutes to walk to school.

errs.d: Using a person as the subject instead of 'it'
errs.w: I take 20 minutes to walk to school.
errs.c: It takes me 20 minutes to walk to school.

errs.d: Using wrong tense of 'take' for past context
errs.w: It takes me two hours to get there yesterday.
errs.c: It took me two hours to get there yesterday.

structures.desc: Intensifying comparisons with much/a lot/a bit/a little + comparative, and using (not) nearly as...as for nuanced comparisons.

rules.t: Use 'much' or 'a lot' before a comparative to show a BIG difference. Use 'a bit' or 'a little' to show a SMALL difference.
rules.td: Man verwendet 'much' oder 'a lot' vor dem Komparativ für einen GROSSEN Unterschied. 'A bit' oder 'a little' zeigt einen KLEINEN Unterschied.

ex.en: Elephants are much bigger than dogs.
ex.de: Elefanten sind viel größer als Hunde.

ex.en: She is a bit taller than her sister.
ex.de: Sie ist ein bisschen größer als ihre Schwester.

rules.t: 'Not nearly as...as' means there is a big difference. 'Nearly as...as' means almost the same.
rules.td: 'Not nearly as...as' bedeutet, es gibt einen grossen Unterschied. 'Nearly as...as' bedeutet fast gleich.

ex.en: A cat is not nearly as dangerous as a lion.
ex.de: Eine Katze ist bei weitem nicht so gefährlich wie ein Löwe.

ex.en: He is nearly as tall as his father.
ex.de: Er ist fast so gross wie sein Vater.

rules.t: 'Fewer' is used with countable nouns (fewer people, fewer cars). 'Less' is used with uncountable nouns (less water, less time).
rules.td: 'Fewer' steht bei zählbaren Nomen (fewer people, fewer cars). 'Less' steht bei nicht zählbaren Nomen (less water, less time).

ex.en: There are fewer students in our class than in yours.
ex.de: Es gibt weniger Schüler in unserer Klasse als in eurer.

ex.en: I have less time than yesterday.
ex.de: Ich habe weniger Zeit als gestern.

affirmative: Elephants are much bigger than dogs.
She is a lot more interesting than him.
He is a bit taller.
negative: A cat is not nearly as dangerous as a lion.
This isn't much more expensive.
questions: Is a cheetah much faster than a lion?
Are there fewer cars today?

errs.d: Using 'more' together with the -er form (double comparative)
errs.w: She is much more bigger than her sister.
errs.c: She is much bigger than her sister.

errs.d: Putting the intensifier after the comparative instead of before
errs.w: He is taller much than me.
errs.c: He is much taller than me.

errs.d: Using 'less' with countable nouns instead of 'fewer'
errs.w: There are less people in the park today.
errs.c: There are fewer people in the park today.

structures.desc: Expressing real possibilities and likely outcomes with If + present simple, will + base verb.

rules.t: Structure: If + present simple, ... will + base verb. The if-clause uses the PRESENT tense, NOT 'will'. Use a comma when the if-clause comes first.
rules.td: Struktur: If + Simple Present, ... will + Grundform. Der if-Satz verwendet die GEGENWART, NICHT 'will'. Beistrich, wenn der if-Satz zuerst kommt.

ex.en: If you study hard, you will pass the exam.
ex.de: Wenn du fleissig lernst, wirst du die Prüfung bestehen.

ex.en: You'll have bad luck if you walk under a ladder.
ex.de: Du wirst Pech haben, wenn du unter einer Leiter durchgehst.

rules.t: Negative forms: If you don't study, you won't pass. Both the if-clause and result clause can be negative.
rules.td: Verneinung: If you don't study, you won't pass. Sowohl der if-Satz als auch der Ergebnissatz können verneint sein.

ex.en: If it doesn't rain, we'll go to the park.
ex.de: Wenn es nicht regnet, gehen wir in den Park.

ex.en: If you don't hurry, you won't catch the bus.
ex.de: Wenn du dich nicht beeilst, wirst du den Bus nicht erwischen.

rules.t: 'Unless' means 'if...not'. Use unless + affirmative verb (NOT unless + negative — that would be a double negative).
rules.td: 'Unless' bedeutet 'wenn... nicht'. Man verwendet unless + bejahtes Verb (NICHT unless + Verneinung — das wäre eine doppelte Verneinung).

ex.en: Unless you hurry, you'll be late.
ex.de: Wenn du dich nicht beeilst, wirst du zu spät kommen.

ex.en: I'll go, unless it rains.
ex.de: Ich werde gehen, es sei denn, es regnet.

affirmative: If you study hard, you'll pass.
If it rains, we'll stay inside.
You'll be late if you don't hurry.
negative: If you don't study, you won't pass.
Unless you hurry, you won't catch the bus.
questions: What will you do if it rains?
Will you come if I invite you?

errs.d: Using 'will' in the if-clause instead of present simple
errs.w: If I will go to London, I will visit Big Ben.
errs.c: If I go to London, I will visit Big Ben.

errs.d: Using present continuous instead of present simple in the if-clause
errs.w: If it is raining tomorrow, we'll stay inside.
errs.c: If it rains tomorrow, we'll stay inside.

errs.d: Using a negative verb after 'unless' (double negative)
errs.w: Unless you don't hurry, you'll be late.
errs.c: Unless you hurry, you'll be late.

structures.desc: Using 'unless' as a synonym for 'if...not' in conditional sentences, always followed by an affirmative verb.

rules.t: 'Unless' = 'if...not'. Transform: 'If you don't hurry' = 'Unless you hurry'. The verb after 'unless' is ALWAYS affirmative.
rules.td: 'Unless' = 'wenn... nicht'. Umformung: 'If you don't hurry' = 'Unless you hurry'. Das Verb nach 'unless' ist IMMER bejahend.

ex.en: Unless it rains, we'll go to the park.
ex.de: Wenn es nicht regnet, gehen wir in den Park.

ex.en: I won't go unless you come with me.
ex.de: Ich gehe nicht, es sei denn, du kommst mit.

rules.t: To transform if...not to unless: remove 'not/don't/doesn't' and replace 'if' with 'unless'. The meaning stays the same.
rules.td: Umformung von if...not zu unless: 'not/don't/doesn't' entfernen und 'if' durch 'unless' ersetzen. Die Bedeutung bleibt gleich.

ex.en: If you don't study, you'll fail. = Unless you study, you'll fail.
ex.de: Wenn du nicht lernst, wirst du durchfallen. = Es sei denn, du lernst, wirst du durchfallen.

ex.en: If she doesn't come, we'll start without her. = Unless she comes, we'll start without her.
ex.de: Wenn sie nicht kommt, fangen wir ohne sie an.

affirmative: Unless it rains, we'll go.
Unless you hurry, you'll be late.

errs.d: Adding a negative verb after 'unless'
errs.w: Unless you don't come, we'll start without you.
errs.c: Unless you come, we'll start without you.

errs.d: Using 'unless' where simple 'if' is needed (positive condition)
errs.w: Unless you study, you'll pass the exam.
errs.c: If you study, you'll pass the exam.

structures.desc: Combining sentences using defining relative clauses with who (people), which (things/animals), that (both), and whose (possession).

rules.t: Use 'who' or 'that' for people. Use 'which' or 'that' for things and animals. In defining relative clauses, 'that' can replace both.
rules.td: Man verwendet 'who' oder 'that' für Personen. 'Which' oder 'that' für Dinge und Tiere. In bestimmenden Relativsätzen kann 'that' beides ersetzen.

ex.en: The man who lives next door is a teacher.
ex.de: Der Mann, der nebenan wohnt, ist Lehrer.

ex.en: The book which I read was great.
ex.de: Das Buch, das ich gelesen habe, war toll.

rules.t: 'Whose' shows possession. It replaces his/her/its/their in the relative clause.
rules.td: 'Whose' zeigt Besitz an. Es ersetzt his/her/its/their im Relativsatz.

ex.en: The girl whose dog ran away is sad.
ex.de: Das Mädchen, dessen Hund weggelaufen ist, ist traurig.

ex.en: That's the boy whose father is a pilot.
ex.de: Das ist der Junge, dessen Vater Pilot ist.

rules.t: Defining relative clauses give essential information about the noun. They have NO commas. Without the relative clause, we don't know which person/thing is meant.
rules.td: Bestimmende Relativsätze geben wesentliche Informationen über das Nomen. Sie haben KEINE Beistriche. Ohne den Relativsatz wüssten wir nicht, welche Person/Sache gemeint ist.

ex.en: The woman who called you is my aunt.
ex.de: Die Frau, die dich angerufen hat, ist meine Tante.

ex.en: I want the shirt that is in the window.
ex.de: Ich will das Hemd, das im Schaufenster ist.

affirmative: The man who lives next door is a teacher.
The book which I read was great.
The girl whose dog ran away is sad.
negative: That's not the person who called me.
This isn't the film that I wanted to see.
questions: Is that the boy who won the race?
Which is the book that you read?

errs.d: Using 'what' as a relative pronoun instead of 'that' or 'which'
errs.w: The thing what I saw was amazing.
errs.c: The thing that I saw was amazing.

errs.d: Confusing 'whose' (possessive) with 'who's' (who is)
errs.w: The boy who's father is a pilot...
errs.c: The boy whose father is a pilot...

errs.d: Using 'which' for people or 'who' for things
errs.w: The teacher which helped me was nice.
errs.c: The teacher who helped me was nice.

structures.nd: Present Perfect mit for/since (Dauer und Zeitpunkt)
structures.desc: Using the present perfect with 'for' (duration) and 'since' (starting point) to talk about actions and states continuing to the present.

rules.t: 'For' is used with a DURATION — a period of time: for two years, for a long time, for ages, for six months.
rules.td: 'For' steht bei einer DAÜR — einem Zeitraum: for two years, for a long time, for ages, for six months.

ex.en: I've lived here for five years.
ex.de: Ich lebe seit fünf Jahren hier.

ex.en: She has been a teacher for a long time.
ex.de: Sie ist seit langem Lehrerin.

rules.t: 'Since' is used with a STARTING POINT — a specific point in time: since 2020, since last Friday, since Christmas, since I was seven.
rules.td: 'Since' steht bei einem ANFANGSPUNKT — einem bestimmten Zeitpunkt: since 2020, since last Friday, since Christmas, since I was seven.

ex.en: I've known her since 2015.
ex.de: Ich kenne sie seit 2015.

ex.en: We've been friends since we met at school.
ex.de: Wir sind Freunde, seit wir uns in der Schule kennengelernt haben.

rules.t: With for/since, you MUST use the present perfect (not present simple). The action started in the past and continues NOW.
rules.td: Mit for/since MUSS man das Present Perfect verwenden (nicht das Simple Present). Die Handlung begann in der Vergangenheit und dauert JETZT noch an.

ex.en: I have lived here for three years.
ex.de: Ich wohne seit drei Jahren hier.

ex.en: How long have you had your phone?
ex.de: Wie lange hast du schon dein Handy?

affirmative: I've lived here for five years.
She has known him since 2018.
We've been friends for ages.
negative: I haven't seen her since Monday.
He hasn't played football for months.
questions: How long have you lived here?
How long has she had that car?

errs.d: Confusing for (duration) and since (point in time)
errs.w: I've lived here since three years.
errs.c: I've lived here for three years.

errs.d: Using present simple instead of present perfect with for/since
errs.w: I live here for five years.
errs.c: I've lived here for five years.

errs.d: Using 'for' with a specific point in time
errs.w: I've known her for 2015.
errs.c: I've known her since 2015.

structures.nd: Simple Past vs Present Perfect (systematischer Vergleich)
structures.desc: Systematically contrasting past simple (finished actions at a specific time) with present perfect (present relevance, no specific time, for/since).

rules.t: Past simple signals: yesterday, last week, ago, in 2010, when I was young. Present perfect signals: ever, never, just, already, yet, for, since, always.
rules.td: Simple Past Signalwörter: yesterday, last week, ago, in 2010, when I was young. Present Perfect Signalwörter: ever, never, just, already, yet, for, since, always.

ex.en: I went to London last year. (past simple — specific time)
ex.de: Ich war letztes Jahr in London.

ex.en: I have been to London twice. (present perfect — no specific time)
ex.de: Ich war zweimal in London.

rules.t: Decision rule: Am I talking about WHEN something happened? Use past simple. Am I talking about THAT it happened or its relevance now? Use present perfect.
rules.td: Entscheidungsregel: Rede ich darüber, WANN etwas passiert ist? Simple Past. Rede ich darüber, DASS es passiert ist oder über die Relevanz jetzt? Present Perfect.

ex.en: When did you go there? — I went there in June. (PS)
ex.de: Wann warst du dort? — Ich war im Juni dort.

ex.en: Have you ever been there? — Yes, I have. (PP)
ex.de: Warst du schon mal dort? — Ja.

rules.t: for/since ALWAYS trigger present perfect (not past simple). These words indicate that the action continues to the present.
rules.td: for/since verlangen IMMER das Present Perfect (nicht Simple Past). Diese Wörter zeigen, dass die Handlung bis jetzt andauert.

ex.en: I've known her since 2020. (not: I knew her since 2020)
ex.de: Ich kenne sie seit 2020.

ex.en: He has worked here for ten years. (not: He worked here for ten years — unless he left)
ex.de: Er arbeitet seit zehn Jahren hier.

affirmative: I went to London last year.
I have been to London twice.
She has lived here since 2018.
negative: I didn't go yesterday.
I haven't been there yet.
She hasn't finished yet.
questions: When did you go?
Have you ever been to London?
How long have you lived here?

errs.d: Using present perfect with a specific past time marker
errs.w: I have been there yesterday.
errs.c: I was there yesterday.

errs.d: Using past simple with ever/never (experience questions)
errs.w: Did you ever go to London?
errs.c: Have you ever been to London?

errs.d: Using past simple with for/since
errs.w: I lived here since 2020.
errs.c: I've lived here since 2020.

structures.desc: Expressing permission and prohibition using be (not) allowed to + verb across present, past, and future tenses.

rules.t: Present: subject + am/is/are (not) allowed to + base verb. Used for rules and permissions from an authority.
rules.td: Präsens: Subjekt + am/is/are (not) allowed to + Grundform. Wird für Regeln und Erlaubnisse von einer Autorität verwendet.

ex.en: I'm not allowed to go out when it's dark.
ex.de: Ich darf nicht rausgehen, wenn es dunkel ist.

ex.en: We aren't allowed to use phones in class.
ex.de: Wir dürfen im Unterricht keine Handys benutzen.

rules.t: Past: subject + was/were (not) allowed to + base verb. Used for past rules and permissions.
rules.td: Vergangenheit: Subjekt + was/were (not) allowed to + Grundform. Für vergangene Regeln und Erlaubnisse.

ex.en: I wasn't allowed to stay up late when I was young.
ex.de: Ich durfte nicht lange aufbleiben, als ich klein war.

ex.en: Women were allowed to attend university from 1897.
ex.de: Frauen durften ab 1897 an der Universität studieren.

rules.t: Questions: Am/Is/Are + subject + allowed to...? Was/Were + subject + allowed to...?
rules.td: Fragen: Am/Is/Are + Subjekt + allowed to...? Was/Were + Subjekt + allowed to...?

ex.en: Are you allowed to have parties at home?
ex.de: Darfst du zuhause Partys machen?

ex.en: Were they allowed to study all subjects?
ex.de: Durften sie alle Faucher studieren?

affirmative: I'm allowed to go out.
She is allowed to use the computer.
They were allowed to leave early.
negative: I'm not allowed to go out.
We aren't allowed to use phones.
She wasn't allowed to stay up late.
questions: Are you allowed to have parties?
Were they allowed to vote?
Is he allowed to drive?

errs.d: Omitting the form of 'be' before 'allowed'
errs.w: I not allowed to go out.
errs.c: I'm not allowed to go out.

errs.d: Wrong form of 'be' (subject-verb agreement error)
errs.w: She are allowed to go.
errs.c: She is allowed to go.

errs.d: Confusing 'be allowed to' (permission) with 'can' (ability)
errs.w: I'm allowed to swim very well.
errs.c: I can swim very well.

structures.desc: Using let/don't let + object + base verb (without 'to') to express giving or withholding permission.

rules.t: Structure: subject + let(s) / don't/doesn't let + object + BASE VERB (no 'to'!). The person giving permission is the subject.
rules.td: Struktur: Subjekt + let(s) / don't/doesn't let + Objekt + GRUNDFORM (ohne 'to'!). Die Person, die die Erlaubnis gibt, ist das Subjekt.

ex.en: My parents let me go out.
ex.de: Meine Eltern lassen mich ausgehen.

ex.en: The teacher doesn't let us eat in class.
ex.de: Der Lehrer lässt uns nicht im Unterricht essen.

rules.t: Past: let stays the same (irregular — let/let/let). Negative past: didn't let + object + base verb.
rules.td: Vergangenheit: let bleibt gleich (unregelmaussig — let/let/let). Verneinte Vergangenheit: didn't let + Objekt + Grundform.

ex.en: She let me borrow her book yesterday.
ex.de: Sie hat mir gestern ihr Buch geliehen.

ex.en: They didn't let me go to the party.
ex.de: Sie haben mich nicht zur Party gehen lassen.

rules.t: let + object + base verb (no 'to') = be allowed to + base verb. 'They let me go' = 'I'm allowed to go.' Note: 'allow' takes 'to', but 'let' does NOT.
rules.td: let + Objekt + Grundform (ohne 'to') = be allowed to + Grundform. 'They let me go' = 'I'm allowed to go.' Achtung: 'allow' braucht 'to', aber 'let' NICHT.

ex.en: They let me stay. = I'm allowed to stay.
ex.de: Sie lassen mich bleiben. = Ich darf bleiben.

ex.en: She allows me to go. / She lets me go. (NOT: lets me to go)
ex.de: Sie erlaubt mir zu gehen. / Sie lässt mich gehen.

affirmative: My parents let me go out.
The teacher lets us use dictionaries.
She let me borrow it.
negative: They don't let me stay up late.
She doesn't let us eat in class.
They didn't let me go.
questions: Do your parents let you go out?
Did the teacher let you use your phone?

errs.d: Adding 'to' after let + object
errs.w: My parents let me to go out.
errs.c: My parents let me go out.

errs.d: Forgetting the -s in third person present (lets vs let)
errs.w: She let us go every Friday. (meaning present habitual)
errs.c: She lets us go every Friday.

errs.d: Mixing up let (no 'to') and allow (with 'to') structures
errs.w: They don't let me to go. / They don't allow me go.
errs.c: They don't let me go. / They don't allow me to go.

structures.nd: could / was able to (Fähigkeit in der Vergangenheit)
structures.desc: Expressing past ability with could (general ability) and was/were able to (specific one-time achievement), including the full tense paradigm of be able to.

rules.t: 'Could' is used for GENERAL past ability — things you were able to do over a period of time. 'Couldn't' works for both general and specific inability.
rules.td: 'Could' wird für ALLGEMEINE vergangene Fähigkeiten verwendet — Dinge, die man über einen Zeitraum konnte. 'Couldn't' funktioniert für allgemeine und spezifische Unfähigkeit.

ex.en: I could swim when I was five.
ex.de: Ich konnte schwimmen, als ich fünf war.

ex.en: She couldn't ride a bike until she was eight.
ex.de: Sie konnte nicht Rad fahren, bis sie acht war.

rules.t: 'Was/were able to' is used for SPECIFIC one-time achievements — something you managed to do on a particular occasion.
rules.td: 'Was/were able to' wird für SPEZIFISCHE einmalige Leistungen verwendet — etwas, das man bei einer bestimmten Gelegenheit geschafft hat.

ex.en: After trying many times, I was able to pass the exam.
ex.de: Nach vielen Versuchen konnte ich die Prüfung bestehen.

ex.en: The firefighters were able to rescue everyone.
ex.de: Die Feuerwehr konnte alle retten.

rules.t: 'Be able to' works in ALL tenses: present (am/is/are able to), past (was/were able to), future (will be able to), present perfect (have/has been able to).
rules.td: 'Be able to' funktioniert in ALLEN Zeiten: Präsens (am/is/are able to), Vergangenheit (was/were able to), Zukunft (will be able to), Present Perfect (have/has been able to).

ex.en: I will be able to come tomorrow.
ex.de: Ich werde morgen kommen können.

ex.en: She hasn't been able to finish yet.
ex.de: Sie hat es noch nicht fertigmachen können.

affirmative: I could swim when I was five.
She was able to pass the test.
He will be able to come.
negative: I couldn't open the door.
She wasn't able to finish.
They won't be able to help.
questions: Could you swim as a child?
Were you able to find it?
Will she be able to come?

errs.d: Using 'could' for a specific one-time achievement (affirmative only)
errs.w: I could pass the exam yesterday.
errs.c: I was able to pass the exam yesterday.

errs.d: Dropping 'be' from the 'be able to' construction
errs.w: I will able to come tomorrow.
errs.c: I will be able to come tomorrow.

errs.d: Using a non-base form after 'able to'
errs.w: She was able to swimming.
errs.c: She was able to swim.

structures.n: Past and Future Permission (was allowed to / will be allowed to)
structures.nd: Erlaubnis in Vergangenheit und Zukunft (was allowed to / will be allowed to)
structures.desc: Expressing permission across all tenses using was/were (not) allowed to (past), will (not) be allowed to (future), and have/has (not) been allowed to (present perfect).

rules.t: Past permission: was/were (not) allowed to + base verb. Used for rules and permissions that existed in the past.
rules.td: Vergangene Erlaubnis: was/were (not) allowed to + Grundform. Für Regeln und Erlaubnisse, die in der Vergangenheit galten.

ex.en: Women weren't allowed to vote until 1918.
ex.de: Frauen durften bis 1918 nicht wählen.

ex.en: We were allowed to leave early yesterday.
ex.de: Wir durften gestern früher gehen.

rules.t: Future permission: will (not) be allowed to + base verb. For rules and permissions in the future.
rules.td: Zukünftige Erlaubnis: will (not) be allowed to + Grundform. Für Regeln und Erlaubnisse in der Zukunft.

ex.en: Next year, we'll be allowed to stay out later.
ex.de: Nauchstes Jahr werden wir länger draußen bleiben dürfen.

ex.en: They won't be allowed to have a supermarket there.
ex.de: Sie werden dort kein Supermarkt haben dürfen.

rules.t: Present perfect permission: have/has (not) been allowed to + base verb. For permission relevant to the present.
rules.td: Present Perfect Erlaubnis: have/has (not) been allowed to + Grundform. Für Erlaubnis mit Gegenwartsrelevanz.

ex.en: Women haven't always been allowed to do what men have.
ex.de: Frauen durften nicht immer das tun, was Männer durften.

ex.en: I've never been allowed to have a pet.
ex.de: Ich durfte noch nie ein Haustier haben.

affirmative: They were allowed to leave.
She will be allowed to drive.
He has been allowed to stay.
negative: They weren't allowed to vote.
She won't be allowed to go.
I haven't been allowed to do that.
questions: Were you allowed to go?
Will we be allowed to stay?
Has she been allowed to leave?

errs.d: Omitting was/were from the past permission structure
errs.w: They allowed to go home early.
errs.c: They were allowed to go home early.

errs.d: Dropping 'be' from 'will be allowed to'
errs.w: We will allowed to stay out later.
errs.c: We will be allowed to stay out later.

errs.d: Wrong was/were agreement with plural subject
errs.w: They was allowed to leave.
errs.c: They were allowed to leave.

structures.nd: Zukunft und Present Perfect bei Fähigkeit/Erlaubnis
structures.desc: Completing the ability and permission system across all tenses: will be able to, will be allowed to, have been able to, have been allowed to.

rules.t: Future ability: will (not) be able to + base verb. Used because 'can' has no future form.
rules.td: Zukünftige Fähigkeit: will (not) be able to + Grundform. Wird verwendet, weil 'can' keine Zukunftsform hat.

ex.en: I will be able to drive when I'm 18.
ex.de: Ich werde Auto fahren können, wenn ich 18 bin.

ex.en: She won't be able to come to the party.
ex.de: Sie wird nicht zur Party kommen können.

rules.t: Present perfect ability: have/has (not) been able to + base verb. For ability with present relevance.
rules.td: Present Perfect Fähigkeit: have/has (not) been able to + Grundform. Für Fähigkeit mit Gegenwartsrelevanz.

ex.en: I haven't been able to finish my homework yet.
ex.de: Ich habe meine Hausaufgaben noch nicht fertigmachen können.

ex.en: She has been able to speak English since she was six.
ex.de: Sie kann seit ihrem sechsten Lebensjahr Englisch sprechen.

affirmative: I will be able to come.
She has been able to help.
They will be allowed to enter.
negative: He won't be able to finish.
I haven't been able to reach her.
We won't be allowed to go.
questions: Will you be able to come?
Have you been able to find it?
Will they be allowed to stay?

errs.d: Dropping 'be' from the complex verb chain
errs.w: I will able to come tomorrow.
errs.c: I will be able to come tomorrow.

errs.d: Confusing 'been' and 'be' in different tenses
errs.w: I have be able to finish.
errs.c: I have been able to finish.

errs.d: Using 'will can' instead of 'will be able to'
errs.w: I will can come tomorrow.
errs.c: I will be able to come tomorrow.

structures.nd: Present Perfect Continuous (Verlaufsform des Present Perfect)
structures.desc: Expressing ongoing activities with duration using have/has + been + -ing form, often with for/since, and contrasting with present perfect simple.

rules.t: Form: subject + have/has + been + verb-ing. Used for activities that have been going on for some time and are still continuing.
rules.td: Form: Subjekt + have/has + been + Verb-ing. Wird für Aktivitäten verwendet, die seit einiger Zeit andauern und noch weitergehen.

ex.en: I've been living here for six weeks.
ex.de: Ich wohne seit sechs Wochen hier.

ex.en: She has been working all morning.
ex.de: Sie arbeitet schon den ganzen Morgen.

rules.t: Present perfect continuous = focus on the ONGOING ACTIVITY. Present perfect simple = focus on the RESULT or completion. 'I've been reading all morning' (activity) vs 'I've read three books' (result).
rules.td: Present Perfect Continuous = Fokus auf die LAUFENDE AKTIVITÄT. Present Perfect Simple = Fokus auf das ERGEBNIS oder den Abschluss. 'I've been reading all morning' (Aktivität) vs 'I've read three books' (Ergebnis).

ex.en: I've been reading all morning. (emphasis on activity)
ex.de: Ich lese schon den ganzen Morgen. (Betonung auf Aktivität)

ex.en: I've read three books this month. (emphasis on result)
ex.de: Ich habe diesen Monat drei Bücher gelesen. (Betonung auf Ergebnis)

rules.t: Stative verbs (know, like, want, be, have) do NOT take the continuous form. Use present perfect simple: I've known her for years (NOT: I've been knowing her).
rules.td: Zustandsverben (know, like, want, be, have) verwenden NICHT die Verlaufsform. Present Perfect Simple: I've known her for years (NICHT: I've been knowing her).

ex.en: I've known her since primary school. (NOT: I've been knowing)
ex.de: Ich kenne sie seit der Volksschule.

ex.en: She has liked chocolate since she was a child. (NOT: has been liking)
ex.de: Sie mag Schokolade, seit sie ein Kind war.

affirmative: I've been living here for six weeks.
She has been studying all day.
We've been waiting for an hour.
negative: I haven't been sleeping well.
She hasn't been feeling well.
They haven't been practising enough.
questions: How long have you been living here?
Has she been working all day?
What have you been doing?

errs.d: Missing the -ing form after 'been'
errs.w: I have been live here for six weeks.
errs.c: I have been living here for six weeks.

errs.d: Omitting 'been' from the construction
errs.w: I have living here for six weeks.
errs.c: I have been living here for six weeks.

errs.d: Using present perfect continuous with stative verbs
errs.w: I've been knowing her for years.
errs.c: I've known her for years.

structures.desc: Forming the present simple passive (am/is/are + past participle) and past simple passive (was/were + past participle), with optional agent (by).

rules.t: Present simple passive: subject + am/is/are + past participle. Used when the action or object is more important than the doer.
rules.td: Präsens Passiv: Subjekt + am/is/are + Partizip Perfekt. Wird verwendet, wenn die Handlung oder das Objekt wichtiger ist als der Handelnde.

ex.en: English is spoken in many countries.
ex.de: Englisch wird in vielen Ländern gesprochen.

ex.en: The letters are delivered every morning.
ex.de: Die Briefe werden jeden Morgen zugestellt.

rules.t: Past simple passive: subject + was/were + past participle. Used for completed passive events in the past.
rules.td: Vergangenheitspassiv: Subjekt + was/were + Partizip Perfekt. Für abgeschlossene passive Ereignisse in der Vergangenheit.

ex.en: The city was destroyed by an earthquake.
ex.de: Die Stadt wurde durch ein Erdbeben zerstört.

ex.en: Hundreds of people were rescued.
ex.de: Hunderte Menschen wurden gerettet.

rules.t: The doer (agent) is introduced with 'by'. Only include the agent when it adds important information. If the doer is unknown or unimportant, omit it.
rules.td: Der Handelnde (Agent) wird mit 'by' eingeführt. Der Agent wird nur erwähnt, wenn er wichtige Information hinzufügt. Wenn der Handelnde unbekannt oder unwichtig ist, lässt man ihn weg.

ex.en: The book was written by J.K. Rowling.
ex.de: Das Buch wurde von J.K. Rowling geschrieben.

ex.en: The window was broken. (agent unknown)
ex.de: Das Fenster wurde zerbrochen. (Handelnder unbekannt)

affirmative: English is spoken here.
The city was destroyed.
The book was written by Rowling.
negative: The letters aren't delivered on Sundays.
The house wasn't damaged.
questions: Is English spoken here?
Was the city destroyed?
Were the people rescued?

errs.d: Using the wrong past participle form (often the base form or past simple)
errs.w: The house was build in 1960.
errs.c: The house was built in 1960.

errs.d: Omitting the form of 'be' from the passive construction
errs.w: The city destroyed by an earthquake.
errs.c: The city was destroyed by an earthquake.

errs.d: Wrong form of 'be' (was/were or is/are) for the subject
errs.w: The people was rescued.
errs.c: The people were rescued.

errs.d: Using 'from' instead of 'by' to introduce the agent
errs.w: The city was hit from an earthquake.
errs.c: The city was hit by an earthquake.

structures.desc: Expressing hypothetical, unlikely, or imaginary situations with If + past simple, would + base verb, and contrasting with the first conditional.

rules.t: Structure: If + past simple, ... would + base verb. The past simple does NOT refer to the past — it signals that the situation is hypothetical. 'Would' can be contracted to 'd.
rules.td: Struktur: If + Simple Past, ... would + Grundform. Das Simple Past bezieht sich NICHT auf die Vergangenheit — es zeigt, dass die Situation hypothetisch ist. 'Would' kann zu 'd verkürzt werden.

ex.en: If I had a million euros, I'd travel the world.
ex.de: Wenn ich eine Million Euro hätte, würde ich die Welt bereisen.

ex.en: If she lived closer, we would see each other more.
ex.de: Wenn sie näher wohnen würde, würden wir uns öfter sehen.

rules.t: Negative: If I didn't have..., I wouldn't... Both clauses can be negative.
rules.td: Verneinung: If I didn't have..., I wouldn't... Beide Sätze können verneint sein.

ex.en: If I didn't have homework, I would go out.
ex.de: Wenn ich keine Hausaufgaben hätte, würde ich ausgehen.

ex.en: She wouldn't be happy if she knew the truth.
ex.de: Sie wäre nicht glücklich, wenn sie die Wahrheit wüsste.

rules.t: First conditional = real possibility (If + present, will). Second conditional = hypothetical/unlikely (If + past, would). 'If I find your wallet, I'll give it back' (possible) vs 'If I found a million euros, I'd buy a house' (unlikely).
rules.td: Erster Konditional = reale Möglichkeit (If + Präsens, will). Zweiter Konditional = hypothetisch/unwahrscheinlich (If + Vergangenheit, would). 'If I find your wallet, I'll give it back' (möglich) vs 'If I found a million euros, I'd buy a house' (unwahrscheinlich).

ex.en: If it rains, we'll stay inside. (1st — real)
ex.de: Wenn es regnet, bleiben wir drinnen. (1. — real)

ex.en: If I were a bird, I would fly to Africa. (2nd — imaginary)
ex.de: Wenn ich ein Vogel wäre, würde ich nach Afrika fliegen. (2. — imaginär)

affirmative: If I had money, I'd buy a car.
If she were here, she would help us.
I'd travel the world if I could.
negative: If I didn't have homework, I'd go out.
She wouldn't be happy if she knew.
I wouldn't do that if I were you.
questions: What would you do if you won the lottery?
Would you move if you could?
If I were you, what would you do?

errs.d: Using 'would' in the if-clause instead of past simple
errs.w: If I would have more money, I would buy a car.
errs.c: If I had more money, I would buy a car.

errs.d: Using present simple (1st conditional) where past simple (2nd conditional) is needed
errs.w: If I have a million euros, I would travel the world.
errs.c: If I had a million euros, I would travel the world.

errs.d: Using 'would' in both the if-clause and the result clause
errs.w: If I would be rich, I would buy a big house.
errs.c: If I were/was rich, I would buy a big house.

structures.nd: Going to für Vorhersagen aufgrund von Beweisen
structures.desc: Expanding the going to future to include evidence-based predictions alongside planned actions, and contrasting with will for opinions and spontaneous decisions.

rules.t: Use 'going to' for predictions based on EVIDENCE you can see or know about right now. Something is clearly about to happen.
rules.td: Man verwendet 'going to' für Vorhersagen, die auf BEWEISEN basieren, die man gerade sehen oder wissen kann. Etwas wird offensichtlich gleich passieren.

ex.en: Look at those clouds — it's going to rain.
ex.de: Schau dir die Wolken an — es wird regnen.

ex.en: The car is out of control — it's going to crash!
ex.de: Das Auto ist ausser Kontrolle — es wird zusammenstossen!

rules.t: 'Going to' = evidence-based predictions and plans. 'Will' = opinions, general predictions (no specific evidence), spontaneous decisions, offers, promises.
rules.td: 'Going to' = Vorhersagen aufgrund von Beweisen und Plaune. 'Will' = Meinungen, allgemeine Vorhersagen (ohne spezifischen Beweis), spontane Entscheidungen, Angebote, Versprechen.

ex.en: It's going to rain. (I can see dark clouds)
ex.de: Es wird regnen. (Ich sehe dunkle Wolken)

ex.en: I think it will rain tomorrow. (my opinion, no evidence)
ex.de: Ich glaube, es wird morgen regnen. (meine Meinung, kein Beweis)

rules.t: Revision: 'going to' is also used for planned future actions — intentions that were made before the moment of speaking.
rules.td: Wiederholung: 'going to' wird auch für geplante zukünftige Handlungen verwendet — Absichten, die vor dem Sprechmoment gefasst wurden.

ex.en: I'm going to visit London next summer. (plan)
ex.de: Ich werde nauchsten Sommer London besuchen. (Plan)

ex.en: Are you going to book a hotel?
ex.de: Wirst du ein Hotel buchen?

affirmative: It's going to rain.
I'm going to visit London.
She's going to be a doctor.
negative: It's not going to work.
I'm not going to tell anyone.
They aren't going to come.
questions: Is it going to rain?
Are you going to call her?
What are you going to do?

errs.d: Using 'will' for evidence-based predictions instead of 'going to'
errs.w: Look at those clouds — it will rain!
errs.c: Look at those clouds — it's going to rain!

errs.d: Omitting the 'be' verb from the going to construction
errs.w: I going to go to the cinema.
errs.c: I'm going to go to the cinema.

errs.d: Writing 'gonna' in formal contexts
errs.w: I'm gonna visit London next summer.
errs.c: I'm going to visit London next summer.

items.p: I ___ (watch) TV when you called me.
a: was watching
items.h: Which form of 'be' goes with 'I' in the past?
items.hd: Welche Form von 'be' passt zu 'I' in der Vergangenheit?
items.e: 'I' takes 'was' in the past, and we add -ing because the action was in progress when the phone call happened.
items.ed: 'I' braucht 'was' in der Vergangenheit, und wir fügen -ing hinzu, weil die Handlung gerade stattfand, als der Anruf kam.

items.p: They ___ (play) football in the park at 5 o'clock.
a: were playing
items.h: 'They' needs a plural form of 'be' in the past.
items.hd: 'They' braucht eine Mehrzahlform von 'be' in der Vergangenheit.
items.e: 'They' takes 'were' in the past continuous. The action was in progress at 5 o'clock.
items.ed: 'They' braucht 'were' in der Verlaufsform der Vergangenheit. Die Handlung fand um 5 Uhr gerade statt.

items.p: While she ___ (sleep), the dog started barking.
a: was sleeping
items.h: 'While' tells you the action was ongoing. Which tense shows an ongoing past action?
items.hd: 'While' zeigt, dass die Handlung gerade lief. Welche Zeitform zeigt eine laufende Handlung in der Vergangenheit?
items.e: 'While' + past continuous describes the longer background action. 'She' takes 'was' + -ing.
items.ed: 'While' + Past Continuous beschreibt die längere Hintergrundhandlung. 'She' braucht 'was' + -ing.

items.p: We ___ (walk) to school when it started to rain.
a: were walking
items.h: The walking was already happening when the rain started. Which tense shows that?
items.hd: Das Gehen fand bereits statt, als der Regen begann. Welche Zeitform zeigt das?
items.e: 'We were walking' (past continuous) is the longer action that was interrupted by the rain (past simple).
items.ed: 'We were walking' (Past Continuous) ist die längere Handlung, die durch den Regen (Past Simple) unterbrochen wurde.

items.p: At midnight, Tom ___ (read) a book and Lisa ___ (listen) to music.
a: was reading ... was listening
was reading...was listening
items.h: Both Tom and Lisa are singular subjects. Both actions were happening at the same time.
items.hd: Sowohl Tom als auch Lisa sind Einzahl-Subjekte. Beide Handlungen fanden gleichzeitig statt.
items.e: Both Tom and Lisa are 3rd person singular, so both take 'was' + -ing. Both actions were in progress at midnight.
items.ed: Sowohl Tom als auch Lisa sind 3. Person Einzahl, also brauchen beide 'was' + -ing. Beide Handlungen liefen um Mitternacht.

items.p: She ___ (not/wait) for the bus. She ___ (walk) home.
a: wasn't waiting ... was walking
was not waiting ... was walking
wasn't waiting...was walking
was not waiting...was walking
items.h: Both gaps describe what 'she' was doing at that moment. One is negative.
items.hd: Beide Lücken beschreiben, was 'sie' in dem Moment tat. Eine ist verneint.
items.e: Both actions use past continuous because they describe what was happening at that moment. Negative: wasn't + -ing.
items.ed: Beide Handlungen stehen im Past Continuous, weil sie beschreiben, was in dem Moment passierte. Verneinung: wasn't + -ing.

items.p: Which sentence correctly describes an action in progress at 7 pm yesterday?
items.c: I was doing my homework at 7 pm.
a: I was doing my homework at 7 pm.
ds: I did my homework at 7 pm.
I were doing my homework at 7 pm.
I were do my homework at 7 pm.
items.h: You need the past continuous to show the action was happening at that exact time.
items.hd: Du brauchst das Past Continuous, um zu zeigen, dass die Handlung genau zu der Zeit stattfand.
items.e: 'I was doing' is the correct past continuous form. 'Was' matches 'I', and the main verb needs -ing.
items.ed: 'I was doing' ist die richtige Past-Continuous-Form. 'Was' passt zu 'I', und das Hauptverb braucht -ing.

items.p: Choose the correct sentence about interrupted actions.
items.c: I was having dinner when the doorbell rang.
a: I was having dinner when the doorbell rang.
ds: I was having dinner when the doorbell was ringing.
I had dinner when the doorbell rang.
I was having dinner when the doorbell was rang.
items.h: One action was longer (background), the other was short (interruption). They need different tenses.
items.hd: Eine Handlung war länger (Hintergrund), die andere war kurz (Unterbrechung). Sie brauchen verschiedene Zeitformen.
items.e: The longer action (having dinner) uses past continuous; the short interruption (doorbell) uses past simple.
items.ed: Die längere Handlung (Abendessen) steht im Past Continuous; die kurze Unterbrechung (Türklingel) im Past Simple.

items.p: Which sentence is correct?
items.c: While we were eating, the lights went out.
a: While we were eating, the lights went out.
ds: While we ate, the lights were going out.
While we were eating, the lights were going out.
While we eating, the lights went out.
items.h: 'While' introduces the longer background action. The sudden event uses a different tense.
items.hd: 'While' leitet die längere Hintergrundhandlung ein. Das plötzliche Ereignis braucht eine andere Zeitform.
items.e: 'While' + past continuous for the background action; past simple for the sudden event that interrupted it.
items.ed: 'While' + Past Continuous für die Hintergrundhandlung; Past Simple für das plötzliche Ereignis, das sie unterbrach.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: I were playing video games when Mum called.
a: was playing
I was playing video games when Mum called.
I was playing video games when Mum called
items.h: Look at the subject 'I'. Which form of 'be' goes with it?
items.hd: Schau dir das Subjekt 'I' an. Welche Form von 'be' passt dazu?
items.e: 'I' always takes 'was' in the past, not 'were'. Correct: 'I was playing'.
items.ed: 'I' braucht in der Vergangenheit immer 'was', nicht 'were'. Richtig: 'I was playing'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She was walk to the shop when she met her friend.
a: walking
was walking
She was walking to the shop when she met her friend.
She was walking to the shop when she met her friend
items.h: What ending does the main verb need in the past continuous?
items.hd: Welche Endung braucht das Hauptverb im Past Continuous?
items.e: The past continuous needs was/were + verb-ing. 'Walk' must become 'walking'.
items.ed: Das Past Continuous braucht was/were + Verb-ing. 'Walk' muss zu 'walking' werden.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: I was walking to school when I was seeing a cat.
a: saw
I saw a cat
I was walking to school when I saw a cat.
I was walking to school when I saw a cat
items.h: One action was long, the other was short. Does the short action need past continuous?
items.hd: Eine Handlung war lang, die andere kurz. Braucht die kurze Handlung Past Continuous?
items.e: 'Seeing a cat' was a short, sudden event — it needs past simple ('saw'), not past continuous.
items.ed: 'Eine Katze sehen' war ein kurzes, plötzliches Ereignis — es braucht Past Simple ('saw'), nicht Past Continuous.

items.p: You're telling a friend about what you were doing when it started raining yesterday. Complete: 'I ________ (play) football when it suddenly started raining.'
a: was playing
items.h: The football was the longer background action. Which tense shows an action in progress in the past?
items.hd: Fußball spielen war die längere Hintergrundhandlung. Welche Zeitform zeigt eine andauernde Handlung in der Vergangenheit?
items.e: Past continuous (was playing) describes the ongoing background action that was interrupted by the rain (past simple).
items.ed: Past continuous (was playing) beschreibt die andauernde Hintergrundhandlung, die durch den Regen unterbrochen wurde (past simple).

items.p: You're describing yesterday evening to your pen pal. Write one sentence: 'She ________ (walk) home while it ________ (rain).'
a: was walking ... was raining
was walking, was raining
items.h: 'While' introduces two simultaneous ongoing actions. Both actions were happening at the same time.
items.hd: 'While' leitet zwei gleichzeitige andauernde Handlungen ein. Beide Handlungen passierten zur gleichen Zeit.
items.e: When two longer actions happen simultaneously in the past, both use past continuous. 'While' signals simultaneous actions.
items.ed: Wenn zwei längere Handlungen gleichzeitig in der Vergangenheit stattfinden, verwenden beide past continuous. 'While' signalisiert gleichzeitige Handlungen.

items.p: Your mum asks what everyone was doing at 7 o'clock. Answer about your family: 'At 7, we ________ (have) dinner.'
a: were having
items.h: At 7 o'clock, the action was in progress. Which tense shows an action happening at a specific time in the past?
items.hd: Um 7 Uhr war die Handlung im Gange. Welche Zeitform zeigt eine Handlung, die zu einem bestimmten Zeitpunkt in der Vergangenheit passierte?
items.e: Past continuous (were having) describes an action in progress at a specific time in the past.
items.ed: Past continuous (were having) beschreibt eine Handlung, die zu einem bestimmten Zeitpunkt in der Vergangenheit im Gange war.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Ich ging gerade zur Schule, als es zu regnen begann.
items.c: I was walking to school when it started to rain.
a: I was walking to school when it started to rain.
I was walking to school when it started to rain
I was walking to school when it started raining.
I was walking to school when it started raining
I was walking to school when it began to rain.
I was walking to school when it began to rain
items.h: German 'ging gerade' signals an ongoing past action. Which English tense expresses that?
items.hd: 'Ging gerade' zeigt eine laufende Handlung an. Welche englische Zeitform drückt das aus?
items.e: 'Ging gerade' = was walking (past continuous). 'Als es zu regnen begann' = when it started to rain (past simple for the interruption).
items.ed: 'Ging gerade' = was walking (Past Continuous). 'Als es zu regnen begann' = when it started to rain (Past Simple für die Unterbrechung).

items.p: 🇩🇪 Was hast du gestern um 9 Uhr gemacht? — Ich habe ein Buch gelesen.
items.c: What were you doing at 9 o'clock yesterday? — I was reading a book.
a: What were you doing at 9 o'clock yesterday? — I was reading a book.
What were you doing at 9 o'clock yesterday? I was reading a book.
What were you doing yesterday at 9 o'clock? — I was reading a book.
What were you doing yesterday at 9 o'clock? I was reading a book.
items.h: The German Perfekt ('habe gelesen') here describes what was happening at a specific time — use past continuous in English.
items.hd: Das deutsche Perfekt ('habe gelesen') beschreibt hier, was zu einer bestimmten Zeit passierte — verwende Past Continuous im Englischen.
items.e: German uses Perfekt for 'was happening at 9', but English uses past continuous: 'What were you doing?' / 'I was reading'.
items.ed: Deutsch verwendet Perfekt für 'was um 9 passierte', aber Englisch verwendet Past Continuous: 'What were you doing?' / 'I was reading'.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: was / she / dinner / cooking / at / 6 pm
items.c: She was cooking dinner at 6 pm.
a: She was cooking dinner at 6 pm.
She was cooking dinner at 6 pm
At 6 pm she was cooking dinner.
At 6 pm she was cooking dinner
items.h: Start with the subject, then the auxiliary, then verb-ing, then the rest.
items.hd: Beginne mit dem Subjekt, dann das Hilfsverb, dann Verb-ing, dann der Rest.
items.e: Past continuous word order: Subject + was/were + verb-ing + rest of sentence.
items.ed: Past-Continuous-Wortstellung: Subjekt + was/were + Verb-ing + Rest des Satzes.

items.p: Match each sentence beginning with the correct ending. 1: While I was reading, 2: When the phone rang, 3: At 10 pm last night, 4: They were playing outside when 5: She wasn't sleeping,
ds: a: I was having a shower.
b: she was studying for the test.
d: someone knocked on the door.
e: it started to get dark.
items.h: Think about which tense each half of the sentence needs — past continuous for ongoing, past simple for short events.
items.hd: Überlege, welche Zeitform jeder Satzteil braucht — Past Continuous für laufend, Past Simple für kurze Ereignisse.
items.e: 1→d (While + continuous → short event), 2→a (When + short event → continuous), 3→c (specific time → continuous), 4→e (continuous + when → short event), 5→b (negative continuous → what she was actually doing).
items.ed: 1→d (While + Verlaufsform → kurzes Ereignis), 2→a (When + kurzes Ereignis → Verlaufsform), 3→c (bestimmte Zeit → Verlaufsform), 4→e (Verlaufsform + when → kurzes Ereignis), 5→b (verneinte Verlaufsform → was sie tatsauchlich tat).

items.p: At 8 o'clock last night, which sentence describes what was happening?
a: My sister was cooking dinner.
ds: My sister cooks dinner every night.
items.h: At 8 o'clock, the action was in the middle of happening. Which tense shows an ongoing action in the past?
items.hd: Um 8 Uhr war die Handlung mitten im Geschehen. Welche Zeitform zeigt eine andauernde Handlung in der Vergangenheit?
items.e: Past continuous describes an action in progress at a specific time in the past. At 8 o'clock, the cooking was still happening.
items.ed: Past continuous beschreibt eine Handlung, die zu einem bestimmten Zeitpunkt in der Vergangenheit noch andauerte.

items.p: ___ they were eating breakfast, the postman arrived.
a: While
items.h: The gap is followed by a subject + verb. Which time word goes before a clause with an ongoing action?
items.hd: Auf die Lücke folgt ein Subjekt + Verb. Welches Zeitwort steht vor einem Satz mit einer laufenden Handlung?
items.e: 'While' is used before a clause (subject + verb) to describe an ongoing action. 'During' can only be used before a noun.
items.ed: 'While' steht vor einem Satz (Subjekt + Verb) für eine laufende Handlung. 'During' kann nur vor einem Nomen stehen.

items.p: We stayed inside ___ the storm.
a: during
items.h: The gap is followed by a noun ('the storm'), not a full sentence.
items.hd: Auf die Lücke folgt ein Nomen ('the storm'), kein ganzer Satz.
items.e: 'During' is used before a noun phrase: 'during the storm'. 'While' would need a full clause: 'while it was storming'.
items.ed: 'During' steht vor einer Nominalphrase: 'during the storm'. 'While' bräuchte einen ganzen Satz: 'while it was storming'.

items.p: ___ she left the house, she checked that all the windows were closed.
a: Before
items.h: She checked the windows first, then she left. Which word shows the checking happened first?
items.hd: Sie überprüfte zuerst die Fenster, dann ging sie. Welches Wort zeigt, dass das Überprüfen zuerst passierte?
items.e: 'Before' shows that the checking happened earlier than leaving: 'Before she left, she checked...'
items.ed: 'Before' zeigt, dass das Überprüfen vor dem Weggehen passierte: 'Before she left, she checked...'

items.p: I was doing my homework ___ my brother was playing a computer game.
a: while
items.h: Both actions were happening at the same time. Which connector links two ongoing actions?
items.hd: Beide Handlungen passierten gleichzeitig. Welcher Konnektor verbindet zwei laufende Handlungen?
items.e: 'While' connects two actions happening at the same time, both in the past continuous.
items.ed: 'While' verbindet zwei gleichzeitige Handlungen, beide im Past Continuous.

items.p: They waited at the bus stop ___ the bus came.
a: until
till
items.h: They kept waiting and stopped when the bus arrived. Which word means 'up to the point when'?
items.hd: Sie warteten weiter und hörten auf, als der Bus kam. Welches Wort bedeutet 'bis zu dem Zeitpunkt, als'?
items.e: 'Until' means 'up to the point when'. They waited and stopped waiting when the bus came.
items.ed: 'Until' bedeutet 'bis zu dem Zeitpunkt, als'. Sie warteten und hörten auf, als der Bus kam.

items.p: I fell asleep ___ the film. I didn't see the ending.
a: during
items.h: The gap is followed by a noun ('the film'). Which connector goes before nouns?
items.hd: Auf die Lücke folgt ein Nomen ('the film'). Welcher Konnektor steht vor Nomen?
items.e: 'During' + noun phrase: 'during the film'. 'While' would need a clause: 'while I was watching the film'.
items.ed: 'During' + Nominalphrase: 'during the film'. 'While' bräuchte einen Satz: 'while I was watching the film'.

items.p: Which sentence is correct?
items.c: We had lunch during the break.
a: We had lunch during the break.
ds: We had lunch during we were on break.
We had lunch while the break.
We had lunch during were on break.
items.h: Think about which word goes before a noun and which goes before a clause.
items.hd: Überlege, welches Wort vor einem Nomen steht und welches vor einem Satz.
items.e: 'During' is followed by a noun phrase ('the break'). 'While' would need a clause: 'while we were on break'.
items.ed: 'During' steht vor einer Nominalphrase ('the break'). 'While' bräuchte einen Satz: 'while we were on break'.

items.p: Choose the correct sentence.
items.c: After the match finished, we went home.
a: After the match finished, we went home.
ds: After the match finished, we were going home.
After the match was finishing, we went home.
During the match finished, we went home.
items.h: 'After' introduces a completed event. What tense do you use for completed past actions?
items.hd: 'After' leitet ein abgeschlossenes Ereignis ein. Welche Zeitform verwendest du für abgeschlossene Handlungen?
items.e: 'After' + past simple for completed events. Both clauses use past simple because both actions were completed.
items.ed: 'After' + Past Simple für abgeschlossene Ereignisse. Beide Satzteile verwenden Past Simple, weil beide Handlungen abgeschlossen waren.

items.p: Which sentence uses 'while' correctly?
items.c: While Dad was cooking, I set the table.
a: While Dad was cooking, I set the table.
ds: While the dinner, I set the table.
While Dad cooked, I was setting the table.
While Dad was cooking, I was setting the table.
items.h: 'While' introduces the longer background action. The other action is shorter.
items.hd: 'While' leitet die längere Hintergrundhandlung ein. Die andere Handlung ist kürzer.
items.e: 'While' + past continuous for the ongoing action. The shorter action (set the table) uses past simple.
items.ed: 'While' + Past Continuous für die laufende Handlung. Die kürzere Handlung (Tisch decken) steht im Past Simple.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: During I was sleeping, someone rang the doorbell.
a: While
While I was sleeping, someone rang the doorbell.
While I was sleeping, someone rang the doorbell
items.h: 'During' is followed by what kind of word — a clause or a noun?
items.hd: 'During' steht vor welcher Wortart — einem Satz oder einem Nomen?
items.e: 'During' can only be followed by a noun phrase. Before a clause (subject + verb), use 'while'.
items.ed: 'During' kann nur vor einer Nominalphrase stehen. Vor einem Satz (Subjekt + Verb) verwende 'while'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: We went swimming while the summer holidays.
a: during
during the summer holidays
We went swimming during the summer holidays.
We went swimming during the summer holidays
items.h: 'The summer holidays' is a noun phrase. Which connector goes before a noun phrase?
items.hd: 'The summer holidays' ist eine Nominalphrase. Welcher Konnektor steht vor einer Nominalphrase?
items.e: 'While' must be followed by a clause. Before a noun phrase like 'the summer holidays', use 'during'.
items.ed: 'While' muss vor einem Satz stehen. Vor einer Nominalphrase wie 'the summer holidays' verwende 'during'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: By they arrived at the cinema, the film had already started.
a: By the time
By the time they arrived at the cinema, the film had already started.
By the time they arrived at the cinema, the film had already started
items.h: The three-word connector 'by ___ ___' is incomplete here.
items.hd: Der dreiteilige Konnektor 'by ___ ___' ist hier unvollständig.
items.e: The full connector is 'by the time', not just 'by'. It means 'when' (often implying something happened too late).
items.ed: Der vollständige Konnektor ist 'by the time', nicht nur 'by'. Er bedeutet 'als' (oft mit der Bedeutung, dass etwas zu spät war).

items.p: You're telling a classmate why you were late. Complete the sentence: 'When I ________ at school, the bell ________ already ________.'
a: arrived ... had already rung
arrived ... was already ringing
arrived, had already rung
items.h: 'When' connects your arrival with something that was already happening or had happened. What tense shows an earlier action?
items.hd: 'When' verbindet deine Ankunft mit etwas, das bereits passierte oder passiert war. Welche Zeitform zeigt eine frühere Handlung?
items.e: 'When I arrived' (past simple) + the bell 'had already rung' (past perfect) shows the bell rang before you arrived.
items.ed: 'When I arrived' (past simple) + die Glocke 'had already rung' (past perfect) zeigt, dass die Glocke vor deiner Ankunft geläutet hatte.

items.p: Your friend asks what your evening was like. Describe what happened: 'While my sister ________ (study), my brother ________ (play) video games.'
a: was studying ... was playing
was studying, was playing
items.h: 'While' introduces two simultaneous actions. Both were happening at the same time in the past.
items.hd: 'While' leitet zwei gleichzeitige Handlungen ein. Beide passierten gleichzeitig in der Vergangenheit.
items.e: 'While' + past continuous for both actions shows two things happening at the same time.
items.ed: 'While' + past continuous für beide Handlungen zeigt zwei Dinge, die gleichzeitig passierten.

items.p: You're writing about your bedtime routine in your English diary. Write one sentence using 'before':
items.c: Before I went to bed, I brushed my teeth.
a: Before I went to bed, I brushed my teeth.
I brushed my teeth before I went to bed.
Before I went to bed I brushed my teeth.
items.h: 'Before' can start the sentence or come in the middle. Think about the order: what happened first?
items.hd: 'Before' kann am Satzanfang oder in der Mitte stehen. Denk an die Reihenfolge: Was passierte zuerst?
items.e: 'Before I went to bed, I brushed my teeth' — 'before' introduces the later action, and the main clause describes what you did first.
items.ed: 'Before I went to bed, I brushed my teeth' — 'before' leitet die spätere Handlung ein, und der Hauptsatz beschreibt, was du zuerst getan hast.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Während des Unterrichts darf man nicht telefonieren.
items.c: During the lesson, you mustn't use your phone.
a: During the lesson, you mustn't use your phone.
During the lesson, you mustn't use your phone
You mustn't use your phone during the lesson.
You mustn't use your phone during the lesson
During class, you mustn't use your phone.
During the lesson you can't use your phone.
items.h: 'Während des Unterrichts' — is 'des Unterrichts' a clause or a noun phrase? Choose the right connector.
items.hd: 'Während des Unterrichts' — ist 'des Unterrichts' ein Satz oder eine Nominalphrase? Wähle den richtigen Konnektor.
items.e: 'Während des Unterrichts' = 'during the lesson' (noun phrase, not a clause). German 'während' can be both 'while' and 'during', but here it's 'during' because it's before a noun.
items.ed: 'Während des Unterrichts' = 'during the lesson' (Nominalphrase). Deutsch 'während' kann 'while' und 'during' sein, aber hier ist es 'during', weil ein Nomen folgt.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Während wir auf den Bus warteten, begann es zu schneien.
items.c: While we were waiting for the bus, it started to snow.
a: While we were waiting for the bus, it started to snow.
While we were waiting for the bus, it started to snow
While we were waiting for the bus, it started snowing.
While we were waiting for the bus, it started snowing
It started to snow while we were waiting for the bus.
It started snowing while we were waiting for the bus.
items.h: 'Während wir warteten' — there is a subject + verb after 'während'. Which English connector do you need?
items.hd: 'Während wir warteten' — nach 'während' folgt Subjekt + Verb. Welchen englischen Konnektor brauchst du?
items.e: 'Während + clause' = 'while' (not 'during'). The waiting was ongoing (past continuous), and the snow was a sudden event (past simple).
items.ed: 'Während + Satz' = 'while' (nicht 'during'). Das Warten war laufend (Past Continuous), und der Schnee war ein plötzliches Ereignis (Past Simple).

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: the / stayed / we / rain / during / inside
items.c: We stayed inside during the rain.
a: We stayed inside during the rain.
We stayed inside during the rain
During the rain we stayed inside.
During the rain we stayed inside
During the rain, we stayed inside.
During the rain, we stayed inside
items.h: 'During' goes right before a noun phrase.
items.hd: 'During' steht direkt vor einer Nominalphrase.
items.e: 'During the rain' is the time phrase. 'During' must come directly before the noun 'the rain'.
items.ed: 'During the rain' ist die Zeitangabe. 'During' muss direkt vor dem Nomen 'the rain' stehen.

items.p: Match each connector with the correct completion. 1: While she was studying, 2: During the concert, 3: Before we left, 4: After the lesson, 5: Until the bus came,
ds: b: they waited at the stop.
c: the phone rang three times.
d: we went to the park.
items.h: Check whether each connector is followed by a noun or a clause, and think about the time sequence.
items.hd: Prüfe, ob nach jedem Konnektor ein Nomen oder ein Satz folgt, und denke an die zeitliche Reihenfolge.
items.e: 1→c (while + continuous → short event), 2→a (during + noun → what happened then), 3→e (before leaving → packing first), 4→d (after lesson → went to park), 5→b (until = waited up to that point).
items.ed: 1→c (while + Verlaufsform → kurzes Ereignis), 2→a (during + Nomen → was dann passierte), 3→e (before Weggehen → vorher packen), 4→d (after Unterricht → in den Park), 5→b (until = warteten bis dahin).

items.p: Tom was sleeping during the lesson. Ask about: what Tom was doing during the lesson.
items.c: What was Tom doing during the lesson?
a: What was Tom doing during the lesson?
What was Tom doing during the lesson
items.h: Start with a question word, then the auxiliary, then the subject.
items.hd: Beginne mit einem Fragewort, dann das Hilfsverb, dann das Subjekt.
items.e: Past continuous question: What + was + subject + verb-ing? 'During the lesson' stays as a noun phrase.
items.ed: Past-Continuous-Frage: What + was + Subjekt + Verb-ing? 'During the lesson' bleibt als Nominalphrase.

items.p: It ___ me twenty minutes to walk to school every day.
a: takes
items.h: The subject is 'it'. What form does the verb need in the present simple?
items.hd: Das Subjekt ist 'it'. Welche Form braucht das Verb im Present Simple?
items.e: 'It takes' is the correct present simple form. 'It' is 3rd person singular, so the verb needs -s.
items.ed: 'It takes' ist die richtige Present-Simple-Form. 'It' ist 3. Person Einzahl, also braucht das Verb -s.

items.p: It ___ us two hours to drive to Vienna last weekend.
a: took
items.h: 'Last weekend' tells you this happened in the past. What is the past form of 'take'?
items.hd: 'Last weekend' zeigt, dass es in der Vergangenheit war. Was ist die Vergangenheitsform von 'take'?
items.e: 'Take' is irregular: take → took → taken. For past events, use 'It took...'.
items.ed: 'Take' ist unregelmäßig: take → took → taken. Für vergangene Ereignisse verwende 'It took...'.

items.p: It takes her an hour ___ finish her homework.
a: to
items.h: After the time expression, you need a small word before the verb.
items.hd: Nach der Zeitangabe brauchst du ein kleines Wort vor dem Verb.
items.e: The structure is: It takes + person + time + TO + base verb. 'To' connects the time to the action.
items.ed: Die Struktur ist: It takes + Person + Zeit + TO + Grundform. 'To' verbindet die Zeit mit der Handlung.

items.p: How long does ___ take you to get to school?
a: it
items.h: In the 'it takes' structure, what is always the subject?
items.hd: In der 'it takes'-Struktur, was ist immer das Subjekt?
items.e: The subject is always 'it' in this structure: 'How long does IT take you to...?'
items.ed: Das Subjekt ist immer 'it' in dieser Struktur: 'How long does IT take you to...?'

items.p: It doesn't ___ long to learn this game.
a: take
items.h: After 'doesn't', what form does the main verb take?
items.hd: Nach 'doesn't', welche Form hat das Hauptverb?
items.e: After 'doesn't', use the base form: 'doesn't take'. The auxiliary 'does' already carries the 3rd person.
items.ed: Nach 'doesn't' steht die Grundform: 'doesn't take'. Das Hilfsverb 'does' trägt bereits die 3. Person.

items.p: It ___ (take) my dad three years to learn English when he was young.
a: took
items.h: Look at 'when he was young' — this tells you the time frame.
items.hd: Schau dir 'when he was young' an — das verrät dir den Zeitrahmen.
items.e: 'When he was young' is a past time marker, so we use 'It took' (past simple of 'take').
items.ed: 'When he was young' ist eine Vergangenheitszeitangabe, also verwenden wir 'It took' (Past Simple von 'take').

items.p: Which sentence correctly describes the time needed to get to school?
items.c: It takes me thirty minutes to get to school.
a: It takes me thirty minutes to get to school.
ds: I take thirty minutes to get to school.
It takes me thirty minutes get to school.
It needs me thirty minutes to get to school.
items.h: The correct structure starts with 'It' and uses 'takes', not 'needs'.
items.hd: Die richtige Struktur beginnt mit 'It' und verwendet 'takes', nicht 'needs'.
items.e: The correct structure is: It takes + person + time + to + base verb. The subject is always 'it', not the person.
items.ed: Die richtige Struktur ist: It takes + Person + Zeit + to + Grundform. Das Subjekt ist immer 'it', nicht die Person.

items.p: Which question is correct?
items.c: How long does it take you to get ready in the morning?
a: How long does it take you to get ready in the morning?
ds: How long does it take you get ready in the morning?
How long do you take to get ready in the morning?
How long it takes you to get ready in the morning?
items.h: The question needs 'does it take' (not 'it takes') and 'to' before the verb.
items.hd: Die Frage braucht 'does it take' (nicht 'it takes') und 'to' vor dem Verb.
items.e: Question form: How long + does + it + take + person + to + verb? All elements must be present.
items.ed: Frageform: How long + does + it + take + Person + to + Verb? Alle Elemente müssen vorhanden sein.

items.p: Choose the correct sentence about a past event.
items.c: It took them an hour to clean the house.
a: It took them an hour to clean the house.
ds: It took them an hour clean the house.
They took an hour to clean the house.
It taked them an hour to clean the house.
items.h: The past form of 'take' is irregular. The subject is always 'it'.
items.hd: Die Vergangenheitsform von 'take' ist unregelmäßig. Das Subjekt ist immer 'it'.
items.e: Past form: It took + person + time + to + verb. 'Take' → 'took' (irregular). Subject is always 'it'.
items.ed: Vergangenheitsform: It took + Person + Zeit + to + Verb. 'Take' → 'took' (unregelmäßig). Das Subjekt ist immer 'it'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: I take twenty minutes to get to school.
a: It takes me
It takes me twenty minutes to get to school.
It takes me twenty minutes to get to school
items.h: Who or what should be the subject of this sentence?
items.hd: Wer oder was sollte das Subjekt dieses Satzes sein?
items.e: In the 'it takes' structure, the subject is always 'it', not the person. 'I take' → 'It takes me'.
items.ed: In der 'it takes'-Struktur ist das Subjekt immer 'it', nicht die Person. 'I take' → 'It takes me'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: It takes me ten minutes walk to the bus stop.
a: to walk
It takes me ten minutes to walk to the bus stop.
It takes me ten minutes to walk to the bus stop
items.h: Something is missing between the time and the verb.
items.hd: Zwischen der Zeitangabe und dem Verb fehlt etwas.
items.e: You need 'to' before the base verb: 'ten minutes TO walk'. The 'to' connects the duration to the action.
items.ed: Du brauchst 'to' vor dem Verb: 'ten minutes TO walk'. Das 'to' verbindet die Dauer mit der Handlung.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: It takes me two hours to got to my grandma's house yesterday.
a: took
It took
It took me two hours to get to my grandma's house yesterday.
It took me two hours to get to my grandma's house yesterday
items.h: 'Yesterday' tells you this was in the past. Is the verb in the right tense?
items.hd: 'Yesterday' zeigt, dass es in der Vergangenheit war. Ist das Verb in der richtigen Zeitform?
items.e: 'Yesterday' requires past simple: 'It took me...'. Also, after 'to' we need the base form: 'to get' (not 'to got').
items.ed: 'Yesterday' verlangt Past Simple: 'It took me...'. Außerdem braucht man nach 'to' die Grundform: 'to get' (nicht 'to got').

items.p: A new student asks how long the journey to school is. Answer them: 'It ________ me ________ to get to school.'
a: takes ... 30 minutes
takes ... about half an hour
takes, 30 minutes
items.h: The pattern is: It takes + person + time + to + verb.
items.hd: Das Muster ist: It takes + Person + Zeit + to + Verb.
items.e: 'It takes me 30 minutes to get to school' follows the pattern: It takes + object + time expression + to-infinitive.
items.ed: 'It takes me 30 minutes to get to school' folgt dem Muster: It takes + Objekt + Zeitausdruck + to-Infinitiv.

items.p: Your friend complains that homework is quick for everyone else. You explain your sister's situation: 'It ________ her two hours ________ her homework every day.'
a: takes ... to do
takes ... to finish
takes, to do
items.h: It takes + person + time + to + verb. The verb describes what activity takes the time.
items.hd: It takes + Person + Zeit + to + Verb. Das Verb beschreibt, welche Aktivität die Zeit braucht.
items.e: 'It takes her two hours to do her homework' — the to-infinitive completes the pattern.
items.ed: 'It takes her two hours to do her homework' — der to-Infinitiv vervollständigt das Muster.

items.p: You're planning a dinner party and your friend asks about preparation time. Tell them: 'It ________ us about an hour ________ dinner.'
a: takes ... to cook
takes ... to prepare
takes ... to make
takes, to cook
items.h: It takes + person + time + to + verb.
items.hd: It takes + Person + Zeit + to + Verb.
items.e: 'It takes us about an hour to cook dinner' — use 'to cook' (to-infinitive) after the time expression.
items.ed: 'It takes us about an hour to cook dinner' — verwende 'to cook' (to-Infinitiv) nach dem Zeitausdruck.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Ich brauche zehn Minuten, um zur Schule zu gehen.
items.c: It takes me ten minutes to walk to school.
a: It takes me ten minutes to walk to school.
It takes me ten minutes to walk to school
It takes me 10 minutes to walk to school.
It takes me 10 minutes to walk to school
It takes me ten minutes to get to school.
It takes me 10 minutes to get to school.
items.h: German 'Ich brauche...' becomes 'It takes me...' in English. The subject changes!
items.hd: Deutsch 'Ich brauche...' wird zu 'It takes me...' im Englischen. Das Subjekt ändert sich!
items.e: German 'Ich brauche 10 Minuten' uses the person as subject, but English uses 'It takes me 10 minutes to...'
items.ed: Deutsch 'Ich brauche 10 Minuten' hat die Person als Subjekt, aber Englisch verwendet 'It takes me 10 minutes to...'

items.p: 🇩🇪 Wie lange hast du gebraucht, um das Buch zu lesen?
items.c: How long did it take you to read the book?
a: How long did it take you to read the book?
How long did it take you to read the book
How long did it take to read the book?
How long did it take to read the book
items.h: 'Hast du gebraucht' is past tense. Use 'did it take' for a past question.
items.hd: 'Hast du gebraucht' ist Vergangenheit. Verwende 'did it take' für eine vergangene Frage.
items.e: Past question: How long + did + it + take + person + to + verb? German 'Wie lange hast du gebraucht' = 'How long did it take you'.
items.ed: Vergangene Frage: How long + did + it + take + Person + to + Verb? Deutsch 'Wie lange hast du gebraucht' = 'How long did it take you'.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: takes / it / minutes / me / ten / to / school / walk / to
items.c: It takes me ten minutes to walk to school.
a: It takes me ten minutes to walk to school.
It takes me ten minutes to walk to school
items.h: Start with 'It takes' and follow the pattern: person + time + to + verb.
items.hd: Beginne mit 'It takes' und folge dem Muster: Person + Zeit + to + Verb.
items.e: The correct order is: It takes + me + ten minutes + to walk + to school.
items.ed: Die richtige Reihenfolge ist: It takes + me + ten minutes + to walk + to school.

items.p: Match each sentence beginning with the correct ending. 1: It takes me five minutes 2: It took us all day 3: How long does it take you 4: It doesn't take long
items.h: Match the time expressions with activities that make sense for that duration.
items.hd: Ordne die Zeitangaben den Aktivitäten zu, die für diese Dauer Sinn ergeben.
items.e: 1→c (5 minutes → brush teeth), 2→d (all day → climb mountain), 3→a (how long → get to school), 4→b (not long → learn a song).
items.ed: 1→c (5 Minuten → Zähneputzen), 2→d (ganzer Tag → Berg besteigen), 3→a (wie lange → zur Schule kommen), 4→b (nicht lange → ein Lied lernen).

items.p: It takes Lisa thirty minutes to cycle to school. Ask about: how long it takes Lisa.
items.c: How long does it take Lisa to cycle to school?
a: How long does it take Lisa to cycle to school?
How long does it take Lisa to cycle to school
How long does it take her to cycle to school?
How long does it take her to cycle to school
items.h: Start with 'How long does it take...' and keep the 'to + verb' pattern.
items.hd: Beginne mit 'How long does it take...' und behalte das 'to + Verb'-Muster bei.
items.e: Question: How long + does it take + person + to + base verb? The subject stays 'it'.
items.ed: Frage: How long + does it take + Person + to + Grundform? Das Subjekt bleibt 'it'.

items.p: An elephant is ___ bigger than a cat.
a: much
items.h: Which word can come before a comparative like 'bigger'? 'Very' or 'much'?
items.hd: Welches Wort kann vor einem Komparativ wie 'bigger' stehen? 'Very' oder 'much'?
items.e: Use 'much' (not 'very') to intensify comparatives. 'Much bigger' is correct; 'very bigger' is wrong.
items.ed: Verwende 'much' (nicht 'very'), um Komparative zu verstärken. 'Much bigger' ist richtig; 'very bigger' ist falsch.

items.p: This film is ___ more interesting than the last one.
a: a lot
items.h: 'Very' doesn't work before comparatives. Try 'a lot', 'much', or 'far'.
items.hd: 'Very' funktioniert nicht vor Komparativen. Versuche 'a lot', 'much' oder 'far'.
items.e: 'A lot more interesting' is correct. Use 'a lot', 'much', or 'far' before comparatives, never 'very'.
