items.e: The sentence is missing the form of 'be'. It should be 'I'm not allowed to' or 'I am not allowed to'. 'Be allowed to' always needs a form of 'be'.
items.ed: Dem Satz fehlt die Form von 'be'. Es muss 'I'm not allowed to' oder 'I am not allowed to' heißen. 'Be allowed to' braucht immer eine Form von 'be'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She are allowed to go to the party on Saturday.
items.c: She is allowed to go to the party on Saturday.
a: She is allowed to go to the party on Saturday.
She's allowed to go to the party on Saturday.
She is allowed to go to the party on Saturday
items.h: 'She' is 3rd person singular. Does 'are' match?
items.hd: 'She' ist 3. Person Einzahl. Passt 'are' dazu?
items.e: 'She' is 3rd person singular and takes 'is', not 'are'. The correct form is 'She is allowed to go'.
items.ed: 'She' ist 3. Person Einzahl und braucht 'is', nicht 'are'. Die richtige Form ist 'She is allowed to go'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: We aren't allowed go swimming after dark.
items.c: We aren't allowed to go swimming after dark.
a: We aren't allowed to go swimming after dark.
We are not allowed to go swimming after dark.
We aren't allowed to go swimming after dark
items.h: Look carefully between 'allowed' and 'go'. Is something missing?
items.hd: Schau genau zwischen 'allowed' und 'go'. Fehlt etwas?
items.e: The word 'to' is missing after 'allowed'. The structure is 'be allowed TO + base verb'.
items.ed: Das Wort 'to' fehlt nach 'allowed'. Die Struktur ist 'be allowed TO + Grundform'.

items.p: Your exchange student asks about school rules. Explain: 'We ________ (not / be allowed to) use phones in class.'
a: aren't allowed to
are not allowed to
items.h: The pattern is: subject + be (not) allowed to + infinitive. 'We' takes 'are'.
items.hd: Das Muster ist: Subjekt + be (not) allowed to + Infinitiv. 'We' nimmt 'are'.
items.e: 'We aren't allowed to use phones' — be (not) allowed to expresses that permission is not given.
items.ed: 'We aren't allowed to use phones' — be (not) allowed to drückt aus, dass keine Erlaubnis gegeben wird.

items.p: A new student asks where they can eat. Tell them: 'You ________ (be allowed to) eat in the canteen.'
a: are allowed to
items.h: Affirmative: subject + be allowed to + infinitive. 'You' takes 'are'.
items.hd: Bejahend: Subjekt + be allowed to + Infinitiv. 'You' nimmt 'are'.
items.e: 'You are allowed to eat in the canteen' — be allowed to expresses that permission is given.
items.ed: 'You are allowed to eat in the canteen' — be allowed to drückt aus, dass Erlaubnis gegeben wird.

items.p: Describe the rules at your school for a project: 'Students ________ (not / be allowed to) run in the corridors.'
a: aren't allowed to
are not allowed to
items.h: 'Students' is plural, so use 'are'. The negative form is 'are not allowed to'.
items.hd: 'Students' ist Plural, also verwende 'are'. Die negative Form ist 'are not allowed to'.
items.e: 'Students aren't allowed to run in the corridors' — third person plural reinforces 'are (not) allowed to'.
items.ed: 'Students aren't allowed to run in the corridors' — dritte Person Plural bekräftigt 'are (not) allowed to'.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Wir dürfen im Unterricht keine Handys benutzen.
items.c: We aren't allowed to use phones in class.
a: We aren't allowed to use phones in class.
We are not allowed to use phones in class.
We aren't allowed to use our phones in class.
We are not allowed to use our phones in class.
items.h: 'dürfen nicht' = 'are not allowed to'. Think about who the subject is and which form of 'be' fits.
items.hd: 'dürfen nicht' = 'are not allowed to'. Wer ist das Subjekt und welche Form von 'be' passt?
items.e: 'dürfen nicht' translates to 'aren't allowed to'. 'Wir' = 'we', so: 'We aren't allowed to use phones in class.'
items.ed: 'dürfen nicht' wird zu 'aren't allowed to' übersetzt. 'Wir' = 'we', also: 'We aren't allowed to use phones in class.'

items.p: 🇩🇪 Darf ich hier Fotos machen?
items.c: Am I allowed to take photos here?
a: Am I allowed to take photos here?
Am I allowed to take pictures here?
Am I allowed to take photos here
Am I allowed to take pictures here
items.h: 'Darf ich' = 'Am I allowed to'. Remember: questions start with the form of 'be'.
items.hd: 'Darf ich' = 'Am I allowed to'. Denk daran: Fragen beginnen mit der Form von 'be'.
items.e: 'Darf ich' (permission question) becomes 'Am I allowed to'. The question form inverts 'be' and subject.
items.ed: 'Darf ich' (Frage nach Erlaubnis) wird zu 'Am I allowed to'. In der Frageform werden 'be' und Subjekt vertauscht.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: not / you / are / to / allowed / run / here
items.c: You are not allowed to run here.
a: You are not allowed to run here.
You are not allowed to run here
items.h: Start with the subject, then 'are not allowed to', then the verb and place.
items.hd: Beginne mit dem Subjekt, dann 'are not allowed to', dann das Verb und der Ort.
items.e: The word order is: Subject + are not + allowed to + base verb + rest. 'You are not allowed to run here.'
items.ed: Die Wortstellung ist: Subjekt + are not + allowed to + Grundform + Rest. 'You are not allowed to run here.'

items.p: Match each subject with the correct form to complete 'be allowed to go out'. 1: I 2: She 3: We 4: They 5: He
ds: a: is allowed to go out
b: are allowed to go out
c: am allowed to go out
items.h: Think about which form of 'be' (am/is/are) goes with each subject.
items.hd: Überlege, welche Form von 'be' (am/is/are) zu jedem Subjekt passt.
items.e: I → am, She/He → is, We/They → are. The form of 'be' must agree with the subject.
items.ed: I → am, She/He → is, We/They → are. Die Form von 'be' muss zum Subjekt passen.

items.p: Lisa is allowed to stay out until 9 pm. Ask about: what time Lisa is allowed to stay out until.
items.c: What time is Lisa allowed to stay out until?
a: What time is Lisa allowed to stay out until?
Until what time is Lisa allowed to stay out?
What time is Lisa allowed to stay out until
items.h: Start with the question word(s), then move 'is' before 'Lisa'.
items.hd: Beginne mit dem Fragewort/den Fragewörtern, dann stelle 'is' vor 'Lisa'.
items.e: For a question about time with 'be allowed to': What time + is + subject + allowed to...? The form of 'be' moves before the subject.
items.ed: Für eine Frage nach der Zeit mit 'be allowed to': What time + is + Subjekt + allowed to...? Die Form von 'be' kommt vor das Subjekt.

items.p: My parents ___ me go to the cinema on Saturdays.
a: let
items.h: 'My parents' is plural. Does 'let' change for plural subjects in the present?
items.hd: 'My parents' ist Mehrzahl. Ändert sich 'let' für Mehrzahl-Subjekte im Präsens?
items.e: 'My parents' is plural, so we use 'let' (not 'lets'). Remember: let + person + base verb (NO 'to').
items.ed: 'My parents' ist Mehrzahl, also verwenden wir 'let' (nicht 'lets'). Merke: let + Person + Grundform (OHNE 'to').

items.p: The teacher ___ us use dictionaries during the test.
a: lets
items.h: 'The teacher' is 3rd person singular. What happens to verbs in the present with he/she/it?
items.hd: 'The teacher' ist 3. Person Einzahl. Was passiert mit Verben im Präsens bei he/she/it?
items.e: 'The teacher' is 3rd person singular, so we add -s: 'lets'. The structure is: lets + us + base verb (no 'to').
items.ed: 'The teacher' ist 3. Person Einzahl, also fügen wir -s hinzu: 'lets'. Die Struktur ist: lets + uns + Grundform (ohne 'to').

items.p: My mum doesn't ___ me play video games on school nights.
a: let
items.h: After 'doesn't', we always use the base form of the verb.
items.hd: Nach 'doesn't' verwenden wir immer die Grundform des Verbs.
items.e: After 'doesn't', we use the base form: 'doesn't let'. Then: let + person + base verb (no 'to').
items.ed: Nach 'doesn't' verwenden wir die Grundform: 'doesn't let'. Dann: let + Person + Grundform (ohne 'to').

items.p: My parents ___ me stay at my friend's house last weekend.
a: let
items.h: 'Last weekend' shows past tense. What is the past form of 'let'? (Careful — it's irregular!)
items.hd: 'Last weekend' zeigt Vergangenheit. Was ist die Vergangenheitsform von 'let'? (Vorsicht — unregelmaussig!)
items.e: 'Let' is irregular: let → let → let. The past form looks the same as the present. 'My parents let me stay' (past).
items.ed: 'Let' ist unregelmaussig: let → let → let. Die Vergangenheitsform sieht genauso aus wie die Gegenwartsform. 'My parents let me stay' (Vergangenheit).

items.p: My dad doesn't let me ___ his computer without asking.
a: use
items.h: After 'let + person', which form of the verb do we use? Base form, to-infinitive, or -ing?
items.hd: Nach 'let + Person', welche Form des Verbs verwenden wir? Grundform, to-Infinitiv oder -ing?
items.e: After 'let + person', we ALWAYS use the base form (no 'to'): 'let me use'. NOT 'let me to use' or 'let me using'.
items.ed: Nach 'let + Person' verwenden wir IMMER die Grundform (ohne 'to'): 'let me use'. NICHT 'let me to use' oder 'let me using'.

items.p: The coach ___ (not / let) us leave practice early yesterday.
a: didn't let
did not let
items.h: 'Yesterday' tells you the tense. How do you make 'let' negative in the past?
items.hd: 'Yesterday' zeigt die Zeitform. Wie verneint man 'let' in der Vergangenheit?
items.e: Past negative: didn't + base form. 'Didn't let' (NOT 'didn't letted'). Remember: 'did' carries the past, so the verb stays as the base form.
items.ed: Vergangenheitsverneinung: didn't + Grundform. 'Didn't let' (NICHT 'didn't letted'). Denk daran: 'did' trägt die Vergangenheit, also bleibt das Verb in der Grundform.

items.p: Which sentence is correct?
items.c: My sister lets me borrow her clothes.
a: My sister lets me borrow her clothes.
ds: My sister lets me to borrow her clothes.
My sister let me to borrow her clothes.
My sister is let me borrow her clothes.
items.h: 'My sister' is 3rd person singular. Does 'let' need -s? And does 'let' take 'to'?
items.hd: 'My sister' ist 3. Person Einzahl. Braucht 'let' ein -s? Und braucht 'let' ein 'to'?
items.e: 'My sister' is 3rd person singular → 'lets'. And 'let' NEVER takes 'to': lets me borrow (NOT lets me to borrow).
items.ed: 'My sister' ist 3. Person Einzahl → 'lets'. Und 'let' braucht NIE 'to': lets me borrow (NICHT lets me to borrow).

items.p: Choose the correct option: They didn't ___ us play outside.
a: let
items.h: After 'didn't', which form of the verb do you use?
items.hd: Nach 'didn't', welche Form des Verbs verwendest du?
items.e: After 'didn't', always use the base form: 'didn't let'. 'Let' is irregular (let/let/let), so it never becomes 'letted'.
items.ed: Nach 'didn't' immer die Grundform: 'didn't let'. 'Let' ist unregelmaussig (let/let/let), es wird nie zu 'letted'.

items.p: Which pair of sentences mean the same thing?
items.c: They let us go. = We are allowed to go.
a: They let us go. = We are allowed to go.
ds: They let us go. = We can go. (ability)
They let us to go. = We are allowed to go.
They let us go. = We allowed to go.
items.h: 'Let someone do something' = 'someone is allowed to do something'. Watch out for 'to' and missing 'be'.
items.hd: 'Let someone do something' = 'someone is allowed to do something'. Achte auf 'to' und fehlendes 'be'.
items.e: 'They let us go' = 'We are allowed to go.' Both express permission. Note: 'let' has no 'to', but 'be allowed to' does.
items.ed: 'They let us go' = 'We are allowed to go.' Beide drücken Erlaubnis aus. Beachte: 'let' hat kein 'to', aber 'be allowed to' schon.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: My parents let me to go to the park.
items.c: My parents let me go to the park.
a: My parents let me go to the park.
My parents let me go to the park
let me go
items.h: Look at what comes after 'let me'. Does 'let' need 'to' before the verb?
items.hd: Schau, was nach 'let me' kommt. Braucht 'let' ein 'to' vor dem Verb?
items.e: 'Let' is NEVER followed by 'to'. The correct structure is: let + person + base verb. Remove 'to': 'let me go'.
items.ed: Nach 'let' kommt NIE 'to'. Die richtige Struktur ist: let + Person + Grundform. Entferne 'to': 'let me go'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: The coach didn't letted us go home early.
items.c: The coach didn't let us go home early.
a: The coach didn't let us go home early.
The coach didn't let us go home early
The coach did not let us go home early.
didn't let
items.h: 'Let' is an irregular verb. What are its three forms?
items.hd: 'Let' ist ein unregelmäßiges Verb. Was sind seine drei Formen?
items.e: 'Let' is irregular: let/let/let. After 'didn't', use the base form 'let', not 'letted'. There is no such word as 'letted'.
items.ed: 'Let' ist unregelmaussig: let/let/let. Nach 'didn't' verwende die Grundform 'let', nicht 'letted'. Das Wort 'letted' gibt es nicht.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: My dad let me to drive his car yesterday.
items.c: My dad let me drive his car yesterday.
a: My dad let me drive his car yesterday.
My dad let me drive his car yesterday
let me drive
items.h: Look at 'let me to drive'. Does 'let' ever take 'to'?
items.hd: Schau dir 'let me to drive' an. Braucht 'let' jemals 'to'?
items.e: Remove 'to': 'let me drive'. 'Let' is NEVER followed by 'to', not even in the past tense. Note: 'let' is the same in present and past.
items.ed: Entferne 'to': 'let me drive'. Nach 'let' kommt NIE 'to', auch nicht in der Vergangenheit. Beachte: 'let' ist in Gegenwart und Vergangenheit gleich.

items.p: You're telling your friend about your parents' rules. Complete: 'My parents ________ (let) me go to the party.'
a: let
items.h: let + object + infinitive WITHOUT 'to'. No -s ending because 'parents' is plural.
items.hd: let + Objekt + Infinitiv OHNE 'to'. Keine -s Endung, weil 'parents' Plural ist.
items.e: 'My parents let me go' — let + object + bare infinitive (no 'to'). Plural subject = no -s on 'let'.
items.ed: 'My parents let me go' — let + Objekt + Infinitiv ohne 'to'. Pluralsubjekt = kein -s bei 'let'.

items.p: Your pen pal asks about your classroom rules. Explain: 'Our teacher ________ (not / let) us chew gum in class.'
a: doesn't let
does not let
items.h: Third person singular: 'Our teacher' needs 'doesn't let' (not 'don't lets').
items.hd: Dritte Person Singular: 'Our teacher' braucht 'doesn't let' (nicht 'don't lets').
items.e: 'Our teacher doesn't let us chew gum' — third person singular negative: doesn't + base form.
items.ed: 'Our teacher doesn't let us chew gum' — dritte Person Singular negativ: doesn't + Grundform.

items.p: You're chatting with a friend about bedtime rules at home. Tell them: 'My mum ________ (not / let) my brother stay up late.'
a: doesn't let
does not let
items.h: 'My mum' is third person singular. Negative: doesn't + let.
items.hd: 'My mum' ist dritte Person Singular. Verneinung: doesn't + let.
items.e: 'My mum doesn't let my brother stay up late' — let + object + bare infinitive in negative form.
items.ed: 'My mum doesn't let my brother stay up late' — let + Objekt + Infinitiv ohne 'to' in der Verneinungsform.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Meine Eltern lassen mich am Wochenende länger aufbleiben.
items.c: My parents let me stay up longer at the weekend.
a: My parents let me stay up longer at the weekend.
My parents let me stay up later at the weekend.
My parents let me stay up longer on the weekend.
My parents let me stay up later at weekends.
My parents let me stay up longer at weekends.
items.h: 'lassen mich' = 'let me'. Remember: no 'to' after 'let'!
items.hd: 'lassen mich' = 'let me'. Denk daran: kein 'to' nach 'let'!
items.e: 'lassen' = 'let' (no 'to'). 'Meine Eltern lassen mich' = 'My parents let me'. Then add the base verb directly: 'let me stay up'.
items.ed: 'lassen' = 'let' (ohne 'to'). 'Meine Eltern lassen mich' = 'My parents let me'. Dann die Grundform direkt: 'let me stay up'.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Der Lehrer lässt uns nicht im Unterricht essen.
items.c: The teacher doesn't let us eat in class.
a: The teacher doesn't let us eat in class.
The teacher does not let us eat in class.
The teacher doesn't let us eat during class.
The teacher doesn't let us eat in lessons.
items.h: 'lässt uns nicht' = 'doesn't let us'. The teacher is 3rd person singular.
items.hd: 'lässt uns nicht' = 'doesn't let us'. Der Lehrer ist 3. Person Einzahl.
items.e: 'lässt nicht' for 3rd person = 'doesn't let'. Structure: The teacher doesn't let us + base verb (eat).
items.ed: 'lässt nicht' für 3. Person = 'doesn't let'. Struktur: The teacher doesn't let us + Grundform (eat).

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: doesn't / the teacher / talk / let / in class / us
items.c: The teacher doesn't let us talk in class.
a: The teacher doesn't let us talk in class.
The teacher doesn't let us talk in class
items.h: Start with the subject, then 'doesn't let', then the person, then the base verb.
items.hd: Beginne mit dem Subjekt, dann 'doesn't let', dann die Person, dann die Grundform.
items.e: Word order: Subject + doesn't let + object (us) + base verb (talk) + rest. No 'to' after 'let'!
items.ed: Wortstellung: Subjekt + doesn't let + Objekt (us) + Grundform (talk) + Rest. Kein 'to' nach 'let'!

items.p: Match each sentence with the correct verb pattern. 1: My parents let me 2: My parents allow me 3: My parents don't let me 4: I'm not allowed
items.h: 'Let' takes the base verb directly (no 'to'). 'Allow' and 'be allowed' need 'to'.
items.hd: 'Let' nimmt die Grundform direkt (ohne 'to'). 'Allow' und 'be allowed' brauchen 'to'.
items.e: 'Let + person + base verb' (no 'to'): 1→b, 3→b. 'Allow + person + to + verb' / 'be allowed + to + verb': 2→a, 4→a.
items.ed: 'Let + Person + Grundform' (ohne 'to'): 1→b, 3→b. 'Allow + Person + to + Verb' / 'be allowed + to + Verb': 2→a, 4→a.

items.p: My parents let me go out on Saturdays. Ask about: whether your parents let you go out on Saturdays.
items.c: Do your parents let you go out on Saturdays?
a: Do your parents let you go out on Saturdays?
Do your parents let you go out on Saturdays
items.h: 'Let' is not 'be', so questions use 'do/does'. 'My parents' is plural.
items.hd: 'Let' ist nicht 'be', also verwenden Fragen 'do/does'. 'My parents' ist Mehrzahl.
items.e: 'Let' forms questions with 'do/does' (not 'be'). Plural subject → 'Do': 'Do your parents let you go out...?'
items.ed: 'Let' bildet Fragen mit 'do/does' (nicht 'be'). Mehrzahl-Subjekt → 'Do': 'Do your parents let you go out...?'

items.p: I ___ swim when I was five years old.
a: could
items.h: This is a general ability in the past (something you could do over a period of time).
items.hd: Das ist eine allgemeine Fähigkeit in der Vergangenheit (etwas, das du über einen Zeitraum konntest).
items.e: For general past ability (things you could do over time), we use 'could': 'I could swim when I was five.'
items.ed: Für allgemeine Fähigkeiten in der Vergangenheit (Dinge, die man über einen Zeitraum konnte) verwenden wir 'could': 'I could swim when I was five.'

items.p: She ___ ride a bike until she was eight.
a: couldn't
could not
items.h: This is about a general inability in the past. Which modal expresses past inability?
items.hd: Es geht um eine allgemeine Unfähigkeit in der Vergangenheit. Welches Modalverb drückt vergangene Unfähigkeit aus?
items.e: 'Couldn't' works for both general and specific past inability. 'She couldn't ride a bike' = she didn't have this ability.
items.ed: 'Couldn't' funktioniert für allgemeine und spezifische vergangene Unfähigkeit. 'She couldn't ride a bike' = sie hatte diese Fähigkeit nicht.

items.p: After practising for weeks, she ___ finally pass the maths test.
a: was able to
items.h: This is a specific achievement on one occasion (she managed to pass). Which form do we use for one-time achievements?
items.hd: Das ist eine spezifische Leistung bei einer Gelegenheit (sie hat es geschafft zu bestehen). Welche Form verwenden wir für einmalige Leistungen?
items.e: For specific one-time achievements in the past, we use 'was/were able to' (not 'could'). 'She was able to pass' = she managed to pass on that occasion.
items.ed: Für spezifische einmalige Leistungen in der Vergangenheit verwenden wir 'was/were able to' (nicht 'could'). 'She was able to pass' = sie hat es bei dieser Gelegenheit geschafft.

items.p: My grandmother ___ speak three languages when she was young.
a: could
items.h: Speaking three languages is a general ability over time, not a one-time event.
items.hd: Drei Sprachen zu sprechen ist eine allgemeine Fähigkeit über einen Zeitraum, kein einmaliges Ereignis.
items.e: Speaking languages is a general ability. For general past ability, 'could' is preferred: 'She could speak three languages.'
items.ed: Sprachen sprechen ist eine allgemeine Fähigkeit. Für allgemeine vergangene Fähigkeiten ist 'could' besser: 'She could speak three languages.'

items.p: The fire was very dangerous, but the firefighters ___ rescue everyone.
a: were able to
items.h: This was one specific event (one rescue). Is this general ability or a specific achievement?
items.hd: Das war ein spezifisches Ereignis (eine Rettung). Ist das eine allgemeine Fähigkeit oder eine spezifische Leistung?
items.e: Rescuing everyone from one fire is a specific one-time achievement. Use 'were able to' (not 'could') for single occasions.
items.ed: Alle aus einem Feuer zu retten ist eine spezifische einmalige Leistung. Verwende 'were able to' (nicht 'could') für einzelne Gelegenheiten.

items.p: Next year, she ___ to join the school basketball team.
a: will be able
items.h: 'Next year' is the future. You can't say 'will can'. Which structure works for future ability?
items.hd: 'Next year' ist die Zukunft. Man kann nicht 'will can' sagen. Welche Struktur funktioniert für zukünftige Fähigkeit?
items.e: For future ability, we use 'will be able to' because 'can' has no future form. You cannot say 'will can'. Don't forget 'be'!
items.ed: Für zukünftige Fähigkeit verwenden wir 'will be able to', weil 'can' keine Zukunftsform hat. Man kann nicht 'will can' sagen. Vergiss 'be' nicht!

items.p: Which sentence is correct?
items.c: After running for two hours, he was able to finish the marathon.
a: After running for two hours, he was able to finish the marathon.
ds: After running for two hours, he could finish the marathon.
After running for two hours, he was able finish the marathon.
After running for two hours, he could to finish the marathon.
items.h: Finishing a marathon is a specific one-time achievement. Which form fits?
items.hd: Einen Marathon zu beenden ist eine spezifische einmalige Leistung. Welche Form passt?
items.e: Finishing the marathon was a specific achievement, so we use 'was able to finish' (not 'could'). Don't forget 'to' after 'able'.
items.ed: Den Marathon zu beenden war eine spezifische Leistung, also verwenden wir 'was able to finish' (nicht 'could'). Vergiss 'to' nach 'able' nicht.

items.p: Which sentence correctly expresses GENERAL past ability?
items.c: When I was a child, I could climb trees really well.
a: When I was a child, I could climb trees really well.
ds: When I was a child, I was able to climb trees really well.
When I was a child, I can climb trees really well.
When I was a child, I could to climb trees really well.
items.h: General past ability (over time, not just one occasion) uses which modal?
items.hd: Allgemeine vergangene Fähigkeit (über einen Zeitraum, nicht nur einmal) verwendet welches Modalverb?
items.e: 'Could' is the natural choice for general past ability. 'I could climb trees' = I had this ability as a child (over time).
items.ed: 'Could' ist die natürliche Wahl für allgemeine vergangene Fähigkeit. 'I could climb trees' = Ich hatte diese Fähigkeit als Kind (über einen Zeitraum).

items.p: Choose the correct option: She ___ to come to the party tomorrow.
a: will be able
items.h: 'Tomorrow' is the future. Can has no future form, so we need a different structure.
items.hd: 'Tomorrow' ist die Zukunft. 'Can' hat keine Zukunftsform, also brauchen wir eine andere Struktur.
items.e: For future ability, use 'will be able to'. You cannot combine two modals ('will can'). Don't forget 'be'!
items.ed: Für zukünftige Fähigkeit verwende 'will be able to'. Man kann nicht zwei Modalverben kombinieren ('will can'). Vergiss 'be' nicht!

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: I was able swim across the lake last summer.
items.c: I was able to swim across the lake last summer.
a: I was able to swim across the lake last summer.
I was able to swim across the lake last summer
was able to swim
items.h: Look at 'able swim'. Is something missing between these two words?
items.hd: Schau dir 'able swim' an. Fehlt etwas zwischen diesen beiden Wörtern?
items.e: The word 'to' is missing. The structure is 'was/were able TO + base verb'. Always include 'to'.
items.ed: Das Wort 'to' fehlt. Die Struktur ist 'was/were able TO + Grundform'. 'To' darf nie fehlen.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: I will able to come to your party next Saturday.
items.c: I will be able to come to your party next Saturday.
a: I will be able to come to your party next Saturday.
I will be able to come to your party next Saturday
I'll be able to come to your party next Saturday.
items.h: Look at 'will able to'. Is something missing between 'will' and 'able'?
items.hd: Schau dir 'will able to' an. Fehlt etwas zwischen 'will' und 'able'?
items.e: 'Be' is missing. The structure is 'will BE able to + base verb'. The full chain is: will + be + able + to + verb.
items.ed: 'Be' fehlt. Die Struktur ist 'will BE able to + Grundform'. Die vollständige Kette ist: will + be + able + to + Verb.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She was able to swimming really fast when she was ten.
items.c: She was able to swim really fast when she was ten.
a: She was able to swim really fast when she was ten.
She could swim really fast when she was ten.
She was able to swim really fast when she was ten
items.h: After 'to' in 'able to', which form of the verb do we use? Base form, -ing, or past?
items.hd: Nach 'to' in 'able to', welche Verbform verwenden wir? Grundform, -ing oder Vergangenheit?
items.e: After 'able to', we use the BASE form: 'able to swim' (NOT 'able to swimming'). 'To' is always followed by the base form.
items.ed: Nach 'able to' verwenden wir die GRUNDFORM: 'able to swim' (NICHT 'able to swimming'). Auf 'to' folgt immer die Grundform.

items.p: Tell a story about what you could do as a child. Complete: 'When I was five, I ________ already swim.'
a: could
items.h: 'Could' is used for general past ability — something you were able to do regularly or as a skill.
items.hd: 'Could' wird für allgemeine vergangene Fähigkeit verwendet — etwas, das du regelmäßig oder als Fertigkeit konntest.
items.e: 'Could' describes general past ability: 'I could swim' = I had the ability to swim (general skill).
items.ed: 'Could' beschreibt allgemeine vergangene Fähigkeit: 'I could swim' = Ich hatte die Fähigkeit zu schwimmen (allgemeine Fertigkeit).

items.p: You're writing about a famous person for a school project. Complete: 'Beethoven ________ hear later in his life, but he still composed amazing music.'
a: couldn't
could not
items.h: 'Couldn't' = general past inability. Beethoven lost his hearing — he was generally unable to hear.
items.hd: 'Couldn't' = allgemeine vergangene Unfähigkeit. Beethoven verlor sein Gehör — er konnte generell nicht hören.
items.e: 'Couldn't' for general past inability: Beethoven couldn't hear (permanent loss of ability).
items.ed: 'Couldn't' für allgemeine vergangene Unfähigkeit: Beethoven couldn't hear (dauerhafter Verlust der Fähigkeit).

items.p: You're telling your friend about something difficult you managed to do. Complete: 'The maths test was really hard, but I ________ finish it in time.'
a: was able to
managed to
items.h: This is about a specific achievement (one occasion, not a general ability). 'Could' doesn't work for one-time successes.
items.hd: Es geht um eine bestimmte Leistung (ein Mal, keine allgemeine Fähigkeit). 'Could' funktioniert nicht für einmalige Erfolge.
items.e: 'Was able to' (or 'managed to') is used for a specific achievement on one occasion. 'Could' is for general ability.
items.ed: 'Was able to' (oder 'managed to') wird für eine bestimmte Leistung bei einer Gelegenheit verwendet. 'Could' ist für allgemeine Fähigkeit.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Ich konnte schwimmen, als ich fünf war.
items.c: I could swim when I was five.
a: I could swim when I was five.
I could swim when I was five years old.
I could swim when I was 5.
I could swim when I was five
items.h: 'konnte' for a general ability = 'could'. Swimming is a general skill, not a one-time event.
items.hd: 'konnte' für eine allgemeine Fähigkeit = 'could'. Schwimmen ist eine allgemeine Fähigkeit, kein einmaliges Ereignis.
items.e: 'konnte schwimmen' (general ability) = 'could swim'. German 'konnte' can mean both 'could' and 'was able to', but for general ability, use 'could'.
items.ed: 'konnte schwimmen' (allgemeine Fähigkeit) = 'could swim'. Deutsch 'konnte' kann sowohl 'could' als auch 'was able to' bedeuten, aber für allgemeine Fähigkeiten verwende 'could'.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Nach vielen Versuchen konnte er die Prüfung bestehen.
items.c: After many attempts, he was able to pass the exam.
a: After many attempts, he was able to pass the exam.
After many attempts he was able to pass the exam.
After many tries, he was able to pass the exam.
After many attempts, he was able to pass the test.
items.h: 'konnte' for a one-time achievement = 'was able to'. Passing an exam is a specific event.
items.hd: 'konnte' für eine einmalige Leistung = 'was able to'. Eine Prüfung bestehen ist ein spezifisches Ereignis.
items.e: 'konnte bestehen' (specific one-time achievement) = 'was able to pass'. In German, 'konnte' covers both, but in English you must choose: 'could' (general) vs 'was able to' (specific).
items.ed: 'konnte bestehen' (spezifische einmalige Leistung) = 'was able to pass'. Im Deutschen deckt 'konnte' beides ab, aber im Englischen muss man wählen: 'could' (allgemein) vs 'was able to' (spezifisch).

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: could / when / I / read / was / I / six
items.c: I could read when I was six.
a: I could read when I was six.
I could read when I was six
When I was six I could read.
When I was six, I could read.
items.h: Start with 'I could' + verb, then add the time clause with 'when'.
items.hd: Beginne mit 'I could' + Verb, dann füge den Zeitsatz mit 'when' hinzu.
items.e: Word order: Subject + could + base verb + when-clause. Or: When-clause + subject + could + base verb.
items.ed: Wortstellung: Subjekt + could + Grundform + when-Satz. Oder: When-Satz + Subjekt + could + Grundform.

items.p: Match each sentence with the correct form. 1: I ___ play the piano as a child. (general ability) 2: After lots of practice, I ___ play the difficult piece. (specific achievement) 3: Next year, I ___ play in the school band. (future) 4: She ___ cook when she was a teenager. (general ability) 5: He ___ fix the computer yesterday. (specific achievement)
items.h: General past ability = could. Specific past achievement = was/were able to. Future = will be able to.
items.hd: Allgemeine vergangene Fähigkeit = could. Spezifische vergangene Leistung = was/were able to. Zukunft = will be able to.
items.e: 1→could (general), 2→was able to (specific achievement), 3→will be able to (future), 4→could (general), 5→was able to (specific achievement).
items.ed: 1→could (allgemein), 2→was able to (spezifische Leistung), 3→will be able to (Zukunft), 4→could (allgemein), 5→was able to (spezifische Leistung).

items.p: Tom could play the guitar when he was eight. Ask about: what Tom could play when he was eight.
items.c: What could Tom play when he was eight?
a: What could Tom play when he was eight?
What could Tom play when he was eight
What could Tom play when he was 8?
items.h: Question word + modal + subject + base verb. 'Could' moves before the subject.
items.hd: Fragewort + Modalverb + Subjekt + Grundform. 'Could' kommt vor das Subjekt.
items.e: Questions with 'could': What + could + subject + base verb? 'What could Tom play when he was eight?'
items.ed: Fragen mit 'could': What + could + Subjekt + Grundform? 'What could Tom play when he was eight?'

items.p: You're excited about turning 18. Tell your friend what will change: 'When I'm 18, I ________ (be allowed to) drive a car!'
a: will be allowed to
'll be allowed to
items.h: Future permission: will be allowed to + infinitive.
items.hd: Zukünftige Erlaubnis: will be allowed to + Infinitiv.
items.e: 'Will be allowed to' expresses future permission. It combines the future 'will' with 'be allowed to'.
items.ed: 'Will be allowed to' drückt zukünftige Erlaubnis aus. Es kombiniert das Futur 'will' mit 'be allowed to'.

items.p: Form a question: 'She was allowed to go to the concert.' → Ask a yes/no question.
items.c: Was she allowed to go to the concert?
a: Was she allowed to go to the concert?
Was she allowed to go to the concert
items.h: Move 'was' to the front to form a yes/no question.
items.hd: Stelle 'was' an den Anfang, um eine Ja/Nein-Frage zu bilden.
items.e: To form a yes/no question with 'was allowed to', move 'was' before the subject: 'Was she allowed to...?'
items.ed: Um eine Ja/Nein-Frage mit 'was allowed to' zu bilden, stelle 'was' vor das Subjekt: 'Was she allowed to...?'

items.p: You're texting your friend about weekend plans. The weather forecast says it will stop raining by Saturday. Write: 'Don't worry! We ________ to the park on Saturday.'
a: will be able to go
items.h: Future ability: will be able to + infinitive.
items.hd: Zukünftige Fähigkeit: will be able to + Infinitiv.
items.e: 'Will be able to go' — future ability form, because the weather will make it possible.
items.ed: 'Will be able to go' — Zukunftsform der Fähigkeit, weil das Wetter es möglich machen wird.

items.p: Form a question: 'He will be able to help us tomorrow.' → Ask a yes/no question.
items.c: Will he be able to help us tomorrow?
a: Will he be able to help us tomorrow?
Will he be able to help us tomorrow
items.h: Move 'will' to the front to form a yes/no question.
items.hd: Stelle 'will' an den Anfang, um eine Ja/Nein-Frage zu bilden.
items.e: For yes/no questions with 'will be able to', move 'will' before the subject: 'Will he be able to...?'
items.ed: Für Ja/Nein-Fragen mit 'will be able to' stelle 'will' vor das Subjekt: 'Will he be able to...?'

items.p: You arrive at the sports ground and see your friends are exhausted and sweaty. They started playing at 3 pm and it's now 5 pm. You say: 'You ________ (play) football for two hours!'
a: have been playing
've been playing
items.h: Visible evidence (exhaustion) + duration (for two hours). Which tense combines ongoing action with duration?
items.hd: Sichtbarer Beweis (Erschöpfung) + Dauer (for two hours). Welche Zeitform kombiniert andauernde Handlung mit Dauer?
items.e: Present perfect continuous: 'You have been playing for two hours' — ongoing action with visible evidence and a duration expression.
items.ed: Present perfect continuous: 'You have been playing for two hours' — andauernde Handlung mit sichtbarem Beweis und einem Dauerausdruck.

items.p: You're writing a paragraph for English class about why English is important. Write: 'English ________ (speak) in more than 50 countries and it ________ (use) in international business.'
a: is spoken ... is used
is spoken, is used
items.h: General facts about English — the subject receives the action. Present simple passive: is + past participle.
items.hd: Allgemeine Fakten über Englisch — das Subjekt empfängt die Handlung. Present simple passive: is + past participle.
items.e: Present simple passive for general facts: 'English is spoken' and 'it is used' — is + past participle.
items.ed: Present simple passive für allgemeine Fakten: 'English is spoken' und 'it is used' — is + past participle.

items.p: Your friend never does homework and always gets bad marks. You're giving advice in a kind way. You say: 'If I ________ (be) you, I ________ (study) a bit more before the test.'
a: were ... would study
were ... 'd study
were, would study
items.h: 'If I were you' is a fixed phrase in the second conditional. Use 'were' (not 'was') for advice.
items.hd: 'If I were you' ist eine feste Redewendung im Second Conditional. Verwende 'were' (nicht 'was') für Ratschläge.
items.e: 'If I were you, I would study' — 'were' (not 'was') is the standard form in the if-clause of the second conditional, especially with 'I'.
items.ed: 'If I were you, I would study' — 'were' (nicht 'was') ist die Standardform im if-Satz des Second Conditional, besonders mit 'I'.

items.p: You're in the school corridor and you see a classmate carrying a huge pile of books. The books are wobbling. You warn her: 'Careful! You ________ (drop) those books!'
a: are going to drop
're going to drop
items.h: Wobbling books = visible evidence of what's about to happen. Which future form is for evidence-based predictions?
items.hd: Wackelnde Bücher = sichtbarer Beweis dafür, was gleich passieren wird. Welche Zukunftsform ist für evidenzbasierte Vorhersagen?
items.e: 'Going to' for warnings based on present evidence. The wobbling books are clear evidence she's about to drop them.
items.ed: 'Going to' für Warnungen basierend auf gegenwärtigen Beweisen. Die wackelnden Bücher sind eindeutiger Beweis, dass sie gleich fallen wird.

items.p: You see dark clouds in the sky. Which sentence fits?
a: It's going to rain.
ds: It rains every day in November.
items.h: You can SEE the dark clouds. Which future form is for predictions based on present evidence?
items.hd: Du kannst die dunklen Wolken SEHEN. Welche Zukunftsform ist für Vorhersagen basierend auf gegenwärtigen Beweisen?
items.e: 'Going to' is used for predictions based on present evidence. Dark clouds = evidence that rain is coming.
items.ed: 'Going to' wird für Vorhersagen basierend auf gegenwärtigen Beweisen verwendet. Dunkle Wolken = Beweis, dass Regen kommt.

items.p: My dad ___ (wash) the car when I got home.
a: was washing
items.h: 'My dad' is singular. Which past form of 'be' matches?
items.hd: 'My dad' ist Einzahl. Welche Vergangenheitsform von 'be' passt?
items.e: 'My dad' is 3rd person singular, so it takes 'was' + -ing. The washing was in progress when you arrived.
items.ed: 'My dad' ist 3. Person Einzahl, also braucht man 'was' + -ing. Das Autowaschen war im Gange, als du ankamst.

items.p: The children ___ (run) around the garden when it started raining.
a: were running
items.h: 'The children' is plural. Which past form of 'be' goes with plural subjects?
items.hd: 'The children' ist Mehrzahl. Welche Vergangenheitsform von 'be' passt zu Mehrzahl-Subjekten?
items.e: 'The children' is plural, so it takes 'were' + -ing. They were running (background) when rain started (interruption).
items.ed: 'The children' ist Mehrzahl, also braucht man 'were' + -ing. Sie liefen (Hintergrund), als der Regen begann (Unterbrechung).

items.p: I ___ (not/listen) to the teacher when she asked me a question.
a: wasn't listening
was not listening
items.h: You need the negative past continuous. 'I' takes 'was', then add 'not' and the -ing form.
items.hd: Du brauchst das verneinte Past Continuous. 'I' braucht 'was', dann 'not' und die -ing-Form.
items.e: Negative past continuous: was/were + not + -ing. 'I wasn't listening' is the correct form.
items.ed: Verneintes Past Continuous: was/were + not + -ing. 'I wasn't listening' ist die richtige Form.

items.p: While my friends ___ (swim) in the lake, I ___ (sit) on the beach.
a: were swimming ... was sitting
were swimming...was sitting
items.h: 'My friends' is plural (were), 'I' is singular (was). Both actions were happening at the same time.
items.hd: 'My friends' ist Mehrzahl (were), 'I' ist Einzahl (was). Beide Handlungen liefen gleichzeitig.
items.e: Two simultaneous past actions: 'my friends' (plural) → were swimming; 'I' (singular) → was sitting.
items.ed: Zwei gleichzeitige Handlungen: 'my friends' (Mehrzahl) → were swimming; 'I' (Einzahl) → was sitting.

items.p: ___ you ___ (wait) for the bus when the accident happened?
a: Were ... waiting
Were...waiting
items.h: For a past continuous question, put 'was/were' before the subject. 'You' takes which form?
items.hd: Für eine Past-Continuous-Frage stelle 'was/were' vor das Subjekt. 'You' braucht welche Form?
items.e: Past continuous question: Was/Were + subject + -ing? 'You' always takes 'were'.
items.ed: Past-Continuous-Frage: Was/Were + Subjekt + -ing? 'You' braucht immer 'were'.

items.p: My mum ___ (not/drive) when the tyre blew out. My dad ___ (drive).
a: wasn't driving ... was driving
was not driving ... was driving
wasn't driving...was driving
was not driving...was driving
items.h: Both 'my mum' and 'my dad' are singular subjects. One sentence is negative, the other positive.
items.hd: Sowohl 'my mum' als auch 'my dad' sind Einzahl-Subjekte. Ein Satz ist verneint, der andere nicht.
items.e: Both singular subjects take 'was'. Negative: wasn't driving. Positive: was driving. Both describe ongoing actions at the moment of the tyre blowout.
items.ed: Beide Einzahl-Subjekte brauchen 'was'. Verneint: wasn't driving. Positiv: was driving. Beide beschreiben laufende Handlungen im Moment des Reifenplatzers.

items.p: Which sentence correctly uses the past continuous?
items.c: My brother was playing the guitar when I came home.
a: My brother was playing the guitar when I came home.
ds: My brother were playing the guitar when I came home.
My brother was play the guitar when I came home.
My brother playing the guitar when I came home.
items.h: Past continuous = was/were + verb-ing. Check the subject to pick the right auxiliary.
items.hd: Past Continuous = was/were + Verb-ing. Prüfe das Subjekt, um das richtige Hilfsverb zu wählen.
items.e: 'My brother' is singular → 'was' + 'playing'. The guitar was the ongoing action; coming home was the interruption.
items.ed: 'My brother' ist Einzahl → 'was' + 'playing'. Das Gitarrespielen war die laufende Handlung; das Heimkommen war die Unterbrechung.

items.p: Choose the correct sentence about what was happening at 10 pm last night.
items.c: We were watching a horror film at 10 pm.
a: We were watching a horror film at 10 pm.
ds: We was watching a horror film at 10 pm.
We watched a horror film at 10 pm.
We are watching a horror film at 10 pm.
items.h: At 10 pm, the action was still happening. 'We' takes which auxiliary?
items.hd: Um 10 Uhr war die Handlung noch im Gange. 'We' braucht welches Hilfsverb?
items.e: 'We were watching' uses past continuous because the action was in progress at the specific time. 'We' always takes 'were'.
items.ed: 'We were watching' steht im Past Continuous, weil die Handlung zur angegebenen Zeit noch lief. 'We' braucht immer 'were'.

items.p: Which sentence correctly describes two things happening at the same time?
items.c: While I was cooking, my sister was setting the table.
a: While I was cooking, my sister was setting the table.
ds: While I cooked, my sister setted the table.
While I was cooking, my sister set the table.
While I cooking, my sister setting the table.
items.h: 'While' + two actions happening at the same time: both should be in the past continuous.
items.hd: 'While' + zwei gleichzeitige Handlungen: beide sollten im Past Continuous stehen.
items.e: When 'while' connects two simultaneous ongoing actions, both use past continuous: was cooking + was setting.
items.ed: Wenn 'while' zwei gleichzeitige laufende Handlungen verbindet, stehen beide im Past Continuous: was cooking + was setting.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: They was having a picnic in the park.
a: were having
They were having a picnic in the park.
They were having a picnic in the park
items.h: 'They' is plural. Which form of 'be' matches plural subjects in the past?
items.hd: 'They' ist Mehrzahl. Welche Form von 'be' passt zu Mehrzahl-Subjekten in der Vergangenheit?
items.e: 'They' takes 'were', not 'was'. Correct: 'They were having a picnic.'
items.ed: 'They' braucht 'were', nicht 'was'. Richtig: 'They were having a picnic.'

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: He was studieing for his English test all evening.
a: studying
was studying
He was studying for his English test all evening.
He was studying for his English test all evening
items.h: Look at the spelling of the -ing form. What happens to the 'y' in 'study'?
items.hd: Schau dir die Schreibweise der -ing-Form an. Was passiert mit dem 'y' bei 'study'?
items.e: When adding -ing to verbs ending in consonant + y, just add -ing: study → studying (not 'studieing'). The 'y' stays.
items.ed: Beim Hinzufügen von -ing zu Verben, die auf Konsonant + y enden, fügt man einfach -ing hinzu: study → studying (nicht 'studieing'). Das 'y' bleibt.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She was reading a magazine while the cat was siting on her lap.
a: sitting
was sitting
She was reading a magazine while the cat was sitting on her lap.
She was reading a magazine while the cat was sitting on her lap
items.h: Check the spelling of the -ing form. Does 'sit' double the final consonant?
items.hd: Prüfe die Schreibweise der -ing-Form. Verdoppelt sich der letzte Konsonant bei 'sit'?
items.e: Short verbs (one syllable, one vowel, one consonant) double the final consonant: sit → sitting, not 'siting'.
items.ed: Kurze Verben (eine Silbe, ein Vokal, ein Konsonant) verdoppeln den letzten Konsonanten: sit → sitting, nicht 'siting'.

items.p: Your friend asks what happened yesterday after school. Describe the scene: 'My best friend ________ (ride) his bike and I ________ (walk) next to him when a dog ________ (run) across the road.'
a: was riding ... was walking ... ran
was riding...was walking...ran
items.h: Two longer actions were happening (continuous) when one sudden event occurred (simple).
items.hd: Zwei längere Handlungen liefen (Verlaufsform), als ein plötzliches Ereignis eintrat (einfache Vergangenheit).
items.e: The riding and walking were ongoing (past continuous), while the dog running across was a sudden event (past simple).
items.ed: Das Radfahren und Gehen waren laufende Handlungen (Past Continuous), während der Hund, der über die Straße lief, ein plötzliches Ereignis war (Past Simple).

items.p: You saw something funny at the park. Tell your friend: 'A man ________ (try) to fly a kite, but the wind ________ (not/blow) at all!'
a: was trying ... wasn't blowing
was trying ... was not blowing
was trying...wasn't blowing
was trying...was not blowing
items.h: Both actions describe what was happening at that moment. The second one is negative.
items.hd: Beide Handlungen beschreiben, was in dem Moment passierte. Die zweite ist verneint.
items.e: Both are describing the scene (past continuous). 'Was trying' and 'wasn't blowing' — both singular subjects take 'was'.
items.ed: Beide beschreiben die Szene (Past Continuous). 'Was trying' und 'wasn't blowing' — beide Einzahl-Subjekte brauchen 'was'.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Während wir zu Abend aßen, klingelte plötzlich das Telefon.
items.c: While we were having dinner, the phone suddenly rang.
a: While we were having dinner, the phone suddenly rang.
While we were having dinner, the phone suddenly rang
While we were eating dinner, the phone suddenly rang.
While we were eating dinner, the phone suddenly rang
While we were having dinner, the phone rang suddenly.
While we were eating dinner, the phone rang suddenly.
items.h: 'Während wir aßen' = ongoing background action. 'Klingelte' = sudden interruption. Different tenses!
items.hd: 'Während wir aßen' = laufende Hintergrundhandlung (Past Continuous). 'Klingelte' = plötzliche Unterbrechung (Past Simple).
items.e: 'While' + past continuous (were having dinner) for the background; past simple (rang) for the sudden event.
items.ed: 'While' + Past Continuous (were having dinner) für den Hintergrund; Past Simple (rang) für das plötzliche Ereignis.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Hast du gerade geschlafen, als ich dich angerufen habe?
items.c: Were you sleeping when I called you?
a: Were you sleeping when I called you?
Were you sleeping when I phoned you?
Were you sleeping when I rang you?
items.h: German 'Hast du gerade geschlafen' needs past continuous in English. The question inverts 'were' and 'you'.
items.hd: 'Hast du gerade geschlafen' braucht im Englischen Past Continuous. Die Frage stellt 'were' und 'you' um.
items.e: German Perfekt + 'gerade' → English past continuous question: Were you sleeping...? The interruption (I called) is past simple.
items.ed: Deutsches Perfekt + 'gerade' → englische Past-Continuous-Frage: Were you sleeping...? Die Unterbrechung (I called) steht im Past Simple.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: was / homework / her / doing / she / when / rang / the / bell
items.c: She was doing her homework when the bell rang.
a: She was doing her homework when the bell rang.
She was doing her homework when the bell rang
items.h: Start with the subject + was + -ing verb, then 'when' + the interrupting event.
items.hd: Beginne mit Subjekt + was + Verb-ing, dann 'when' + das unterbrechende Ereignis.
items.e: Past continuous clause first (She was doing her homework), then 'when' + past simple for the interruption (the bell rang).
items.ed: Zuerst der Past-Continuous-Satz (She was doing her homework), dann 'when' + Past Simple für die Unterbrechung (the bell rang).

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: not / was / sleeping / at / he / midnight / was / he / reading
items.c: He was not sleeping at midnight. He was reading.
a: He was not sleeping at midnight. He was reading.
He was not sleeping at midnight, he was reading.
He wasn't sleeping at midnight. He was reading.
He wasn't sleeping at midnight, he was reading.
At midnight he was not sleeping. He was reading.
At midnight he wasn't sleeping. He was reading.
items.h: Make a negative past continuous sentence, then a positive one. The time expression can go at the end or beginning.
items.hd: Bilde einen verneinten Past-Continuous-Satz, dann einen positiven. Der Zeitausdruck kann am Ende oder Anfang stehen.
items.e: Negative: He was not sleeping. Positive: He was reading. Both describe ongoing actions at midnight.
items.ed: Verneint: He was not sleeping. Positiv: He was reading. Beide beschreiben laufende Handlungen um Mitternacht.

items.p: Match each person with what they were doing at 6 pm. 1: Mum 2: Dad 3: My sister 4: The dog 5: I
ds: b: was sleeping in its basket.
e: was talking on the phone.
items.h: All sentences use 'was' + -ing because they describe what was happening at 6 pm. Match each person to a typical activity.
items.hd: Alle Sätze verwenden 'was' + -ing, weil sie beschreiben, was um 18 Uhr passierte. Ordne jeder Person eine typische Aktivität zu.
items.e: All actions use past continuous (was + -ing) because they were in progress at the specific time of 6 pm.
items.ed: Alle Handlungen stehen im Past Continuous (was + -ing), weil sie um 18 Uhr gerade im Gange waren.

items.p: You're telling a story about what was happening when the fire alarm went off at school. Which sentence fits?
items.c: Our teacher was explaining the exercise when the alarm went off.
a: Our teacher was explaining the exercise when the alarm went off.
ds: Our teacher explained the exercise when the alarm went off.
Our teacher explains the exercise when the alarm goes off.
Our teacher has explained the exercise when the alarm went off.
items.h: The explaining was the longer background action. The alarm was the sudden interruption.
items.hd: Das Erklären war die längere Hintergrundhandlung. Der Alarm war die plötzliche Unterbrechung.
items.e: Past continuous (was explaining) for the background action in progress; past simple (went off) for the sudden interruption.
items.ed: Past Continuous (was explaining) für die laufende Hintergrundhandlung; Past Simple (went off) für die plötzliche Unterbrechung.

items.p: ___ she finished her homework, she went outside to play.
a: After
items.h: First she finished homework, then she played. Which connector shows the order?
items.hd: Zuerst erledigte sie die Hausaufgaben, dann spielte sie. Welcher Konnektor zeigt die Reihenfolge?
items.e: 'After' introduces the first action in a sequence: After A happened, B happened.
items.ed: 'After' leitet die erste Handlung in einer Reihenfolge ein: After A happened, B happened.

items.p: The baby fell asleep ___ the car ride.
a: during
items.h: 'The car ride' is a noun. Which connector goes before a noun?
items.hd: 'The car ride' ist ein Nomen. Welcher Konnektor steht vor einem Nomen?
items.e: 'During' + noun phrase: 'during the car ride'. Use 'while' only before a clause (subject + verb).
items.ed: 'During' + Nominalphrase: 'during the car ride'. Verwende 'while' nur vor einem Satz (Subjekt + Verb).

items.p: ___ the teacher was explaining the rules, some students were talking.
a: While
items.h: The explaining and talking were happening at the same time. The gap is followed by a full clause.
items.hd: Das Erklären und Reden passierten gleichzeitig. Auf die Lücke folgt ein ganzer Satz.
items.e: 'While' + clause describes two simultaneous actions. 'While the teacher was explaining' = during that time.
items.ed: 'While' + Satz beschreibt zwei gleichzeitige Handlungen. 'While the teacher was explaining' = während dieser Zeit.

items.p: We always brush our teeth ___ we go to bed.
a: before
items.h: We brush first, then go to bed. Which word shows the brushing happens first?
items.hd: Wir putzen zuerst die Zähne, dann gehen wir ins Bett. Welches Wort zeigt, dass das Putzen zuerst passiert?
items.e: 'Before' shows sequence: the brushing happens first, then going to bed.
items.ed: 'Before' zeigt die Reihenfolge: Das Zähneputzen passiert zuerst, dann das Ins-Bett-Gehen.

items.p: I saw my favourite singer ___ the concert last Saturday.
a: during
items.h: 'The concert' is a noun phrase. Which connector is used before nouns, not clauses?
items.hd: 'The concert' ist eine Nominalphrase. Welcher Konnektor steht vor Nomen, nicht vor Sätzen?
items.e: 'During' + noun: 'during the concert'. 'While' would need a clause: 'while I was at the concert'.
items.ed: 'During' + Nomen: 'during the concert'. 'While' bräuchte einen Satz: 'while I was at the concert'.

items.p: We didn't leave the cinema ___ the film was over.
a: until
till
items.h: We stayed and waited for the end. Which word means 'up to the point when'?
items.hd: Wir blieben und warteten auf das Ende. Welches Wort bedeutet 'bis zu dem Zeitpunkt, als'?
items.e: 'Until' means 'up to the time when'. We stayed in the cinema and left when the film ended.
items.ed: 'Until' bedeutet 'bis zu dem Zeitpunkt, als'. Wir blieben im Kino und gingen, als der Film zu Ende war.

items.p: Which sentence uses 'during' correctly?
items.c: I fell asleep during the maths lesson.
a: I fell asleep during the maths lesson.
ds: I fell asleep during the teacher was talking.
I fell asleep during was the maths lesson.
I fell asleep during I was in class.
items.h: 'During' can only go before a noun or noun phrase, never before a clause.
items.hd: 'During' steht nur vor einem Nomen oder einer Nominalphrase, nie vor einem Satz.
items.e: 'During the maths lesson' is correct because 'the maths lesson' is a noun phrase. The others wrongly use 'during' before clauses.
items.ed: 'During the maths lesson' ist richtig, weil 'the maths lesson' eine Nominalphrase ist. Die anderen verwenden 'during' fälschlicherweise vor Sätzen.

items.p: Choose the correct sentence.
items.c: Before we left for school, we had breakfast.
a: Before we left for school, we had breakfast.
ds: Before we left for school, we were having breakfast.
During we left for school, we had breakfast.
While the breakfast, we left for school.
items.h: 'Before' introduces a sequence: one thing happens first, then another.
items.hd: 'Before' zeigt eine Reihenfolge: Erst passiert etwas, dann etwas anderes.
items.e: 'Before we left, we had breakfast' — both completed actions in past simple. 'Before' shows the order clearly.
items.ed: 'Before we left, we had breakfast' — beide abgeschlossene Handlungen im Past Simple. 'Before' zeigt die Reihenfolge.

items.p: Which sentence correctly uses 'when' and 'while'?
items.c: While she was reading, the lights went out when there was a storm.
a: While she was reading, the lights went out when there was a storm.
ds: During she was reading, the lights went out when there was a storm.
While she was reading, the lights went out while there was a storm.
When she was reading, the lights went out during there was a storm.
items.h: 'While' goes with the ongoing action. 'When' introduces the sudden event or the new situation.
items.hd: 'While' steht bei der laufenden Handlung. 'When' leitet das plötzliche Ereignis oder die neue Situation ein.
items.e: 'While' + ongoing action (was reading), 'when' + sudden event or time reference (there was a storm).
items.ed: 'While' + laufende Handlung (was reading), 'when' + plötzliches Ereignis oder Zeitangabe (there was a storm).

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: While the lunch break, we played football.
a: During
During the lunch break
During the lunch break, we played football.
During the lunch break, we played football
items.h: 'The lunch break' is a noun phrase. Which connector goes before noun phrases?
items.hd: 'The lunch break' ist eine Nominalphrase. Welcher Konnektor steht vor Nominalphrasen?
items.e: 'While' needs a clause (subject + verb). Before a noun phrase like 'the lunch break', use 'during'.
items.ed: 'While' braucht einen Satz (Subjekt + Verb). Vor einer Nominalphrase wie 'the lunch break' verwende 'during'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: During my mum was cooking, I watched a video.
a: While
While my mum was cooking
While my mum was cooking, I watched a video.
While my mum was cooking, I watched a video
items.h: 'My mum was cooking' is a clause (subject + verb). 'During' can't go before clauses.
items.hd: 'My mum was cooking' ist ein Satz (Subjekt + Verb). 'During' steht nicht vor Sätzen.
items.e: 'During' only goes before nouns. Before a full clause, use 'while': 'While my mum was cooking...'
items.ed: 'During' steht nur vor Nomen. Vor einem ganzen Satz verwende 'while': 'While my mum was cooking...'

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: I always eat an apple before I will go to training.
a: go
before I go
I always eat an apple before I go to training.
I always eat an apple before I go to training
items.h: After 'before', do we use 'will' or present simple for future events?
items.hd: Nach 'before' verwendet man 'will' oder Present Simple für zukünftige Ereignisse?
items.e: After time connectors (before, after, when, while, until), use present simple — NOT 'will' — even for future meaning.
items.ed: Nach Zeitkonnektoren (before, after, when, while, until) verwendet man Present Simple — NICHT 'will' — auch wenn die Zukunft gemeint ist.

items.p: Your friend wants to know about your morning routine. Complete with the right connector: 'I have a shower ________ (before/after) I eat breakfast. Then ________ (during/while) I'm eating, I check my phone.'
a: before ... while
before...while
items.h: Shower first, then breakfast. 'I'm eating' is a clause, not a noun.
items.hd: Erst duschen, dann frühstücken. 'I'm eating' ist ein Satz, kein Nomen.
items.e: 'Before I eat' — shower happens first. 'While I'm eating' — 'while' before a clause (subject + verb).
items.ed: 'Before I eat' — Duschen passiert zuerst. 'While I'm eating' — 'while' vor einem Satz (Subjekt + Verb).

items.p: You're writing an email about a school trip. Complete: '________ (During/While) the bus ride, we sang songs. ________ (When/During) we arrived at the museum, it was already closed.'
a: During ... When
During...When
items.h: 'The bus ride' is a noun phrase. 'We arrived' is a clause with subject + verb.
items.hd: 'The bus ride' ist eine Nominalphrase. 'We arrived' ist ein Satz mit Subjekt + Verb.
items.e: 'During' + noun (the bus ride). 'When' + clause (we arrived). Each connector matches its grammatical partner.
items.ed: 'During' + Nomen (the bus ride). 'When' + Satz (we arrived). Jeder Konnektor passt zu seinem grammatischen Partner.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Bevor wir ins Kino gingen, haben wir Pizza gegessen.
items.c: Before we went to the cinema, we ate pizza.
a: Before we went to the cinema, we ate pizza.
Before we went to the cinema, we ate pizza
Before we went to the cinema, we had pizza.
Before we went to the cinema, we had pizza
We ate pizza before we went to the cinema.
We had pizza before we went to the cinema.
items.h: 'Bevor' = 'before'. Both actions are completed in the past.
items.hd: 'Bevor' = 'before'. Beide Handlungen sind in der Vergangenheit abgeschlossen.
items.e: 'Before' introduces the later action in the sequence. Both clauses use past simple for completed events.
items.ed: 'Before' leitet die spätere Handlung ein. Beide Satzteile verwenden Past Simple für abgeschlossene Ereignisse.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Während der Pause hat es angefangen zu regnen.
items.c: During the break, it started to rain.
a: During the break, it started to rain.
During the break, it started to rain
During the break, it started raining.
During the break, it started raining
It started to rain during the break.
It started raining during the break.
items.h: 'Während der Pause' = 'during the break'. 'Pause' is a noun, so use 'during', not 'while'.
items.hd: 'Während der Pause' = 'during the break'. 'Pause' ist ein Nomen, also verwende 'during', nicht 'while'.
items.e: German 'während' can mean both 'during' (+ noun) and 'while' (+ clause). Here it's + noun, so 'during'.
items.ed: Deutsch 'während' kann sowohl 'during' (+ Nomen) als auch 'while' (+ Satz) bedeuten. Hier steht es vor einem Nomen, also 'during'.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: after / home / got / we / watched / we / TV
items.c: After we got home, we watched TV.
a: After we got home, we watched TV.
After we got home, we watched TV
After we got home we watched TV.
We watched TV after we got home.
We watched TV after we got home
items.h: 'After' introduces the first event. Then comes the second event.
items.hd: 'After' leitet das erste Ereignis ein. Dann kommt das zweite Ereignis.
items.e: 'After we got home' = the first action, 'we watched TV' = the second action. The 'after' clause can come first or second.
items.ed: 'After we got home' = die erste Handlung, 'we watched TV' = die zweite Handlung. Der 'after'-Satz kann am Anfang oder Ende stehen.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: while / sleeping / was / I / alarm / the / rang
items.c: While I was sleeping, the alarm rang.
a: While I was sleeping, the alarm rang.
While I was sleeping, the alarm rang
While I was sleeping the alarm rang.
The alarm rang while I was sleeping.
The alarm rang while I was sleeping
items.h: 'While' + subject + was + -ing for the ongoing action. Then the short event.
items.hd: 'While' + Subjekt + was + -ing für die laufende Handlung. Dann das kurze Ereignis.
items.e: 'While I was sleeping' (past continuous for the background) + 'the alarm rang' (past simple for the interruption).
items.ed: 'While I was sleeping' (Past Continuous für den Hintergrund) + 'the alarm rang' (Past Simple für die Unterbrechung).

items.p: Match each connector with the correct ending. 1: While I was doing my homework, 2: During the film, 3: Before the lesson started, 4: After we finished eating, 5: When the teacher arrived,
ds: c: my phone vibrated three times.
d: my cat jumped on my desk.
items.h: Think about the meaning: 'while' = at the same time, 'during' + noun, 'before' = first, 'after' = then, 'when' = at that moment.
items.hd: Denke an die Bedeutung: 'while' = gleichzeitig, 'during' + Nomen, 'before' = zuerst, 'after' = danach, 'when' = in dem Moment.
items.e: 1→d (while doing homework → sudden interruption), 2→c (during + noun phrase), 3→e (before = preparation), 4→a (after = consequence), 5→b (when = immediate reaction).
items.ed: 1→d (while Hausaufgaben → plötzliche Unterbrechung), 2→c (during + Nomen), 3→e (before = Vorbereitung), 4→a (after = Folge), 5→b (when = sofortige Reaktion).

items.p: Your friend says: 'I saw a celebrity.' Ask them when it happened. Use 'while' or 'when' in your question. Start with: 'Did you see them while...' or 'When did you...'
items.c: Did you see them while you were shopping?
a: Did you see them while you were shopping?
When did you see them?
Did you see them while you were walking?
Did you see them while you were in town?
When did you see the celebrity?
items.h: You can use 'When did you...?' or 'Did you see them while you were...?'
items.hd: Du kannst 'When did you...?' oder 'Did you see them while you were...?' verwenden.
items.e: 'When' asks about the time. 'While you were...' asks about what was happening at the same time as the sighting.
items.ed: 'When' fragt nach der Zeit. 'While you were...' fragt, was gleichzeitig mit der Sichtung passierte.

items.p: It ___ her about fifteen minutes to get dressed in the morning.
a: takes
items.h: The subject is 'it'. In present simple, what ending does the verb need?
items.hd: Das Subjekt ist 'it'. Im Present Simple, welche Endung braucht das Verb?
items.e: 'It takes' is 3rd person singular present simple. The structure is: It takes + person + time + to + verb.
items.ed: 'It takes' ist 3. Person Einzahl Present Simple. Die Struktur ist: It takes + Person + Zeit + to + Verb.

items.p: It ___ the whole class thirty minutes to finish the test yesterday.
a: took
items.h: 'Yesterday' tells you the sentence is about the past. What is the past form of 'take'?
items.hd: 'Yesterday' zeigt, dass der Satz von der Vergangenheit handelt. Was ist die Vergangenheitsform von 'take'?
items.e: 'Take' is irregular: take → took → taken. With a past time word like 'yesterday', use 'It took...'.
items.ed: 'Take' ist unregelmäßig: take → took → taken. Mit einem Vergangenheitswort wie 'yesterday' verwende 'It took...'.

items.p: It takes me an hour ___ do my homework every day.
a: to
items.h: The structure is: It takes + person + time + ___ + verb. What little word comes before the verb?
items.hd: Die Struktur ist: It takes + Person + Zeit + ___ + Verb. Welches kleine Wort kommt vor dem Verb?
items.e: The structure requires 'to' before the infinitive: It takes me an hour TO do my homework.
items.ed: Die Struktur verlangt 'to' vor dem Infinitiv: It takes me an hour TO do my homework.

items.p: How long does it ___ you to cycle to school?
a: take
items.h: After 'does', the main verb goes back to its base form.
items.hd: Nach 'does' steht das Hauptverb in der Grundform.
items.e: In questions: Does it + base form (take)? 'Does' already carries the 3rd person marking, so no -s on 'take'.
items.ed: In Fragen: Does it + Grundform (take)? 'Does' trägt bereits die 3.-Person-Markierung, also kein -s bei 'take'.

items.p: It will ___ us about three hours to drive to the seaside.
a: take
items.h: After 'will', which form of the verb do you use?
items.hd: Nach 'will', welche Form des Verbs verwendet man?
items.e: After 'will' (and all modal verbs), use the base form: 'It will take us three hours.'
items.ed: Nach 'will' (und allen Modalverben) verwendet man die Grundform: 'It will take us three hours.'

items.p: It didn't ___ them very long to learn the new dance.
a: take
items.h: After 'didn't', the main verb is always in its base form.
items.hd: Nach 'didn't' steht das Hauptverb immer in der Grundform.
items.e: Negative past: didn't + base form. 'It didn't take them long' is the correct structure.
items.ed: Verneinte Vergangenheit: didn't + Grundform. 'It didn't take them long' ist die richtige Struktur.

items.p: Which sentence is correct?
items.c: It takes me ten minutes to walk to the bus stop.
a: It takes me ten minutes to walk to the bus stop.
ds: It takes me ten minutes for walk to the bus stop.
It takes I ten minutes to walk to the bus stop.
It takes to me ten minutes to walk to the bus stop.
items.h: The structure is: It takes + object pronoun (me/you/him) + time + to + verb.
items.hd: Die Struktur ist: It takes + Objektpronomen (me/you/him) + Zeit + to + Verb.
items.e: 'It takes me' — use the object pronoun directly after 'takes'. Then: time + to + infinitive.
items.ed: 'It takes me' — verwende das Objektpronomen direkt nach 'takes'. Dann: Zeit + to + Infinitiv.

items.p: Which sentence correctly talks about the past?
items.c: It took us two hours to finish the project.
a: It took us two hours to finish the project.
ds: It taked us two hours to finish the project.
It takes us two hours to finish the project yesterday.
It was taking us two hours to finish the project.
items.h: The past of 'take' is irregular. It's not 'taked'!
items.hd: Die Vergangenheitsform von 'take' ist unregelmäßig. Es heißt nicht 'taked'!
items.e: 'Take' is irregular: take → took. 'It took us two hours' is the correct past form of 'It takes'.
items.ed: 'Take' ist unregelmäßig: take → took. 'It took us two hours' ist die richtige Vergangenheitsform von 'It takes'.

items.p: Which question is correct?
items.c: How long does it take you to get to school?
a: How long does it take you to get to school?
ds: How long does it takes you to get to school?
How long it takes you to get to school?
How long takes it you to get to school?
items.h: Question form: How long + does + it + base form (take)?
items.hd: Frageform: How long + does + it + Grundform (take)?
items.e: Questions with 'How long': How long does it take + person + to + verb? 'Does' requires the base form 'take'.
items.ed: Fragen mit 'How long': How long does it take + Person + to + Verb? 'Does' verlangt die Grundform 'take'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: It needs me twenty minutes to walk to school.
a: takes
It takes me
It takes me twenty minutes to walk to school.
It takes me twenty minutes to walk to school
items.h: German uses 'brauchen' (to need) for time, but English uses a different verb.
items.hd: Im Deutschen sagt man 'brauchen', aber im Englischen verwendet man ein anderes Verb.
items.e: L1 transfer: German 'Ich brauche 20 Minuten' → wrong: 'It needs me...'. Correct: 'It takes me 20 minutes.'
items.ed: L1-Transfer: 'Ich brauche 20 Minuten' → falsch: 'It needs me...'. Richtig: 'It takes me 20 minutes.'

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: It taked us a long time to find the restaurant.
a: took
It took us
It took us a long time to find the restaurant.
It took us a long time to find the restaurant
items.h: 'Take' is an irregular verb. What is its past form?
items.hd: 'Take' ist ein unregelmäßiges Verb. Was ist seine Vergangenheitsform?
items.e: 'Take' is irregular: take → took → taken. The form 'taked' does not exist. Correct: 'It took us...'.
items.ed: 'Take' ist unregelmäßig: take → took → taken. Die Form 'taked' gibt es nicht. Richtig: 'It took us...'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: It takes me thirty minutes for getting to school.
a: to get
to get to school
It takes me thirty minutes to get to school.
It takes me thirty minutes to get to school
items.h: After the time expression, what comes before the verb? 'For + -ing' or 'to + base form'?
items.hd: Nach dem Zeitausdruck, was kommt vor dem Verb? 'For + -ing' oder 'to + Grundform'?
items.e: The structure requires 'to + infinitive', not 'for + -ing': 'It takes me 30 minutes TO GET to school.'
items.ed: Die Struktur verlangt 'to + Infinitiv', nicht 'for + -ing': 'It takes me 30 minutes TO GET to school.'

items.p: You're telling your pen pal about your daily routine. Complete: 'It ________ (take) me about five minutes ________ (brush) my teeth and wash my face.'
a: takes ... to brush
takes...to brush
items.h: It's a daily routine (present simple). The verb after the time needs 'to + base form'.
items.hd: Es ist eine tägliche Routine (Present Simple). Das Verb nach der Zeitangabe braucht 'to + Grundform'.
items.e: 'It takes' (present simple for routines) + me + time + 'to brush' (to + infinitive).
items.ed: 'It takes' (Present Simple für Routinen) + me + Zeit + 'to brush' (to + Infinitiv).

items.p: You're telling your friend about last weekend's hiking trip. Complete: 'It ________ (take) us four hours ________ (reach) the top of the mountain.'
a: took ... to reach
took...to reach
items.h: It was last weekend — past tense. The verb 'take' is irregular in the past.
items.hd: Es war letztes Wochenende — Vergangenheit. Das Verb 'take' ist in der Vergangenheit unregelmäßig.
items.e: Past: 'It took' (irregular past of 'take') + us + time + 'to reach' (to + infinitive).
items.ed: Vergangenheit: 'It took' (unregelmäßige Vergangenheit von 'take') + us + Zeit + 'to reach' (to + Infinitiv).

items.p: 🇩🇪 Wie lange hast du gebraucht, um den Aufsatz zu schreiben?
items.c: How long did it take you to write the essay?
a: How long did it take you to write the essay?
How long did it take you to write the essay
items.h: Past question: 'How long did it take you to...?' German 'Wie lange hast du gebraucht' = 'How long did it take you'.
items.hd: Vergangene Frage: 'How long did it take you to...?' 'Wie lange hast du gebraucht' = 'How long did it take you'.
items.e: German 'Wie lange hast du gebraucht, um... zu...' → 'How long did it take you to...?' Past question with 'did'.
items.ed: 'Wie lange hast du gebraucht, um... zu...' → 'How long did it take you to...?' Vergangene Frage mit 'did'.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: takes / it / to / me / half an hour / homework / do / my
items.c: It takes me half an hour to do my homework.
a: It takes me half an hour to do my homework.
It takes me half an hour to do my homework
items.h: Start with 'It takes', then the person, then the time, then 'to' + verb.
items.hd: Beginne mit 'It takes', dann die Person, dann die Zeit, dann 'to' + Verb.
items.e: Word order: It takes + me + half an hour + to do + my homework.
items.ed: Wortstellung: It takes + me + half an hour + to do + my homework.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: long / did / it / how / take / you / to / there / get / ?
items.c: How long did it take you to get there?
a: How long did it take you to get there?
How long did it take you to get there
items.h: Question word + did + it + base form + person + to + verb.
items.hd: Fragewort + did + it + Grundform + Person + to + Verb.
items.e: Question order: How long + did + it + take + you + to get + there?
items.ed: Fragereihenfolge: How long + did + it + take + you + to get + there?

items.p: Match each beginning with its correct ending. 1: It takes me ten minutes 2: It took her two hours 3: It will take us a whole day 4: How long does it take you 5: It doesn't take long
ds: b: to get ready in the morning?
c: to walk to the bus stop.
e: to finish the puzzle yesterday.
items.h: Match the tenses: 'takes' = present routine, 'took' = past, 'will take' = future. All end with 'to + verb'.
items.hd: Ordne die Zeitformen zu: 'takes' = gegenwärtige Routine, 'took' = Vergangenheit, 'will take' = Zukunft. Alle enden mit 'to + Verb'.
items.e: 1→c (daily routine), 2→e (past with 'yesterday'), 3→a (future journey), 4→b (question about routine), 5→d (quick action).
items.ed: 1→c (tägliche Routine), 2→e (Vergangenheit mit 'yesterday'), 3→a (zukünftige Reise), 4→b (Frage über Routine), 5→d (schnelle Handlung).

items.p: Your new classmate lives far away. Ask how long it takes them to get to school. Start with: 'How long...'
items.c: How long does it take you to get to school?
a: How long does it take you to get to school?
How long does it take you to come to school?
How long does it take you to travel to school?
items.h: How long + does + it + take + you + to + verb?
items.hd: How long + does + it + take + you + to + Verb?
items.e: The question form is: 'How long does it take you to + infinitive?' This asks about the duration of getting to school.
items.ed: Die Frageform ist: 'How long does it take you to + Infinitiv?' Das fragt nach der Dauer des Schulwegs.

items.p: A cheetah is ___ faster than a human.
a: much
items.h: Which word goes before comparatives — 'very' or 'much'?
items.hd: Welches Wort steht vor Komparativen — 'very' oder 'much'?
items.e: 'Much' intensifies comparatives: 'much faster'. 'Very' cannot go before comparatives.
items.ed: 'Much' verstärkt Komparative: 'much faster'. 'Very' kann nicht vor Komparativen stehen.

items.p: My new phone is ___ better than my old one.
a: a lot
items.h: Which expression can go before a comparative like 'better'? Not 'very'!
items.hd: Welcher Ausdruck kann vor einem Komparativ wie 'better' stehen? Nicht 'very'!
items.e: 'A lot better' is correct. You can use 'much', 'a lot', 'far', or 'a bit' before comparatives — never 'very'.
items.ed: 'A lot better' ist richtig. Man kann 'much', 'a lot', 'far' oder 'a bit' vor Komparativen verwenden — nie 'very'.

items.p: This test was only ___ harder than the last one. Don't worry!
a: a bit
a little
items.h: The speaker is reassuring someone — only a small difference. Which intensifier means 'slightly'?
items.hd: Der Sprecher beruhigt jemanden — nur ein kleiner Unterschied. Welcher Verstärker bedeutet 'etwas'?
items.e: 'A bit harder' = slightly harder. 'A bit' shows a small difference. 'Much' or 'a lot' would mean a big difference.
items.ed: 'A bit harder' = etwas schwieriger. 'A bit' zeigt einen kleinen Unterschied. 'Much' oder 'a lot' würden einen großen Unterschied bedeuten.

items.p: Today is ___ colder than yesterday. I need a warmer jacket.
a: much
a lot
far
items.h: You need a word that makes the comparative stronger. 'Very' doesn't work with comparatives.
items.hd: Du brauchst ein Wort, das den Komparativ verstärkt. 'Very' funktioniert nicht mit Komparativen.
items.e: 'Much colder', 'a lot colder', or 'far colder' — all correct intensifiers for comparatives.
items.ed: 'Much colder', 'a lot colder' oder 'far colder' — alles richtige Verstärker für Komparative.

items.p: The book is ___ more exciting than the film. You should read it!
a: far
much
a lot
items.h: You need a strong intensifier before 'more exciting'. Try 'far', 'much', or 'a lot'.
items.hd: Du brauchst einen starken Verstärker vor 'more exciting'. Versuche 'far', 'much' oder 'a lot'.
items.e: 'Far more exciting' is a strong intensifier. 'Much' and 'a lot' also work before comparatives.
items.ed: 'Far more exciting' ist ein starker Verstärker. 'Much' und 'a lot' funktionieren auch vor Komparativen.

items.p: The new computer is only ___ more expensive than the old model, but it's ___ faster.
a: slightly ... much
slightly...much
a bit ... much
a bit...much
a little ... much
a little...much
items.h: The price is only a small difference ('only ___'), but the speed is a big difference.
items.hd: Der Preis ist nur ein kleiner Unterschied ('only ___'), aber die Geschwindigkeit ist ein großer Unterschied.
items.e: 'Slightly/a bit more expensive' = small difference. 'Much faster' = big difference. Context determines the intensifier.
items.ed: 'Slightly/a bit more expensive' = kleiner Unterschied. 'Much faster' = großer Unterschied. Der Kontext bestimmt den Verstärker.

items.p: Which sentence is correct?
items.c: Summer is much hotter than winter.
a: Summer is much hotter than winter.
ds: Summer is very hotter than winter.
Summer is more hotter than winter.
Summer is most hotter than winter.
items.h: Before comparatives, use 'much', 'a lot', or 'far' — never 'very' or 'more' before a -er form.
items.hd: Vor Komparativen verwende 'much', 'a lot' oder 'far' — nie 'very' oder 'more' vor einer -er-Form.
items.e: 'Much hotter' is correct. 'Very' can't go before comparatives, and 'more hotter' is a double comparative.
items.ed: 'Much hotter' ist richtig. 'Very' kann nicht vor Komparativen stehen, und 'more hotter' ist ein doppelter Komparativ.

items.p: My new school is ___ bigger than my old one.
a: a lot
items.h: Which of these words can come before a comparative like 'bigger'?
items.hd: Welches dieser Wörter kann vor einem Komparativ wie 'bigger' stehen?
items.e: 'A lot bigger' is correct. Only 'much', 'a lot', 'far', 'a bit', and 'slightly' can intensify comparatives.
