items.ed: 'A lot bigger' ist richtig. Nur 'much', 'a lot', 'far', 'a bit' und 'slightly' können Komparative verstärken.

items.p: Which pair of sentences uses intensifiers correctly?
items.c: The film was a bit longer than I expected, but it was much better than the first one.
a: The film was a bit longer than I expected, but it was much better than the first one.
ds: The film was very longer than I expected, but it was very better than the first one.
The film was a bit longer than I expected, but it was very better than the first one.
The film was more longer than I expected, but it was much more better than the first one.
items.h: Comparatives use 'much', 'a lot', 'far', 'a bit', 'slightly' — never 'very' or 'more + -er form'.
items.hd: Komparative verwenden 'much', 'a lot', 'far', 'a bit', 'slightly' — nie 'very' oder 'more + -er-Form'.
items.e: 'A bit longer' (small difference) and 'much better' (big difference) — both correctly intensified comparatives.
items.ed: 'A bit longer' (kleiner Unterschied) und 'much better' (großer Unterschied) — beide korrekt verstärkte Komparative.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: My dog is very bigger than yours.
a: much bigger
a lot bigger
My dog is much bigger than yours.
My dog is a lot bigger than yours.
items.h: 'Very' doesn't go before comparatives. Use a different intensifier.
items.hd: 'Very' steht nicht vor Komparativen. Verwende einen anderen Verstärker.
items.e: 'Very' cannot modify comparatives. Use 'much', 'a lot', or 'far': 'much bigger', not 'very bigger'.
items.ed: 'Very' kann Komparative nicht verstärken. Verwende 'much', 'a lot' oder 'far': 'much bigger', nicht 'very bigger'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: Science is much more harder than art.
a: much harder
Science is much harder than art.
Science is much harder than art
items.h: 'Harder' is already the comparative form. Do you also need 'more'?
items.hd: 'Harder' ist schon die Komparativform. Brauchst du auch noch 'more'?
items.e: Double comparative: 'more harder' is wrong because 'harder' already IS the comparative. Correct: 'much harder'.
items.ed: Doppelter Komparativ: 'more harder' ist falsch, weil 'harder' bereits der Komparativ IST. Richtig: 'much harder'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: The maths test was very more difficult than the English test.
a: much more difficult
a lot more difficult
far more difficult
The maths test was much more difficult than the English test.
items.h: 'More difficult' is a comparative. What can go before it — 'very' or 'much'?
items.hd: 'More difficult' ist ein Komparativ. Was kann davor stehen — 'very' oder 'much'?
items.e: 'Very' cannot intensify comparatives. Replace with 'much', 'a lot', or 'far': 'much more difficult'.
items.ed: 'Very' kann Komparative nicht verstärken. Ersetze durch 'much', 'a lot' oder 'far': 'much more difficult'.

items.p: You're comparing two holiday destinations for your family. Complete: 'Croatia is ________ (much/cheap) than Switzerland, but Switzerland is ________ (a bit/beautiful).'
items.c: much cheaper ... a bit more beautiful
a: much cheaper ... a bit more beautiful
much cheaper...a bit more beautiful
items.h: 'Cheap' → 'cheaper' (one syllable = -er). 'Beautiful' → 'more beautiful' (3 syllables = 'more'). Add the intensifiers!
items.hd: 'Cheap' → 'cheaper' (eine Silbe = -er). 'Beautiful' → 'more beautiful' (3 Silben = 'more'). Füge die Verstärker hinzu!
items.e: 'Much cheaper' (big difference, short adjective) and 'a bit more beautiful' (small difference, long adjective).
items.ed: 'Much cheaper' (großer Unterschied, kurzes Adjektiv) und 'a bit more beautiful' (kleiner Unterschied, langes Adjektiv).

items.p: You're telling a friend about two video games. Complete: 'Game A is only ________ (slightly/easy) than Game B, but Game B is ________ (far/exciting).'
a: slightly easier ... far more exciting
slightly easier...far more exciting
items.h: 'Easy' → 'easier' (ends in -y). 'Exciting' → 'more exciting' (long adjective). Add the intensifiers.
items.hd: 'Easy' → 'easier' (endet auf -y). 'Exciting' → 'more exciting' (langes Adjektiv). Füge die Verstärker hinzu.
items.e: 'Slightly easier' = only a small difference. 'Far more exciting' = a very big difference.
items.ed: 'Slightly easier' = nur ein kleiner Unterschied. 'Far more exciting' = ein sehr großer Unterschied.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Das neue Videospiel ist nur ein bisschen teurer als das alte, aber es ist viel besser.
items.c: The new video game is only a bit more expensive than the old one, but it is much better.
a: The new video game is only a bit more expensive than the old one, but it is much better.
The new video game is only a bit more expensive than the old one, but it is much better
The new video game is only a little more expensive than the old one, but it is much better.
The new video game is only slightly more expensive than the old one, but it's much better.
The new video game is only a bit more expensive than the old one, but it's a lot better.
items.h: 'Ein bisschen teurer' = 'a bit more expensive'. 'Viel besser' = 'much better'. Both use comparative intensifiers.
items.hd: 'Ein bisschen teurer' = 'a bit more expensive'. 'Viel besser' = 'much better'. Beide verwenden Komparativ-Verstärker.
items.e: 'A bit more expensive' (small difference with long adjective). 'Much better' (big difference with irregular comparative).
items.ed: 'A bit more expensive' (kleiner Unterschied mit langem Adjektiv). 'Much better' (großer Unterschied mit unregelmäßigem Komparativ).

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: than / is / a lot / football / popular / more / tennis
items.c: Football is a lot more popular than tennis.
a: Football is a lot more popular than tennis.
Football is a lot more popular than tennis
items.h: Subject + is + intensifier + comparative + than + comparison.
items.hd: Subjekt + is + Verstärker + Komparativ + than + Vergleich.
items.e: Word order: Football is + a lot (intensifier) + more popular (comparative) + than tennis.
items.ed: Wortstellung: Football is + a lot (Verstärker) + more popular (Komparativ) + than tennis.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: only / is / a bit / tablet / the / lighter / than / the / laptop
items.c: The tablet is only a bit lighter than the laptop.
a: The tablet is only a bit lighter than the laptop.
The tablet is only a bit lighter than the laptop
items.h: Subject + is + only + intensifier + comparative + than + comparison.
items.hd: Subjekt + is + only + Verstärker + Komparativ + than + Vergleich.
items.e: The tablet is + only a bit (small intensifier) + lighter (comparative) + than the laptop.
items.ed: The tablet is + only a bit (kleiner Verstärker) + lighter (Komparativ) + than the laptop.

items.p: Match each intensifier with the best sentence. 1: much 2: a lot 3: a bit 4: slightly 5: far
ds: a: ___ more dangerous than I thought. (The river was very rough!)
b: ___ more interesting than the last unit. (I really love this topic!)
c: ___ taller than his sister. (They are almost the same height.)
d: ___ better than yesterday. (I feel great now!)
e: ___ harder than I expected. (But I still passed.)
items.h: 'Much', 'a lot', 'far' = big difference. 'A bit', 'slightly' = small difference. Match to the context!
items.hd: 'Much', 'a lot', 'far' = großer Unterschied. 'A bit', 'slightly' = kleiner Unterschied. Ordne dem Kontext zu!
items.e: Strong intensifiers (much, a lot, far) for big differences. Weak ones (a bit, slightly) for small differences.
items.ed: Starke Verstärker (much, a lot, far) für große Unterschiede. Schwache (a bit, slightly) für kleine Unterschiede.

items.p: Your friend says: 'I think basketball is better than football.' You want to know how much better. Form a question using a comparative intensifier. Start with: 'Is it...' or 'How much...'
items.c: Is it much better or just a bit better?
a: Is it much better or just a bit better?
How much better is basketball than football?
Is it a lot better or only a bit better?
How much better do you think it is?
items.h: Use 'How much better...' or 'Is it much better or a bit better?'
items.hd: Verwende 'How much better...' oder 'Is it much better or a bit better?'
items.e: You can ask about the degree of difference with 'How much + comparative?' or give options: 'much better or a bit better?'
items.ed: Du kannst nach dem Grad des Unterschieds fragen mit 'How much + Komparativ?' oder Optionen geben: 'much better or a bit better?'

items.p: If she ___ (work) hard, she will get good marks.
a: works
items.h: In the if-clause of a first conditional, which tense do you use?
items.hd: Im if-Satz des First Conditional, welche Zeitform verwendet man?
items.e: First conditional: if + present simple, will + base form. 'She' is 3rd person, so: 'works'.
items.ed: First Conditional: if + Present Simple, will + Grundform. 'She' ist 3. Person, also: 'works'.

items.p: We ___ be late if we don't hurry up.
a: will
'll
items.h: The result clause uses 'will' + base form. The if-clause has present simple.
items.hd: Der Ergebnissatz verwendet 'will' + Grundform. Der if-Satz steht im Present Simple.
items.e: First conditional: if + present simple (don't hurry), will + base form (will be late). Note: the 'if' clause can come second.
items.ed: First Conditional: if + Present Simple (don't hurry), will + Grundform (will be late). Hinweis: Der if-Satz kann auch am Ende stehen.

items.p: If I ___ (have) enough money, I'll buy a new skateboard.
a: have
items.h: First conditional: if + present simple. Don't put 'will' in the if-clause!
items.hd: First Conditional: if + Present Simple. Kein 'will' im if-Satz!
items.e: 'If I have' (present simple) + 'I'll buy' (will + base form). A real possibility about the future.
items.ed: 'If I have' (Present Simple) + 'I'll buy' (will + Grundform). Eine echte Möglichkeit in der Zukunft.

items.p: If you ___ (not/eat) your vegetables, you won't get any dessert.
a: don't eat
do not eat
items.h: Negative in the if-clause: don't/doesn't + base form (present simple negative).
items.hd: Verneinung im if-Satz: don't/doesn't + Grundform (verneintes Present Simple).
items.e: Negative first conditional: if + don't/doesn't + base form, won't + base form. 'If you don't eat... you won't get...'
items.ed: Verneintes First Conditional: if + don't/doesn't + Grundform, won't + Grundform. 'If you don't eat... you won't get...'

items.p: If the weather ___ (be) nice tomorrow, we ___ (go) to the beach.
a: is ... will go
is ... 'll go
is...will go
is...'ll go
items.h: If-clause: present simple. Main clause: will + base form. Only ONE clause gets 'will'!
items.hd: If-Satz: Present Simple. Hauptsatz: will + Grundform. Nur EIN Satz bekommt 'will'!
items.e: 'If the weather is nice' (present simple) + 'we will go' (will + base). Never put 'will' in the if-clause.
items.ed: 'If the weather is nice' (Present Simple) + 'we will go' (will + Grundform). Nie 'will' im if-Satz verwenden.

items.p: I ___ (not/pass) the exam if I ___ (not/study) harder.
a: won't pass ... don't study
will not pass ... do not study
won't pass...don't study
will not pass...do not study
items.h: The main clause (result) gets 'won't'. The if-clause (condition) gets 'don't'. Both are negative here.
items.hd: Der Hauptsatz (Ergebnis) bekommt 'won't'. Der if-Satz (Bedingung) bekommt 'don't'. Beide sind hier verneint.
items.e: Both clauses negative: won't pass (main clause) + don't study (if-clause). The if-clause never gets 'will'.
items.ed: Beide Sätze verneint: won't pass (Hauptsatz) + don't study (if-Satz). Der if-Satz bekommt nie 'will'.

items.p: Which sentence correctly uses the first conditional?
items.c: If you touch that, you'll burn your hand.
a: If you touch that, you'll burn your hand.
ds: If you will touch that, you'll burn your hand.
If you touch that, you burn your hand.
If you touched that, you'll burn your hand.
items.h: First conditional: if + present simple, will + base form.
items.hd: First Conditional: if + Present Simple, will + Grundform.
items.e: 'If you touch' (present simple) + 'you'll burn' (will + base form). This is a real warning about the future.
items.ed: 'If you touch' (Present Simple) + 'you'll burn' (will + Grundform). Das ist eine echte Warnung für die Zukunft.

items.p: Choose the correct sentence about a school trip.
items.c: If it rains tomorrow, we won't go on the trip.
a: If it rains tomorrow, we won't go on the trip.
ds: If it will rain tomorrow, we won't go on the trip.
If it rained tomorrow, we won't go on the trip.
If it rains tomorrow, we don't go on the trip.
items.h: If-clause = present simple. Result clause = will/won't + base form.
items.hd: If-Satz = Present Simple. Ergebnissatz = will/won't + Grundform.
items.e: 'If it rains' (present simple) + 'we won't go' (won't + base). Real possibility about tomorrow.
items.ed: 'If it rains' (Present Simple) + 'we won't go' (won't + Grundform). Echte Möglichkeit für morgen.

items.p: Which sentence is NOT a first conditional?
items.c: If I had a million euros, I would buy a house.
a: If I had a million euros, I would buy a house.
ds: If you are hungry, I'll make you a sandwich.
We'll miss the bus if we don't leave now.
If she passes the test, her parents will be happy.
items.h: First conditional: if + present simple, will + base. One sentence uses past simple + would instead.
items.hd: First Conditional: if + Present Simple, will + Grundform. Ein Satz verwendet Past Simple + would stattdessen.
items.e: 'If I had... I would buy...' is a second conditional (hypothetical). The others all use first conditional (real possibility).
items.ed: 'If I had... I would buy...' ist ein Second Conditional (hypothetisch). Die anderen verwenden alle First Conditional (echte Möglichkeit).

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: If it will be sunny, we will go swimming.
a: is
If it is sunny
If it is sunny, we will go swimming.
If it is sunny, we will go swimming
items.h: Look at the if-clause. Should 'will' be there?
items.hd: Schau dir den if-Satz an. Sollte 'will' dort stehen?
items.e: Never use 'will' in the if-clause of a first conditional. Use present simple: 'If it IS sunny, we will go swimming.'
items.ed: Verwende nie 'will' im if-Satz eines First Conditional. Verwende Present Simple: 'If it IS sunny, we will go swimming.'

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: If you won't hurry, we'll be late for school.
a: don't hurry
do not hurry
If you don't hurry, we'll be late for school.
If you don't hurry, we'll be late for school
items.h: The negative form in the if-clause uses 'don't/doesn't', not 'won't'.
items.hd: Die Verneinung im if-Satz verwendet 'don't/doesn't', nicht 'won't'.
items.e: Negative in the if-clause: 'don't + base form', not 'won't'. 'If you don't hurry' is correct.
items.ed: Verneinung im if-Satz: 'don't + Grundform', nicht 'won't'. 'If you don't hurry' ist richtig.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: If she will study hard, she will passes the exam.
items.c: If she studies hard, she will pass the exam.
a: If she studies hard, she will pass the exam.
If she studies hard, she will pass the exam
items.h: There are TWO mistakes: 'will' in the if-clause, and 'passes' after 'will'. Fix both!
items.hd: Es gibt ZWEI Fehler: 'will' im if-Satz und 'passes' nach 'will'. Korrigiere beide!
items.e: Two errors: (1) Remove 'will' from if-clause → 'If she studies' (present simple). (2) After 'will', use base form → 'will pass' (not 'passes').
items.ed: Zwei Fehler: (1) Entferne 'will' aus dem if-Satz → 'If she studies' (Present Simple). (2) Nach 'will' steht die Grundform → 'will pass' (nicht 'passes').

items.p: Your friend is worried about the football match. Encourage them: 'If you ________ (practice) every day, you ________ (play) really well on Saturday.'
a: practice ... will play
practice ... 'll play
practice...will play
practice...'ll play
practise ... will play
practise...'ll play
items.h: If-clause: present simple. Result clause: will + base form.
items.hd: If-Satz: Present Simple. Ergebnissatz: will + Grundform.
items.e: 'If you practice' (present simple, the condition) + 'you will play' (will + base, the predicted result).
items.ed: 'If you practice' (Present Simple, die Bedingung) + 'you will play' (will + Grundform, das vorhergesagte Ergebnis).

items.p: You're warning your little sister about the consequences. Complete: 'If you ________ (not/finish) your homework, Mum ________ (not/let) you play outside.'
a: don't finish ... won't let
do not finish ... will not let
don't finish...won't let
do not finish...will not let
items.h: Both parts are negative. If-clause: don't + base form. Main clause: won't + base form.
items.hd: Beide Teile sind verneint. If-Satz: don't + Grundform. Hauptsatz: won't + Grundform.
items.e: Negative first conditional: 'If you don't finish' (present simple negative) + 'Mum won't let' (won't + base form).
items.ed: Verneintes First Conditional: 'If you don't finish' (verneintes Present Simple) + 'Mum won't let' (won't + Grundform).

items.p: 🇩🇪 Wenn du nicht aufpasst, wirst du hinfallen.
items.c: If you don't pay attention, you will fall.
a: If you don't pay attention, you will fall.
If you don't pay attention, you will fall
If you don't pay attention, you'll fall.
You will fall if you don't pay attention.
If you aren't careful, you will fall.
If you aren't careful, you'll fall.
items.h: 'Wenn du nicht aufpasst' = 'If you don't pay attention' (present simple). 'Wirst du hinfallen' = 'you will fall'.
items.hd: 'Wenn du nicht aufpasst' → if + present simple negative. 'Wirst du hinfallen' → will + Grundform.
items.e: German uses present or future in the wenn-clause, but English always uses present simple: 'If you don't..., you will...'
items.ed: Deutsch verwendet Präsens oder Futur im wenn-Satz, aber Englisch verwendet immer Present Simple: 'If you don't..., you will...'

items.p: 🇩🇪 Wenn wir den Bus verpassen, werden wir zu spät in die Schule kommen.
items.c: If we miss the bus, we will be late for school.
a: If we miss the bus, we will be late for school.
If we miss the bus, we will be late for school
If we miss the bus, we'll be late for school.
We will be late for school if we miss the bus.
We'll be late for school if we miss the bus.
items.h: 'Wenn wir verpassen' = 'If we miss' (present simple, not 'will miss'). 'Werden wir kommen' = 'we will be'.
items.hd: 'Wenn wir verpassen' = 'If we miss' (Present Simple, nicht 'will miss'). 'Werden wir kommen' = 'we will be'.
items.e: German 'Wenn wir verpassen' uses present, and English does too: 'If we miss'. The main clause uses 'will': 'we will be late'.
items.ed: Deutsch 'Wenn wir verpassen' verwendet Präsens, und Englisch auch: 'If we miss'. Der Hauptsatz verwendet 'will': 'we will be late'.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: if / rains / it / won't / we / go / the / to / park
items.c: If it rains, we won't go to the park.
a: If it rains, we won't go to the park.
If it rains, we won't go to the park
We won't go to the park if it rains.
We won't go to the park if it rains
items.h: 'If' + subject + present simple verb, then subject + won't + base form.
items.hd: 'If' + Subjekt + Present-Simple-Verb, dann Subjekt + won't + Grundform.
items.e: 'If it rains' (condition) + 'we won't go to the park' (negative result with 'won't').
items.ed: 'If it rains' (Bedingung) + 'we won't go to the park' (verneintes Ergebnis mit 'won't').

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: will / you / if / be / your / you / happy / parents / get / marks / good
items.c: Your parents will be happy if you get good marks.
a: Your parents will be happy if you get good marks.
Your parents will be happy if you get good marks
If you get good marks, your parents will be happy.
If you get good marks, your parents will be happy
items.h: The main clause can come first: 'Your parents will be happy' + 'if you get good marks'.
items.hd: Der Hauptsatz kann zuerst kommen: 'Your parents will be happy' + 'if you get good marks'.
items.e: Either order works: Result + if + condition, or If + condition, result. Both are correct first conditional.
items.ed: Beide Reihenfolgen funktionieren: Ergebnis + if + Bedingung, oder If + Bedingung, Ergebnis. Beides ist richtiges First Conditional.

items.p: Match each if-clause with the correct result. 1: If I don't feel well tomorrow, 2: If we save enough money, 3: If the bus is late, 4: If you practise every day, 5: If they don't invite me,
ds: b: I won't go to the party.
c: we'll go on holiday to Italy.
d: you'll get much better at the guitar.
items.h: Match the condition to the most logical result. All results use 'will/won't'.
items.hd: Ordne die Bedingung dem logischsten Ergebnis zu. Alle Ergebnisse verwenden 'will/won't'.
items.e: 1→e (sick → stay home), 2→c (save money → holiday), 3→a (bus late → walk), 4→d (practise → get better), 5→b (not invited → not go).
items.ed: 1→e (krank → zu Hause bleiben), 2→c (Geld sparen → Urlaub), 3→a (Bus spät → zu Fuß gehen), 4→d (üben → besser werden), 5→b (nicht eingeladen → nicht gehen).

items.p: Your mum is talking about the weekend plans. She says: 'We might go to the zoo.' Which reply uses the first conditional correctly?
items.c: If we go to the zoo, I'll take my camera.
a: If we go to the zoo, I'll take my camera.
ds: If we will go to the zoo, I'll take my camera.
If we went to the zoo, I'll take my camera.
If we go to the zoo, I take my camera.
items.h: First conditional for a real future possibility: if + present simple, will + base form.
items.hd: First Conditional für eine echte zukünftige Möglichkeit: if + Present Simple, will + Grundform.
items.e: The zoo trip is a real possibility. First conditional: 'If we go' (present simple) + 'I'll take' (will + base form).
items.ed: Der Zoobesuch ist eine echte Möglichkeit. First Conditional: 'If we go' (Present Simple) + 'I'll take' (will + Grundform).

items.p: We can't play outside ___ it stops raining.
a: unless
items.h: The sentence means: 'We can't play outside if it DOESN'T stop raining.' Which word replaces 'if...not'?
items.hd: Der Satz bedeutet: 'Wir können nicht draußen spielen, wenn es NICHT aufhört zu regnen.' Welches Wort ersetzt 'wenn...nicht'?
items.e: 'Unless it stops raining' = 'if it doesn't stop raining'. 'Unless' means 'except if' or 'if not'.
items.ed: 'Unless it stops raining' = 'wenn es nicht aufhört zu regnen'. 'Unless' bedeutet 'es sei denn' oder 'wenn nicht'.

items.p: She won't pass the test ___ she studies harder.
a: unless
items.h: She needs to study harder. Without it, she won't pass. 'Unless' = 'if...not'.
items.hd: Sie muss mehr lernen. Ohne das wird sie nicht bestehen. 'Unless' = 'wenn...nicht'.
items.e: 'Unless she studies harder' = 'if she doesn't study harder'. Both mean the same — no studying → no passing.
items.ed: 'Unless she studies harder' = 'wenn sie nicht mehr lernt'. Beides bedeutet dasselbe — kein Lernen → kein Bestehen.

items.p: I'll go to the cinema tomorrow ___ I have too much homework.
a: unless
items.h: You plan to go, EXCEPT IF homework stops you. Which word means 'except if'?
items.hd: Du planst hinzugehen, AUSSER WENN Hausaufgaben dich davon abhalten. Welches Wort bedeutet 'außer wenn'?
items.e: 'Unless I have too much homework' = 'if I don't have too much homework'. The cinema is the plan; homework is the exception.
items.ed: 'Unless I have too much homework' = 'wenn ich nicht zu viele Hausaufgaben habe'. Kino ist der Plan; Hausaufgaben sind die Ausnahme.

items.p: You will get sunburnt ___ you put on sunscreen.
a: unless
items.h: No sunscreen = sunburn. The only way to avoid it is putting on sunscreen. 'Unless' = 'if not'.
items.hd: Keine Sonnencreme = Sonnenbrand. Der einzige Weg es zu vermeiden ist Sonnencreme. 'Unless' = 'wenn nicht'.
items.e: 'Unless you put on sunscreen' = 'if you don't put on sunscreen'. Without the action, the negative result happens.
items.ed: 'Unless you put on sunscreen' = 'wenn du keine Sonnencreme aufträgst'. Ohne die Handlung tritt das negative Ergebnis ein.

items.p: The match will be cancelled ___ the rain stops before 3 o'clock.
a: unless
items.h: The match stays cancelled EXCEPT IF the rain stops. Which word = 'except if'?
items.hd: Das Spiel bleibt abgesagt, AUSSER WENN der Regen aufhört. Welches Wort = 'außer wenn'?
items.e: 'Unless the rain stops' = 'if the rain doesn't stop'. Cancellation is the default; rain stopping is the only exception.
items.ed: 'Unless the rain stops' = 'wenn der Regen nicht aufhört'. Absage ist der Standard; Regenende ist die einzige Ausnahme.

items.p: They won't let you into the pool ___ you wear a swimming cap.
a: unless
items.h: No swimming cap = no entry. 'Unless' introduces the one condition that changes the outcome.
items.hd: Keine Badekappe = kein Eintritt. 'Unless' führt die eine Bedingung ein, die das Ergebnis ändert.
items.e: 'Unless you wear a swimming cap' = 'if you don't wear one'. The rule is clear: cap required for entry.
items.ed: 'Unless you wear a swimming cap' = 'wenn du keine trägst'. Die Regel ist klar: Badekappe erforderlich für den Eintritt.

items.p: Which sentence means the same as 'If you don't clean your room, you can't go out'?
items.c: Unless you clean your room, you can't go out.
a: Unless you clean your room, you can't go out.
ds: If you clean your room, you can't go out.
Unless you don't clean your room, you can't go out.
While you clean your room, you can't go out.
items.h: 'Unless' already means 'if...not'. So 'unless you clean' = 'if you don't clean'.
items.hd: 'Unless' bedeutet bereits 'wenn...nicht'. Also 'unless you clean' = 'wenn du nicht aufräumst'.
items.e: 'Unless you clean your room' = 'if you don't clean your room'. Don't add 'not' after 'unless' — it's already negative!
items.ed: 'Unless you clean your room' = 'wenn du dein Zimmer nicht aufräumst'. Füge kein 'not' nach 'unless' hinzu — es ist schon verneint!

items.p: Choose the correct sentence.
items.c: Unless you hurry, we'll miss the train.
a: Unless you hurry, we'll miss the train.
ds: Unless you won't hurry, we'll miss the train.
Unless you will hurry, we'll miss the train.
Unless you don't hurry, we'll miss the train.
items.h: 'Unless' works like 'if' — use present simple after it. And 'unless' already means 'if...not', so don't add 'not'.
items.hd: 'Unless' funktioniert wie 'if' — verwende Present Simple danach. Und 'unless' bedeutet bereits 'wenn...nicht', also kein extra 'not'.
items.e: 'Unless' + present simple (hurry), no 'will' or 'not'. 'Unless you hurry' = 'if you don't hurry'.
items.ed: 'Unless' + Present Simple (hurry), kein 'will' oder 'not'. 'Unless you hurry' = 'wenn du dich nicht beeilst'.

items.p: Which pair of sentences has the same meaning?
items.c: 'I'll help you unless you tell me not to.' = 'I'll help you if you don't tell me not to.'
a: 'I'll help you unless you tell me not to.' = 'I'll help you if you don't tell me not to.'
ds: 'I'll help you unless you ask.' = 'I'll help you if you ask.'
'Unless you call me, I'll come.' = 'If you call me, I'll come.'
'I won't go unless you don't come.' = 'I won't go if you don't come.'
items.h: Remember: 'unless X' = 'if not X'. Check which pair correctly applies this rule.
items.hd: Merke: 'unless X' = 'if not X'. Prüfe, welches Paar diese Regel richtig anwendet.
items.e: 'Unless you tell me not to' = 'if you don't tell me not to'. This pair correctly converts unless → if...not.
items.ed: 'Unless you tell me not to' = 'wenn du mir nicht sagst, ich soll nicht'. Dieses Paar wandelt unless → if...not korrekt um.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: Unless you don't hurry, we'll miss the bus.
a: Unless you hurry
Unless you hurry, we'll miss the bus.
Unless you hurry, we'll miss the bus
items.h: 'Unless' already means 'if...not'. Adding 'don't' makes a double negative.
items.hd: 'Unless' bedeutet bereits 'wenn...nicht'. 'Don't' hinzufügen ergibt eine doppelte Verneinung.
items.e: 'Unless' = 'if...not'. So 'unless you don't hurry' = 'if you DO hurry' — which is the wrong meaning. Remove 'don't'.
items.ed: 'Unless' = 'wenn...nicht'. Also 'unless you don't hurry' = 'wenn du dich beeilst' — das ist die falsche Bedeutung. Entferne 'don't'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: Unless you will study, you won't pass the test.
a: study
Unless you study
Unless you study, you won't pass the test.
Unless you study, you won't pass the test
items.h: 'Unless' works like 'if'. What tense comes after 'if' in first conditional?
items.hd: 'Unless' funktioniert wie 'if'. Welche Zeitform kommt nach 'if' im First Conditional?
items.e: 'Unless' takes present simple, just like 'if': 'Unless you study' (not 'will study'). No 'will' after 'unless'.
items.ed: 'Unless' braucht Present Simple, genau wie 'if': 'Unless you study' (nicht 'will study'). Kein 'will' nach 'unless'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: I'll come to the party if not you tell me it's cancelled.
a: unless
unless you tell me
I'll come to the party unless you tell me it's cancelled.
I'll come to the party unless you tell me it's cancelled
items.h: English has a single word that means 'if not'. It's smoother than saying 'if not'.
items.hd: Englisch hat ein einzelnes Wort, das 'wenn nicht' bedeutet. Es klingt besser als 'if not'.
items.e: 'If not' should be replaced with 'unless': 'I'll come unless you tell me it's cancelled.' One word instead of two.
items.ed: 'If not' sollte durch 'unless' ersetzt werden: 'I'll come unless you tell me it's cancelled.' Ein Wort statt zwei.

items.p: Your friend asks if you're going to the school dance. Answer with 'unless': 'I'll go ________ I feel sick on Friday.'
a: unless
items.h: You're planning to go. The only thing that would stop you is feeling sick. 'Unless' = 'except if'.
items.hd: Du planst hinzugehen. Das Einzige, was dich aufhalten würde, ist Kranksein. 'Unless' = 'es sei denn'.
items.e: 'I'll go unless I feel sick' = 'I'll go if I don't feel sick'. The plan is to go; sickness is the only exception.
items.ed: 'I'll go unless I feel sick' = 'Ich gehe, wenn ich mich nicht krank fühle'. Der Plan ist zu gehen; Krankheit ist die einzige Ausnahme.

items.p: Rewrite using 'unless' so the meaning stays the same: 'If Tom doesn't apologise, I won't talk to him again.' → '________ Tom ________, I won't talk to him again.'
a: Unless Tom apologises
Unless Tom apologizes
items.h: 'If Tom doesn't apologise' = 'Unless Tom ________'. Remove the negative and use 'unless'.
items.hd: 'If Tom doesn't apologise' = 'Unless Tom ________'. Entferne die Verneinung und verwende 'unless'.
items.e: 'If...doesn't' becomes 'unless' + positive verb: 'Unless Tom apologises' = 'If Tom doesn't apologise'.
items.ed: 'If...doesn't' wird zu 'unless' + positives Verb: 'Unless Tom apologises' = 'Wenn Tom sich nicht entschuldigt'.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Wir werden zu spät kommen, es sei denn, wir laufen schneller.
items.c: We will be late unless we run faster.
a: We will be late unless we run faster.
We will be late unless we run faster
We'll be late unless we run faster.
We'll be late unless we walk faster.
Unless we run faster, we will be late.
Unless we run faster, we'll be late.
items.h: 'Es sei denn' = 'unless'. Remember: 'unless' + present simple in the condition clause.
items.hd: 'Es sei denn' = 'unless'. Denke daran: 'unless' + Present Simple im Bedingungssatz.
items.e: 'Es sei denn' = 'unless'. 'Unless we run faster' = present simple after 'unless'. 'We will be late' = result with 'will'.
items.ed: 'Es sei denn' = 'unless'. 'Unless we run faster' = Present Simple nach 'unless'. 'We will be late' = Ergebnis mit 'will'.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Du darfst nicht mit deinen Freunden spielen, wenn du deine Hausaufgaben nicht machst.
items.c: You can't play with your friends unless you do your homework.
a: You can't play with your friends unless you do your homework.
You can't play with your friends unless you do your homework
Unless you do your homework, you can't play with your friends.
You won't be allowed to play with your friends unless you do your homework.
items.h: 'Wenn du nicht machst' = 'unless you do'. Convert 'if you don't' to 'unless you...' (positive verb).
items.hd: 'Wenn du nicht machst' = 'unless you do'. Wandle 'wenn du nicht' in 'unless you...' (positives Verb) um.
items.e: 'Wenn du nicht machst' can be translated as 'if you don't do' OR 'unless you do' — both are correct.
items.ed: 'Wenn du nicht machst' kann übersetzt werden als 'if you don't do' ODER 'unless you do' — beides ist richtig.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: unless / you / won't / she / invite / apologise / her / you
items.c: She won't invite you unless you apologise.
a: She won't invite you unless you apologise.
She won't invite you unless you apologise
She won't invite you unless you apologize.
Unless you apologise, she won't invite you.
Unless you apologize, she won't invite you.
items.h: Result (she won't invite you) + unless + condition (you apologise). Or reverse the order.
items.hd: Ergebnis (she won't invite you) + unless + Bedingung (you apologise). Oder umgekehrt.
items.e: 'Unless you apologise' = the only condition that changes the outcome. Word order: result + unless + condition.
items.ed: 'Unless you apologise' = die einzige Bedingung, die das Ergebnis ändert. Wortstellung: Ergebnis + unless + Bedingung.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: the / unless / cancelled / be / match / will / stops / rain / the
items.c: The match will be cancelled unless the rain stops.
a: The match will be cancelled unless the rain stops.
The match will be cancelled unless the rain stops
Unless the rain stops, the match will be cancelled.
Unless the rain stops, the match will be cancelled
items.h: Start with the result ('The match will be cancelled') then add 'unless' + the condition.
items.hd: Beginne mit dem Ergebnis ('The match will be cancelled'), dann füge 'unless' + die Bedingung hinzu.
items.e: 'The match will be cancelled' (result) + 'unless the rain stops' (exception condition).
items.ed: 'The match will be cancelled' (Ergebnis) + 'unless the rain stops' (Ausnahmebedingung).

items.p: Match each 'unless' sentence with its 'if...not' equivalent. 1: Unless you study, you'll fail. 2: I won't go unless you come. 3: Unless it rains, we'll have a picnic. 4: She'll be angry unless you call her. 5: We won't win unless we try harder.
ds: a: If it doesn't rain, we'll have a picnic.
b: We won't win if we don't try harder.
c: If you don't study, you'll fail.
d: She'll be angry if you don't call her.
e: I won't go if you don't come.
items.h: 'Unless X' = 'If not X'. Just swap 'unless' for 'if...not' and the meaning stays the same.
items.hd: 'Unless X' = 'If not X'. Tausche einfach 'unless' gegen 'if...not' und die Bedeutung bleibt gleich.
items.e: Each 'unless + positive' matches 'if + negative': unless you study = if you don't study, etc.
items.ed: Jedes 'unless + positiv' passt zu 'if + negativ': unless you study = if you don't study, usw.

items.p: Your friend says: 'I'm not going to the party.' You want to know the condition. Ask using 'unless'. Start with: 'Will you go...' or 'Won't you go...'
items.c: Won't you go unless someone invites you?
a: Won't you go unless someone invites you?
Won't you go unless your friends go too?
Will you go unless it's boring?
Won't you go to the party unless someone asks you?
items.h: Use 'unless' to ask about the one condition that could change their mind.
items.hd: Verwende 'unless', um nach der einen Bedingung zu fragen, die ihre Meinung ändern könnte.
items.e: 'Won't you go unless...?' asks about the exception that would make them go. 'Unless' + present simple in the condition.
items.ed: 'Won't you go unless...?' fragt nach der Ausnahme, die sie doch gehen lassen würde. 'Unless' + Present Simple in der Bedingung.

items.p: I have a friend ___ speaks three languages.
a: who
that
items.h: The relative pronoun refers to 'a friend' (a person).
items.hd: Das Relativpronomen bezieht sich auf 'a friend' (eine Person).
items.e: We use 'who' or 'that' for people. 'A friend' is a person, so 'who' is correct.
items.ed: Wir verwenden 'who' oder 'that' fuer Personen. 'A friend' ist eine Person, also ist 'who' richtig.

items.p: She's reading a book ___ is about animals.
a: which
that
items.h: The relative pronoun refers to 'a book' (a thing).
items.hd: Das Relativpronomen bezieht sich auf 'a book' (eine Sache).
items.e: We use 'which' or 'that' for things. 'A book' is a thing, so 'which' is correct.
items.ed: Wir verwenden 'which' oder 'that' fuer Dinge. 'A book' ist eine Sache, also ist 'which' richtig.

items.p: The teacher ___ teaches us maths is very funny.
a: who
that
items.h: The pronoun replaces 'the teacher' and is the subject of the relative clause.
items.hd: Das Pronomen ersetzt 'the teacher' und ist das Subjekt des Relativsatzes.
items.e: 'Who' is used because the teacher is a person and is the subject of the relative clause (the teacher teaches).
items.ed: 'Who' wird verwendet, weil der Lehrer eine Person ist und das Subjekt des Relativsatzes ist.

items.p: Have you seen the film ___ won the award?
a: which
that
items.h: What does the pronoun refer to? Is 'the film' a person or a thing?
items.hd: Worauf bezieht sich das Pronomen? Ist 'the film' eine Person oder eine Sache?
items.e: 'Which' or 'that' is used for things. The film is a thing that won the award.
items.ed: 'Which' oder 'that' wird fuer Dinge verwendet. Der Film ist eine Sache, die den Preis gewonnen hat.

items.p: The dog ___ bit the postman belongs to our neighbour.
a: which
that
items.h: Is 'the dog' a person or an animal/thing?
items.hd: Ist 'the dog' eine Person oder ein Tier/eine Sache?
items.e: For animals we normally use 'which' or 'that', not 'who'. The dog is the subject of the relative clause.
items.ed: Fuer Tiere verwenden wir normalerweise 'which' oder 'that', nicht 'who'. Der Hund ist das Subjekt des Relativsatzes.

items.p: The girl ___ I met at the party is in my class.
a: who
that
items.h: The pronoun refers to 'the girl' (a person). Here it's the object of the relative clause.
items.hd: Das Pronomen bezieht sich auf 'the girl' (eine Person). Hier ist es das Objekt des Relativsatzes.
items.e: 'Who' or 'that' is used for people, even when the pronoun is the object of the relative clause ('I met the girl').
items.ed: 'Who' oder 'that' wird fuer Personen verwendet, auch wenn das Pronomen das Objekt des Relativsatzes ist.

items.p: Choose the correct sentence:
items.c: The man who lives next door is a doctor.
a: The man who lives next door is a doctor.
ds: The man which lives next door is a doctor.
The man where lives next door is a doctor.
The man what lives next door is a doctor.
items.h: Which relative pronoun do we use for people?
items.hd: Welches Relativpronomen verwenden wir fuer Personen?
items.e: 'Who' is the correct relative pronoun for people. 'Which' is for things, 'where' is for places.
items.ed: 'Who' ist das richtige Relativpronomen fuer Personen. 'Which' ist fuer Dinge, 'where' ist fuer Orte.

items.p: Which sentence uses the relative pronoun correctly?
items.c: The phone which I bought last week is broken.
a: The phone which I bought last week is broken.
ds: The phone who I bought last week is broken.
The phone where I bought last week is broken.
The phone whose I bought last week is broken.
items.h: 'The phone' is a thing, not a person. Which pronoun do we use for things?
items.hd: 'The phone' ist eine Sache, keine Person. Welches Pronomen verwenden wir fuer Dinge?
items.e: 'Which' (or 'that') is used for things. 'The phone' is a thing, so 'which' is correct.
items.ed: 'Which' (oder 'that') wird fuer Dinge verwendet. 'The phone' ist eine Sache, also ist 'which' richtig.

items.p: Choose the correct sentence about the picture of a boy holding a trophy:
items.c: That's the boy who won the competition.
a: That's the boy who won the competition.
ds: That's the boy which won the competition.
That's the boy who he won the competition.
That's the boy who won he the competition.
items.h: In a relative clause, the relative pronoun replaces the subject. Don't add an extra pronoun.
items.hd: Im Relativsatz ersetzt das Relativpronomen das Subjekt. Fuege kein zusaetzliches Pronomen hinzu.
items.e: 'Who' replaces 'the boy' as subject. We don't need 'he' again — that would be a double subject.
items.ed: 'Who' ersetzt 'the boy' als Subjekt. Wir brauchen 'he' nicht nochmal — das waere ein doppeltes Subjekt.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: The woman which helped me was very nice.
a: who
The woman who helped me was very nice.
The woman who helped me was very nice
items.h: Is 'the woman' a person or a thing? Which relative pronoun is correct?
items.hd: Ist 'the woman' eine Person oder eine Sache? Welches Relativpronomen ist richtig?
items.e: 'Which' is for things, but 'the woman' is a person. We must use 'who' (or 'that').
items.ed: 'Which' ist fuer Dinge, aber 'the woman' ist eine Person. Wir muessen 'who' (oder 'that') verwenden.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: I know a girl who she plays the guitar very well.
a: who plays
I know a girl who plays the guitar very well.
I know a girl who plays the guitar very well
items.h: Does 'who' replace 'she'? Do we need both?
items.hd: Ersetzt 'who' das Wort 'she'? Brauchen wir beides?
items.e: 'Who' already acts as the subject. Adding 'she' creates a double subject. Remove 'she'.
items.ed: 'Who' ist bereits das Subjekt. 'She' hinzuzufuegen erzeugt ein doppeltes Subjekt. Entferne 'she'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: The computer who I use at school is very old.
a: which
that
The computer which I use at school is very old.
The computer that I use at school is very old.
items.h: Is 'the computer' a person or a thing?
items.hd: Ist 'the computer' eine Person oder eine Sache?
items.e: 'Who' is for people. 'The computer' is a thing, so we use 'which' or 'that'.
items.ed: 'Who' ist fuer Personen. 'The computer' ist eine Sache, also verwenden wir 'which' oder 'that'.

items.p: You are describing your best friend to a new classmate. Combine: 'That's the girl. She sits next to me.' Use a relative pronoun.
items.c: That's the girl who sits next to me.
a: That's the girl who sits next to me.
That's the girl who sits next to me
That's the girl that sits next to me.
That's the girl that sits next to me
items.h: Replace 'She' with a relative pronoun for people and join the sentences.
items.hd: Ersetze 'She' durch ein Relativpronomen fuer Personen und verbinde die Saetze.
items.e: 'She' becomes 'who' because it refers to 'the girl' (a person). The relative clause comes directly after the noun.
items.ed: 'She' wird zu 'who', weil es sich auf 'the girl' (eine Person) bezieht. Der Relativsatz steht direkt nach dem Nomen.

items.p: You are writing about your favourite gadget. Combine: 'I have a tablet. It has a huge screen.' Use a relative pronoun.
items.c: I have a tablet which has a huge screen.
a: I have a tablet which has a huge screen.
I have a tablet which has a huge screen
I have a tablet that has a huge screen.
I have a tablet that has a huge screen
items.h: Replace 'It' with a relative pronoun for things.
items.hd: Ersetze 'It' durch ein Relativpronomen fuer Dinge.
items.e: 'It' becomes 'which' (or 'that') because it refers to 'a tablet' (a thing).
items.ed: 'It' wird zu 'which' (oder 'that'), weil es sich auf 'a tablet' (eine Sache) bezieht.

items.p: Translate: Das ist der Junge, der neben mir wohnt.
items.c: That's the boy who lives next to me.
a: That's the boy who lives next to me.
That's the boy who lives next to me
That's the boy that lives next to me.
That's the boy that lives next to me
That is the boy who lives next to me.
That is the boy that lives next to me.
items.h: German 'der' here is a relative pronoun for a male person. What's the English equivalent?
items.hd: 'Der' ist hier ein Relativpronomen fuer eine maennliche Person.
items.e: German 'der' (relative pronoun) = 'who' or 'that' in English when referring to people.
items.ed: Deutsch 'der' (Relativpronomen) = 'who' oder 'that' auf Englisch, wenn es sich auf Personen bezieht.

items.p: Translate: Ich habe ein Handy, das eine tolle Kamera hat.
items.c: I have a phone which has a great camera.
a: I have a phone which has a great camera.
I have a phone which has a great camera
I have a phone that has a great camera.
I have a phone that has a great camera
I've got a phone which has a great camera.
I've got a phone that has a great camera.
items.h: German 'das' here is a relative pronoun for a thing. What's the English equivalent?
items.hd: 'Das' ist hier ein Relativpronomen fuer eine Sache.
items.e: German 'das' (relative pronoun for neuter nouns) = 'which' or 'that' in English for things.
items.ed: Deutsch 'das' (Relativpronomen fuer sachliche Nomen) = 'which' oder 'that' auf Englisch fuer Dinge.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: is / who / the / singer / that / sings / my / favourite / song
items.c: That is the singer who sings my favourite song.
a: That is the singer who sings my favourite song.
That is the singer who sings my favourite song
That's the singer who sings my favourite song.
That's the singer who sings my favourite song
items.h: Start with 'That is', then the noun, then the relative clause with 'who'.
items.hd: Beginne mit 'That is', dann das Nomen, dann der Relativsatz mit 'who'.
items.e: The relative clause 'who sings my favourite song' comes directly after the noun 'the singer'.
items.ed: Der Relativsatz 'who sings my favourite song' steht direkt nach dem Nomen 'the singer'.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: which / the / I / pizza / ordered / was / delicious
items.c: The pizza which I ordered was delicious.
a: The pizza which I ordered was delicious.
The pizza which I ordered was delicious
The pizza that I ordered was delicious.
The pizza that I ordered was delicious
items.h: The relative clause 'which I ordered' describes 'the pizza' and comes right after it.
items.hd: Der Relativsatz 'which I ordered' beschreibt 'the pizza' und steht direkt danach.
items.e: The relative clause sits between the subject ('the pizza') and the main verb ('was').
items.ed: Der Relativsatz steht zwischen dem Subjekt ('the pizza') und dem Hauptverb ('was').

items.p: Match each sentence beginning with the correct relative clause. 1: The boy 2: The cake 3: The city 4: The girl 5: The bike
ds: a: which we visited last summer was beautiful.
b: which my dad gave me is red.
c: who sits behind me is very tall.
d: who won the race was so happy.
e: which my mum made was amazing.
items.h: People need 'who', things need 'which'. Match the noun to the correct pronoun and meaning.
items.hd: Personen brauchen 'who', Dinge brauchen 'which'. Ordne das Nomen dem richtigen Pronomen und der Bedeutung zu.
items.e: Boy/girl = who (people), cake/city/bike = which (things). Each ending must make sense with the noun.
items.ed: Boy/girl = who (Personen), cake/city/bike = which (Dinge). Jede Endung muss zum Nomen passen.

items.p: You want to describe the sandwich your friend made. Which sentence is correct?
items.c: The sandwich which my friend made was really tasty.
a: The sandwich which my friend made was really tasty.
ds: The sandwich who my friend made was really tasty.
The sandwich where my friend made was really tasty.
The sandwich whose my friend made was really tasty.
items.h: A sandwich is a thing. Which relative pronoun do we use for things?
items.hd: Ein Sandwich ist eine Sache. Welches Relativpronomen verwenden wir fuer Dinge?
items.e: 'Which' is correct because 'sandwich' is a thing. 'Who' is only for people.
items.ed: 'Which' ist richtig, weil 'sandwich' eine Sache ist. 'Who' ist nur fuer Personen.

items.p: I have lived here ___ ten years.
a: for
items.h: 'Ten years' is a duration (how long). Which word goes with a duration?
items.hd: 'Ten years' ist eine Dauer (wie lange). Welches Wort passt zu einer Dauer?
items.e: 'For' is used with a period of time (duration): for ten years, for two hours, for a long time.
items.ed: 'For' wird mit einer Zeitspanne (Dauer) verwendet: for ten years, for two hours, for a long time.

items.p: She has known him ___ 2019.
a: since
items.h: '2019' is a specific point in time. Which word goes with a point in time?
items.hd: '2019' ist ein bestimmter Zeitpunkt. Welches Wort passt zu einem Zeitpunkt?
items.e: 'Since' is used with a point in time: since 2019, since Monday, since I was a child.
items.ed: 'Since' wird mit einem Zeitpunkt verwendet: since 2019, since Monday, since I was a child.

items.p: We have been friends ___ primary school.
a: since
items.h: 'Primary school' is when the friendship started — a starting point.
items.hd: 'Primary school' ist der Anfang der Freundschaft — ein Anfangspunkt.
items.e: 'Since primary school' means 'from primary school until now'. 'Since' marks the starting point.
items.ed: 'Since primary school' bedeutet 'seit der Volksschule bis jetzt'. 'Since' markiert den Anfangspunkt.

items.p: Tom has played the guitar ___ three months.
a: for
items.h: 'Three months' tells you how long. Duration or point in time?
items.hd: 'Three months' sagt dir wie lange. Dauer oder Zeitpunkt?
items.e: 'For three months' = a duration. German 'seit drei Monaten' = English 'for three months' (not 'since').
items.ed: 'For three months' = eine Dauer. Deutsch 'seit drei Monaten' = Englisch 'for three months' (nicht 'since').

items.p: They haven't seen each other ___ last Christmas.
a: since
items.h: 'Last Christmas' is a specific point in time when they last met.
items.hd: 'Last Christmas' ist ein bestimmter Zeitpunkt, als sie sich zuletzt getroffen haben.
items.e: 'Since last Christmas' = from that point until now. 'Last Christmas' is a specific time marker.
items.ed: 'Since last Christmas' = von diesem Zeitpunkt bis jetzt. 'Last Christmas' ist ein bestimmter Zeitpunkt.

items.p: My grandma has had that car ___ I was born.
a: since
items.h: 'I was born' describes a point in time (the moment you were born).
items.hd: 'I was born' beschreibt einen Zeitpunkt (den Moment deiner Geburt).
items.e: 'Since' can be followed by a clause: 'since I was born' = from that moment until now.
items.ed: 'Since' kann von einem Satz gefolgt werden: 'since I was born' = von diesem Moment bis jetzt.

items.p: Choose the correct sentence:
items.c: I have lived in Vienna since 2020.
a: I have lived in Vienna since 2020.
ds: I have lived in Vienna for 2020.
I have lived in Vienna since five years.
I have lived in Vienna for last summer.
items.h: Is the time expression a duration (how long) or a point in time (when)?
items.hd: Ist der Zeitausdruck eine Dauer (wie lange) oder ein Zeitpunkt (wann)?
items.e: '2020' is a point in time, so we use 'since'. 'Five years' is a duration, so it needs 'for'. German 'seit' = both 'for' and 'since'!
items.ed: '2020' ist ein Zeitpunkt, also verwenden wir 'since'. 'Five years' ist eine Dauer, also braucht es 'for'. Deutsch 'seit' = sowohl 'for' als auch 'since'!

items.p: Which sentence uses 'for' correctly?
items.c: She has studied English for six years.
a: She has studied English for six years.
ds: She has studied English for last Monday.
She has studied English for she was ten.
items.h: 'For' always needs a duration: for two weeks, for a year, for ages.
items.hd: 'For' braucht immer eine Dauer: for two weeks, for a year, for ages.
items.e: 'For six years' is correct because 'six years' is a duration. Years, months, hours, days are durations.
items.ed: 'For six years' ist richtig, weil 'six years' eine Dauer ist. Jahre, Monate, Stunden, Tage sind Zeitspannen.

items.p: Which sentence is correct?
items.c: He has been ill since last Friday.
a: He has been ill since last Friday.
ds: He has been ill for last Friday.
He is ill since last Friday.
He has been ill since a week.
items.h: 'Last Friday' is a specific day. And 'since' always needs present perfect, not present simple.
items.hd: 'Last Friday' ist ein bestimmter Tag. 'Since' braucht immer das Present Perfect, nicht das Present Simple.
items.e: 'Since last Friday' needs present perfect ('has been'), not present simple ('is'). German uses present tense with 'seit', but English uses present perfect.
items.ed: 'Since last Friday' braucht das Present Perfect ('has been'), nicht das Praesens ('is'). Im Deutschen verwendet man Praesens mit 'seit', aber im Englischen Present Perfect.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: I have known her since three years.
a: for three years
I have known her for three years.
I have known her for three years
items.h: 'Three years' is a duration (how long), not a point in time.
items.hd: 'Three years' ist eine Dauer (wie lange), kein Zeitpunkt.
items.e: 'Three years' is a duration, so it needs 'for', not 'since'. German 'seit drei Jahren' tricks students into using 'since'.
items.ed: 'Three years' ist eine Dauer, also braucht es 'for', nicht 'since'. Deutsch 'seit drei Jahren' verfuehrt dazu, 'since' zu verwenden.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She is in hospital since Monday.
a: has been in hospital
She has been in hospital since Monday.
She has been in hospital since Monday
items.h: 'Since Monday' tells you the situation started in the past and continues now. Which tense do you need?
items.hd: 'Since Monday' zeigt, dass die Situation in der Vergangenheit begann und bis jetzt andauert. Welche Zeitform brauchst du?
items.e: With 'since', we need present perfect, not present simple. German uses 'ist ... seit', but English needs 'has been ... since'.
items.ed: Mit 'since' brauchen wir das Present Perfect, nicht das Praesens. Deutsch verwendet 'ist ... seit', aber Englisch braucht 'has been ... since'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: We have waited for 8 o'clock.
a: since 8 o'clock
We have waited since 8 o'clock.
We have waited since 8 o'clock
items.h: Is '8 o'clock' a duration or a point in time?
items.hd: Ist '8 o'clock' eine Dauer oder ein Zeitpunkt?
items.e: '8 o'clock' is a specific point in time, so it needs 'since', not 'for'.
items.ed: '8 o'clock' ist ein bestimmter Zeitpunkt, also braucht es 'since', nicht 'for'.

items.p: Your friend asks how long you've had your pet. Answer using 'for': 'I ________ (have) my cat ________ five years.'
a: have had ... for
have had...for
've had ... for
've had...for
items.h: 'Five years' is a duration. Which word do you use with durations?
items.hd: 'Five years' ist eine Dauer. Welches Wort verwendest du bei Zeitspannen?
items.e: Present perfect ('have had') because the action started in the past and continues. 'For' because 'five years' is a duration.
items.ed: Present Perfect ('have had'), weil die Handlung in der Vergangenheit begann und bis jetzt dauert. 'For', weil 'five years' eine Dauer ist.

items.p: You are telling your pen pal about your home. Rewrite with 'since': 'We moved here in 2018. We still live here.' → 'We ________ here ________ 2018.'
a: have lived ... since
have lived...since
've lived ... since
've lived...since
items.h: '2018' is a specific year — a point in time.
items.hd: '2018' ist ein bestimmtes Jahr — ein Zeitpunkt.
items.e: Two sentences (past action + still true now) combine into present perfect with 'since' + point in time.
items.ed: Zwei Saetze (vergangene Handlung + immer noch wahr) werden mit Present Perfect und 'since' + Zeitpunkt verbunden.

items.p: Translate: Ich lerne seit zwei Jahren Englisch.
items.c: I have been learning English for two years.
a: I have been learning English for two years.
I have been learning English for two years
I have learned English for two years.
I have learned English for two years
I have learnt English for two years.
I've been learning English for two years.
items.h: German 'seit zwei Jahren' = 'for two years' in English (duration!). And the German present tense becomes English present perfect.
items.hd: 'Seit zwei Jahren' ist eine Dauer, also 'for two years'. Im Englischen braucht man Present Perfect.
items.e: German 'seit' + duration = English 'for'. German present tense + 'seit' = English present perfect. 'Seit zwei Jahren' = 'for two years'.
items.ed: Deutsch 'seit' + Dauer = Englisch 'for'. Deutsches Praesens + 'seit' = englisches Present Perfect.

items.p: Translate: Er wohnt seit September in Wien.
items.c: He has lived in Vienna since September.
a: He has lived in Vienna since September.
He has lived in Vienna since September
He's lived in Vienna since September.
He has been living in Vienna since September.
items.h: German 'seit September' — is September a duration or a point in time? And what tense does English need?
items.hd: 'September' ist ein Zeitpunkt, kein Zeitraum. Welche Zeitform braucht Englisch?
items.e: 'September' is a point in time, so 'since'. German present tense + 'seit' = English present perfect + 'since'.
items.ed: 'September' ist ein Zeitpunkt, also 'since'. Deutsches Praesens + 'seit' = englisches Present Perfect + 'since'.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: for / played / has / she / tennis / years / three
items.c: She has played tennis for three years.
a: She has played tennis for three years.
She has played tennis for three years
items.h: Subject + has/have + past participle + object + for + duration.
items.hd: Subjekt + has/have + Past Participle + Objekt + for + Dauer.
items.e: Present perfect structure: She + has + played + tennis + for three years.
items.ed: Present-Perfect-Struktur: She + has + played + tennis + for three years.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: haven't / since / they / each other / seen / July
items.c: They haven't seen each other since July.
a: They haven't seen each other since July.
They haven't seen each other since July
items.h: Subject + haven't/hasn't + past participle + object + since + point in time.
items.hd: Subjekt + haven't/hasn't + Past Participle + Objekt + since + Zeitpunkt.
items.e: Negative present perfect: They + haven't + seen + each other + since July.
items.ed: Verneintes Present Perfect: They + haven't + seen + each other + since July.

items.p: Match each time expression with 'for' or 'since'. 1: ___ two hours 2: ___ last summer 3: ___ a long time 4: ___ 2015 5: ___ Monday
items.h: Duration (how long) = for. Point in time (when) = since.
items.hd: Dauer (wie lange) = for. Zeitpunkt (wann) = since.
items.e: For: two hours (duration), a long time (duration). Since: last summer, 2015, Monday (all points in time).
items.ed: For: two hours (Dauer), a long time (Dauer). Since: last summer, 2015, Monday (alle Zeitpunkte).

items.p: Your friend started learning Spanish in September. It's now March. Which sentence is correct?
items.c: She has been learning Spanish since September.
a: She has been learning Spanish since September.
ds: She has been learning Spanish for September.
She has been learning Spanish since six months.
items.h: September is a specific month (point in time). Which word: 'for' or 'since'? And which tense?
items.hd: September ist ein bestimmter Monat (Zeitpunkt). Welches Wort: 'for' oder 'since'? Und welche Zeitform?
items.e: 'September' is a point in time → 'since'. The action started in the past and continues → present perfect.
items.ed: 'September' ist ein Zeitpunkt → 'since'. Die Handlung begann in der Vergangenheit und dauert an → Present Perfect.

items.p: I ___ (visit) London last summer.
a: visited
items.h: 'Last summer' tells you exactly when it happened. Which tense do we use for finished past times?
items.hd: 'Last summer' sagt dir genau, wann es passiert ist. Welche Zeitform verwenden wir fuer abgeschlossene Zeitpunkte?
items.e: 'Last summer' is a specific past time, so we use past simple. Present perfect cannot be used with specific past times.
items.ed: 'Last summer' ist ein bestimmter vergangener Zeitpunkt, also verwenden wir Past Simple. Present Perfect kann nicht mit bestimmten Vergangenheitsangaben verwendet werden.

items.p: Have you ever ___ (be) to Italy?
a: been
items.h: 'Have you ever...?' is a present perfect question. What is the past participle of 'be'?
items.hd: 'Have you ever...?' ist eine Present-Perfect-Frage. Was ist das Past Participle von 'be'?
items.e: 'Have you ever been to...?' is a fixed present perfect pattern. The past participle of 'be' is 'been'.
items.ed: 'Have you ever been to...?' ist ein festes Present-Perfect-Muster. Das Past Participle von 'be' ist 'been'.

items.p: She ___ (just / finish) her homework.
a: has just finished
's just finished
items.h: 'Just' means it happened a very short time ago. Which tense shows recent events?
items.hd: 'Just' bedeutet, es ist gerade eben passiert. Welche Zeitform zeigt kuerzlich geschehene Ereignisse?
items.e: 'Just' = very recently → present perfect. Word order: has + just + past participle.
items.ed: 'Just' = gerade eben → Present Perfect. Wortstellung: has + just + Past Participle.

items.p: We ___ (not / see) that film yet.
a: haven't seen
have not seen
items.h: 'Yet' at the end of a negative sentence means 'up to now'. Which tense is that?
items.hd: 'Yet' am Ende eines verneinten Satzes bedeutet 'bis jetzt'. Welche Zeitform ist das?
items.e: 'Yet' in negative sentences = present perfect. 'We' takes 'have' (not 'has').
items.ed: 'Yet' in verneinten Saetzen = Present Perfect. 'We' braucht 'have' (nicht 'has').

items.p: He ___ (break) his arm two weeks ago.
a: broke
items.h: 'Two weeks ago' is a specific past time. Which tense goes with 'ago'?
items.hd: 'Two weeks ago' ist eine bestimmte Vergangenheitsangabe. Welche Zeitform passt zu 'ago'?
items.e: 'Ago' always needs past simple, never present perfect. 'Break' is irregular: break → broke → broken.
items.ed: 'Ago' braucht immer Past Simple, nie Present Perfect. 'Break' ist unregelmaessig: break → broke → broken.

items.p: I ___ (never / eat) sushi. Is it good?
a: have never eaten
've never eaten
items.h: 'Never' talks about your whole life experience up to now. Which tense for life experiences?
items.hd: 'Never' bezieht sich auf deine gesamte Lebenserfahrung bis jetzt. Welche Zeitform fuer Lebenserfahrungen?
items.e: 'Never' = at no time in my life until now → present perfect. Word order: have + never + past participle.
items.ed: 'Never' = zu keinem Zeitpunkt in meinem Leben bis jetzt → Present Perfect. Wortstellung: have + never + Past Participle.

items.p: Choose the correct sentence:
items.c: I went to the cinema yesterday.
a: I went to the cinema yesterday.
ds: I have gone to the cinema yesterday.
I have went to the cinema yesterday.
I was go to the cinema yesterday.
items.h: 'Yesterday' is a finished past time. Which tense do we always use with 'yesterday'?
items.hd: 'Yesterday' ist ein abgeschlossener Zeitpunkt. Welche Zeitform verwenden wir immer mit 'yesterday'?
items.e: 'Yesterday' = past simple. We can never use present perfect with 'yesterday', 'last week', or 'ago'.
items.ed: 'Yesterday' = Past Simple. Present Perfect kann nie mit 'yesterday', 'last week' oder 'ago' verwendet werden.

items.p: Your friend asks about your life experiences. Choose the correct question:
items.c: Have you ever ridden a horse?
a: Have you ever ridden a horse?
ds: Did you ever ride a horse?
Have you ever rode a horse?
Did you ever ridden a horse?
items.h: 'Ever' asks about life experience (any time in your life). Which tense for life experiences?
items.hd: 'Ever' fragt nach Lebenserfahrung (irgendwann im Leben). Welche Zeitform fuer Lebenserfahrungen?
items.e: 'Ever' for life experience → present perfect. 'Ride' is irregular: ride → rode → ridden.
items.ed: 'Ever' fuer Lebenserfahrung → Present Perfect. 'Ride' ist unregelmaessig: ride → rode → ridden.

items.p: Choose the correct pair of sentences: A) 'I have lost my keys. I can't find them.' / B) 'I lost my keys yesterday. I found them under the sofa.'
items.c: Both A and B are correct.
a: Both A and B are correct.
ds: Neither A nor B is correct.
items.h: Think about the result: In A, are the keys still lost? In B, were they found?
items.hd: Denke an das Ergebnis: Sind die Schluessel in A noch verloren? Wurden sie in B gefunden?
items.e: A: Present perfect — the keys are still lost (present result). B: Past simple — the story is finished (found them yesterday).
items.ed: A: Present Perfect — die Schluessel sind noch verloren (gegenwaertiges Ergebnis). B: Past Simple — die Geschichte ist abgeschlossen (gestern gefunden).

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: I have seen that film last week.
a: saw
I saw that film last week.
I saw that film last week
items.h: 'Last week' is a specific past time. Can we use present perfect with 'last week'?
items.hd: 'Last week' ist ein bestimmter Zeitpunkt in der Vergangenheit. Kann man Present Perfect mit 'last week' verwenden?
items.e: 'Last week' requires past simple. Present perfect cannot be used with specific finished time markers like 'last week', 'yesterday', or 'ago'.
items.ed: 'Last week' erfordert Past Simple. Present Perfect kann nicht mit abgeschlossenen Zeitangaben wie 'last week', 'yesterday' oder 'ago' verwendet werden.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: Did you ever try Indian food?
a: Have you ever tried
Have you ever tried Indian food?
Have you ever tried Indian food
items.h: 'Ever' asks about life experience up to now. Which tense is that?
items.hd: 'Ever' fragt nach Lebenserfahrung bis jetzt. Welche Zeitform ist das?
items.e: 'Ever' with general life experience uses present perfect: 'Have you ever tried...?' not 'Did you ever try...?'
items.ed: 'Ever' bei allgemeiner Lebenserfahrung verwendet Present Perfect: 'Have you ever tried...?' nicht 'Did you ever try...?'

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She has gone to Paris in 2022.
a: went
She went to Paris in 2022.
She went to Paris in 2022
items.h: 'In 2022' is a specific time in the past. Which tense do we need?
items.hd: 'In 2022' ist ein bestimmter Zeitpunkt in der Vergangenheit. Welche Zeitform brauchen wir?
items.e: 'In 2022' is a specific past time marker that requires past simple. We cannot use present perfect with specific dates.
items.ed: 'In 2022' ist eine bestimmte Vergangenheitsangabe, die Past Simple erfordert. Present Perfect kann nicht mit bestimmten Daten verwendet werden.

items.p: Your friend is asking about your weekend. Complete with past simple or present perfect: 'What ___ you ___ (do) last Saturday?' — 'I ___ (go) shopping with my mum.'
a: did ... do ... went
did...do...went
items.h: 'Last Saturday' is a specific past time. Which tense goes with specific past times?
items.hd: 'Last Saturday' ist ein bestimmter Zeitpunkt. Welche Zeitform passt zu bestimmten Vergangenheitsangaben?
items.e: 'Last Saturday' = specific past time → past simple for both question and answer.
items.ed: 'Last Saturday' = bestimmter Zeitpunkt → Past Simple fuer Frage und Antwort.

items.p: You lost your pen. You still can't find it. Write one sentence using present perfect to explain: 'I ________ (lose) my pen.'
a: have lost
've lost
items.h: The pen is still lost (present result). Which tense shows a past action with a present result?
items.hd: Der Stift ist immer noch verloren (gegenwaertiges Ergebnis). Welche Zeitform zeigt eine vergangene Handlung mit einem gegenwaertigen Ergebnis?
items.e: Present perfect for a past action with a present result: I have lost my pen (and I still don't have it).
items.ed: Present Perfect fuer eine vergangene Handlung mit gegenwaertigem Ergebnis: I have lost my pen (und ich habe ihn immer noch nicht).

items.p: Translate: Warst du schon einmal in England?
items.c: Have you ever been to England?
a: Have you ever been to England?
Have you ever been to England
items.h: German 'schon einmal' = English 'ever'. Life experience = which tense?
items.hd: 'Schon einmal' = 'ever'. Lebenserfahrung braucht welche Zeitform?
items.e: German 'Warst du schon einmal' uses past tense, but English uses present perfect: 'Have you ever been to...?'
items.ed: Deutsch verwendet die Vergangenheit, aber Englisch braucht Present Perfect: 'Have you ever been to...?'

items.p: Translate: Ich habe gerade mein Zimmer aufgeraeumt.
items.c: I have just tidied my room.

I have just tidied my room
I've just tidied my room.
I've just tidied my room
I have just cleaned my room.
I've just cleaned my room.
items.h: 'Gerade' = 'just' (very recently). Which tense for recent events?
items.hd: 'Gerade' = 'just'. Welche Zeitform fuer kuerzlich Geschehenes?
items.e: 'Gerade' = 'just' → present perfect. German Perfekt ('habe aufgeraeumt') = English present perfect ('have tidied').
items.ed: 'Gerade' = 'just' → Present Perfect. Das deutsche Perfekt entspricht hier dem englischen Present Perfect.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: never / has / she / pizza / eaten / Hawaiian
items.c: She has never eaten Hawaiian pizza.
a: She has never eaten Hawaiian pizza.
She has never eaten Hawaiian pizza
items.h: Present perfect word order: Subject + has/have + never + past participle + object.
items.hd: Present-Perfect-Wortstellung: Subjekt + has/have + never + Past Participle + Objekt.
items.e: With 'never': She + has + never + eaten + Hawaiian pizza. 'Never' goes between 'has' and the past participle.
items.ed: Mit 'never': She + has + never + eaten + Hawaiian pizza. 'Never' steht zwischen 'has' und dem Past Participle.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: already / the / I / finished / have / test
items.c: I have already finished the test.
a: I have already finished the test.
I have already finished the test
items.h: 'Already' goes between 'have' and the past participle.
items.hd: 'Already' steht zwischen 'have' und dem Past Participle.
items.e: Word order with 'already': I + have + already + finished + the test.
items.ed: Wortstellung mit 'already': I + have + already + finished + the test.

items.p: Sort each time word into 'Past Simple' or 'Present Perfect'. 1: yesterday 2: ever 3: last Monday 4: just 5: two days ago 6: never
items.h: PS signal words: yesterday, last..., ...ago. PP signal words: ever, never, just, already, yet.
items.hd: PS-Signalwoerter: yesterday, last..., ...ago. PP-Signalwoerter: ever, never, just, already, yet.
items.e: Past simple: yesterday, last Monday, two days ago (specific finished times). Present perfect: ever, just, never (no specific time, up to now).
items.ed: Past Simple: yesterday, last Monday, two days ago (bestimmte abgeschlossene Zeitpunkte). Present Perfect: ever, just, never (kein bestimmter Zeitpunkt, bis jetzt).

items.p: You want to know if your friend has ever tried surfing (any time in their life). Which question do you ask?
a: Have you ever tried surfing?
items.h: You're asking about their life experience (any time, ever). Which tense for general experience?
items.hd: Du fragst nach Lebenserfahrung (irgendwann, jemals). Welche Zeitform fuer allgemeine Erfahrungen?
items.e: 'Ever' for life experience uses present perfect: 'Have you ever + past participle?'
items.ed: 'Ever' fuer Lebenserfahrung verwendet Present Perfect: 'Have you ever + Past Participle?'

items.p: She ___ allowed to stay up until 10 pm.
a: is
's
items.h: 'She' is 3rd person singular. Which form of 'be' matches?
items.hd: 'She' ist 3. Person Einzahl. Welche Form von 'be' passt?
items.e: 'She' + 'is' + 'allowed to'. The structure is: subject + be + allowed to + verb.
items.ed: 'She' + 'is' + 'allowed to'. Die Struktur ist: Subjekt + be + allowed to + Verb.

items.p: ___ you allowed to eat in the library?
a: Are
items.h: Questions with 'be allowed to' start with a form of 'be'. Which form for 'you'?
items.hd: Fragen mit 'be allowed to' beginnen mit einer Form von 'be'. Welche Form fuer 'you'?
items.e: Questions: Be + subject + allowed to + verb? → 'Are you allowed to eat...?'
items.ed: Fragen: Be + Subjekt + allowed to + Verb? → 'Are you allowed to eat...?'

items.p: At our school, students ___ allowed to wear hats in the classroom.
a: aren't
are not
items.h: 'Students' is plural. Which negative form of 'be' matches?
items.hd: 'Students' ist Mehrzahl. Welche verneinte Form von 'be' passt?
items.e: 'Students' (plural) + 'aren't' + 'allowed to'. This is a school rule about permission.
items.ed: 'Students' (Mehrzahl) + 'aren't' + 'allowed to'. Das ist eine Schulregel ueber Erlaubnis.

items.p: My little brother ___ allowed to cross the road alone yet.
a: isn't
is not
items.h: 'My little brother' is one person (singular). Which negative of 'be' fits?
items.hd: 'My little brother' ist eine Person (Einzahl). Welche Verneinung von 'be' passt?
items.e: 'My little brother' is 3rd person singular → 'isn't allowed to'. Not old enough yet = no permission.
items.ed: 'My little brother' ist 3. Person Einzahl → 'isn't allowed to'. Noch nicht alt genug = keine Erlaubnis.

items.p: When I was younger, I ___ allowed to stay up late.
a: wasn't
was not
items.h: 'When I was younger' is in the past. What is the past form of 'am not'?
items.hd: 'When I was younger' ist in der Vergangenheit. Was ist die Vergangenheitsform von 'am not'?
items.e: In the past: I + wasn't + allowed to. The past of 'be allowed to' uses 'was/were'.
items.ed: In der Vergangenheit: I + wasn't + allowed to. Die Vergangenheit von 'be allowed to' verwendet 'was/were'.

items.p: Choose the correct sentence about a school rule:
items.c: We are not allowed to run in the corridors.
a: We are not allowed to run in the corridors.
ds: We do not allowed to run in the corridors.
We are not allowed run in the corridors.
We are not allow to run in the corridors.
items.h: The full structure is: subject + be (not) + allowed + to + verb.
items.hd: Die vollstaendige Struktur ist: Subjekt + be (not) + allowed + to + Verb.
items.e: 'Be allowed to' = be + allowed + to + base form. All four parts are needed.
items.ed: 'Be allowed to' = be + allowed + to + Grundform. Alle vier Teile werden gebraucht.

items.p: Which sentence correctly asks about permission?
items.c: Are we allowed to use a dictionary in the test?
a: Are we allowed to use a dictionary in the test?
ds: Do we allowed to use a dictionary in the test?
Are we allowed use a dictionary in the test?
Is we allowed to use a dictionary in the test?
items.h: Questions: form of 'be' + subject + allowed to + verb?
items.hd: Fragen: Form von 'be' + Subjekt + allowed to + Verb?
items.e: Question formation: Are + we + allowed to + use? 'Be' inverts with the subject.
items.ed: Fragebildung: Are + we + allowed to + use? 'Be' wird mit dem Subjekt umgestellt.

items.p: Which sentence about home rules is correct?
items.c: I'm not allowed to play video games on school nights.
a: I'm not allowed to play video games on school nights.
ds: I don't allowed to play video games on school nights.
I'm not allowed to playing video games on school nights.
I'm not allowed playing video games on school nights.
items.h: After 'allowed to', use the base form of the verb (play, not playing).
items.hd: Nach 'allowed to' verwende die Grundform des Verbs (play, nicht playing).
items.e: 'Allowed to' + base form: 'allowed to play', not 'allowed to playing' or 'allowed playing'.
items.ed: 'Allowed to' + Grundform: 'allowed to play', nicht 'allowed to playing' oder 'allowed playing'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: We don't allowed to chew gum at school.
a: aren't allowed
are not allowed
We aren't allowed to chew gum at school.
We are not allowed to chew gum at school.
items.h: 'Be allowed to' uses forms of 'be', not 'do'.
items.hd: 'Be allowed to' verwendet Formen von 'be', nicht 'do'.
items.e: It must be 'aren't allowed to', not 'don't allowed to'. The auxiliary is 'be', not 'do'.
items.ed: Es muss 'aren't allowed to' heissen, nicht 'don't allowed to'. Das Hilfsverb ist 'be', nicht 'do'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: He is allowed to goes out after 8 pm.
a: go out
go
He is allowed to go out after 8 pm.
He is allowed to go out after 8 pm
items.h: After 'allowed to', which form of the verb do we use?
items.hd: Nach 'allowed to', welche Form des Verbs verwenden wir?
items.e: After 'to' we always use the base form: 'go', not 'goes'. No -s ending after 'to'.
items.ed: Nach 'to' verwenden wir immer die Grundform: 'go', nicht 'goes'. Keine -s-Endung nach 'to'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: Is they allowed to park here?
a: Are they
Are they allowed to park here?
Are they allowed to park here
items.h: 'They' is plural. Which form of 'be' goes with 'they'?
items.hd: 'They' ist Mehrzahl. Welche Form von 'be' passt zu 'they'?
items.e: 'They' + 'are', not 'is'. In questions: 'Are they allowed to...?'
items.ed: 'They' + 'are', nicht 'is'. In Fragen: 'Are they allowed to...?'

items.p: A new student asks about school rules. Rewrite using 'be allowed to': 'You can't eat in class.' → 'You ________ eat in class.'
a: aren't allowed to
are not allowed to
items.h: 'Can't' (no permission) = 'be not allowed to'. Which form of 'be' for 'you'?
items.hd: 'Can't' (keine Erlaubnis) = 'be not allowed to'. Welche Form von 'be' fuer 'you'?
items.e: 'Can't' (permission) → 'aren't allowed to'. Both mean 'no permission'.
items.ed: 'Can't' (Erlaubnis) → 'aren't allowed to'. Beide bedeuten 'keine Erlaubnis'.

items.p: You're explaining home rules to your friend. Rewrite using 'be allowed to': 'My parents say I can go to the park after school.' → 'I ________ go to the park after school.'
a: am allowed to
'm allowed to
items.h: 'Can' (permission) = 'be allowed to'. Which form of 'be' for 'I'?
items.hd: 'Can' (Erlaubnis) = 'be allowed to'. Welche Form von 'be' fuer 'I'?
items.e: 'I can' (permission from parents) → 'I am allowed to'. 'Am' is the form of 'be' for 'I'.
items.ed: 'I can' (Erlaubnis der Eltern) → 'I am allowed to'. 'Am' ist die Form von 'be' fuer 'I'.

items.ed: Deutsch 'duerfen' = be allowed to. 'Wir duerfen nicht' = 'We aren't allowed to'.

items.e: German 'Darf er...?' = 'Is he allowed to...?' Question word order: Is + he + allowed to + verb.
items.ed: Deutsch 'Darf er...?' = 'Is he allowed to...?' Frage-Wortstellung: Is + he + allowed to + Verb.

She is allowed to ride her bike to school

items.ed: Wortstellung: She + is + allowed to + ride + her bike + to school.

items.h: I = am, he/she = is, we/they = are.
items.hd: I = am, he/she = is, we/they = are.
items.e: I + am allowed to, She/He + is allowed to, We/They + are allowed to.
items.ed: I + am allowed to, She/He + is allowed to, We/They + are allowed to.

What kind of pet are you allowed to have

What kind of pet you are allowed to have?
What kind of pet is you allowed to have?
items.h: Question word + form of 'be' + subject + allowed to + verb?
items.hd: Fragewort + Form von 'be' + Subjekt + allowed to + Verb?
items.e: Wh-question: What kind of pet + are + you + allowed to + have?
items.ed: W-Frage: What kind of pet + are + you + allowed to + have?

items.p: My mum ___ me watch TV after dinner.
