items.hd: 'My mum' ist 3. Person Einzahl. Welche Endung braucht das Verb?
items.e: 'My mum' is singular → 'lets'. Structure: let/lets + person + base form.
items.ed: 'My mum' ist Einzahl → 'lets'. Struktur: let/lets + Person + Grundform.

items.p: Our teacher doesn't ___ us use our phones.
a: let
items.h: After 'doesn't', always use the base form of the verb (no -s).
items.hd: Nach 'doesn't' immer die Grundform des Verbs verwenden (ohne -s).
items.e: After 'doesn't' we use the base form: 'doesn't let'. The -s is already in 'doesn't'.
items.ed: Nach 'doesn't' verwenden wir die Grundform: 'doesn't let'. Das -s ist bereits in 'doesn't'.

items.p: My parents don't ___ me stay out after 9 pm.
a: let
items.h: After 'don't', use the base form of the verb.
items.hd: Nach 'don't' verwende die Grundform des Verbs.
items.e: 'Don't let' = base form after 'don't'. Structure: don't/doesn't + let + person + base form.
items.ed: 'Don't let' = Grundform nach 'don't'. Struktur: don't/doesn't + let + Person + Grundform.

items.p: Dad ___ my sister drive his car. She's only 14!
a: doesn't let
does not let
items.h: 'Dad' is 3rd person singular. How do we make negatives with 3rd person?
items.hd: 'Dad' ist 3. Person Einzahl. Wie bilden wir die Verneinung in der 3. Person?
items.e: 'Dad' = he → 'doesn't let'. The -s moves to 'does', so the verb stays as 'let'.
items.ed: 'Dad' = he → 'doesn't let'. Das -s wandert zu 'does', also bleibt das Verb als 'let'.

items.p: My parents ___ me ___ (go) to the concert last weekend.
a: let ... go
let...go
items.h: 'Last weekend' is past. What is the past form of 'let'? And what form follows 'let'?
items.hd: 'Last weekend' ist Vergangenheit. Was ist die Vergangenheitsform von 'let'? Und welche Form folgt auf 'let'?
items.e: 'Let' is irregular — past form is also 'let'. After 'let': person + base form (no 'to').
items.ed: 'Let' ist unregelmaessig — die Vergangenheitsform ist auch 'let'. Nach 'let': Person + Grundform (ohne 'to').

items.p: My older sister never ___ me ___ (borrow) her clothes.

lets...borrow
items.h: 'My older sister' is 3rd person. After 'let', always use the base form of the next verb.
items.hd: 'My older sister' ist 3. Person. Nach 'let' immer die Grundform des naechsten Verbs verwenden.
items.e: 'Sister' is 3rd person → 'lets'. After 'let': person + base form → 'lets me borrow'.
items.ed: 'Sister' ist 3. Person → 'lets'. Nach 'let': Person + Grundform → 'lets me borrow'.

items.p: Choose the correct sentence:
items.c: My teacher lets us work in pairs.

My teacher lets us working in pairs.
items.h: After 'let/lets' + person, use the base form of the verb (no 'to', no '-ing').
items.hd: Nach 'let/lets' + Person die Grundform des Verbs verwenden (ohne 'to', ohne '-ing').
items.e: 'Let + person + base form': 'lets us work', not 'lets us to work' or 'lets us working'.
items.ed: 'Let + Person + Grundform': 'lets us work', nicht 'lets us to work' oder 'lets us working'.

My parents don't let me playing video games on weekdays.
My parents doesn't let me play video games on weekdays.
items.h: 'Parents' is plural → 'don't'. After 'let' + person, use the base form.
items.hd: 'Parents' ist Mehrzahl → 'don't'. Nach 'let' + Person die Grundform verwenden.
items.e: Plural subject → 'don't let'. Then: person + base form → 'don't let me play'.
items.ed: Subjekt in der Mehrzahl → 'don't let'. Dann: Person + Grundform → 'don't let me play'.

items.p: Which sentence about permission is correct?
items.c: The coach doesn't let us leave training early.

The coach doesn't lets us leave training early.
The coach don't let us leave training early.
items.h: 'Coach' is singular → 'doesn't'. After 'doesn't let', use base form.
items.hd: 'Coach' ist Einzahl → 'doesn't'. Nach 'doesn't let' die Grundform verwenden.
items.e: Singular 'coach' → 'doesn't let'. After 'let': person + base form → 'doesn't let us leave'.
items.ed: Einzahl 'coach' → 'doesn't let'. Nach 'let': Person + Grundform → 'doesn't let us leave'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She lets me to use her computer.
a: lets me use
She lets me use her computer.
She lets me use her computer
items.h: Is there a 'to' after 'let'? Should there be?
items.hd: Gibt es ein 'to' nach 'let'? Sollte es dort stehen?
items.e: After 'let' there is no 'to'. It's 'let + person + base form': 'lets me use', not 'lets me to use'.
items.ed: Nach 'let' kommt kein 'to'. Es heisst 'let + Person + Grundform': 'lets me use', nicht 'lets me to use'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: My dad doesn't lets me drive his car.

My dad doesn't let me drive his car.
My dad doesn't let me drive his car

items.hd: Nach 'doesn't', brauchen wir das -s am Hauptverb?
items.e: After 'doesn't', the verb goes back to base form: 'doesn't let', not 'doesn't lets'.
items.ed: Nach 'doesn't' steht das Verb in der Grundform: 'doesn't let', nicht 'doesn't lets'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: My parents didn't letted me go to the party.

My parents didn't let me go to the party.
My parents didn't let me go to the party
items.h: 'Let' is irregular. What is its past form?
items.hd: 'Let' ist unregelmaessig. Wie lautet die Vergangenheitsform?
items.e: 'Let' is irregular: let → let → let (same form!). After 'didn't', always use the base form.
items.ed: 'Let' ist unregelmaessig: let → let → let (gleiche Form!). Nach 'didn't' immer die Grundform verwenden.

items.p: You're telling a friend about your school rules. Rewrite using 'let': 'Our teacher allows us to listen to music during art.' → 'Our teacher ________ to music during art.'

items.hd: 'Allows us to listen' → 'lets us...' (welche Form folgt auf 'let'?)
items.e: 'Allows us to listen' = 'lets us listen'. After 'let': no 'to', just the base form.
items.ed: 'Allows us to listen' = 'lets us listen'. Nach 'let': kein 'to', nur die Grundform.

items.p: You're telling a friend about home rules. Rewrite using 'let': 'I'm not allowed to have a dog.' → 'My parents ________ a dog.'

items.hd: 'I'm not allowed to' = 'My parents don't let me...' (wer gibt die Erlaubnis?)
items.e: 'Not allowed to' = 'don't/doesn't let'. The subject changes to the person who gives or denies permission.
items.ed: 'Not allowed to' = 'don't/doesn't let'. Das Subjekt aendert sich zur Person, die die Erlaubnis gibt oder verweigert.

items.ed: Deutsch 'lassen' = 'let'. 'Lassen mich aufbleiben' = 'let me stay up'. Kein 'to' nach 'let'.

items.p: My grandmother ___ speak four languages when she was young.
a: could
items.h: Speaking languages is a general ability (she always had this skill).
items.hd: Sprachen sprechen ist eine allgemeine Faehigkeit (sie hatte diese Faehigkeit immer).
items.e: 'Could' for general abilities: She could speak four languages = she had this ability in general.
items.ed: 'Could' fuer allgemeine Faehigkeiten: Sie konnte vier Sprachen sprechen = sie hatte diese Faehigkeit allgemein.

items.p: The exam was hard, but I ___ pass it.
a: was able to
managed to
items.h: This is about a specific achievement (one exam, one success). 'Could' or 'was able to'?
items.hd: Hier geht es um einen bestimmten Erfolg (eine Pruefung, ein Erfolg). 'Could' oder 'was able to'?
items.e: 'Was able to' is used for specific achievements: you succeeded in doing something on a particular occasion.
items.ed: 'Was able to' wird fuer bestimmte Erfolge verwendet: du hast etwas bei einer bestimmten Gelegenheit geschafft.

items.p: After trying for hours, she ___ fix the computer.
a: was able to
managed to
items.h: 'After trying for hours' shows this was a specific success — she succeeded on that one occasion.
items.hd: 'After trying for hours' zeigt, dass dies ein bestimmter Erfolg war — sie hat es bei dieser Gelegenheit geschafft.
items.e: 'Was able to' for specific success: after effort, she succeeded. 'Could' would mean general ability.
items.ed: 'Was able to' fuer bestimmten Erfolg: nach Anstrengung hat sie es geschafft. 'Could' wuerde allgemeine Faehigkeit bedeuten.

items.p: He ___ swim when he was a child, but yesterday he ___ swim across the lake for the first time.
a: could ... was able to
could...was able to
ds: was able to ... was able to
items.h: First gap: general ability (always could swim). Second gap: specific achievement (first time across the lake).
items.hd: Erste Luecke: allgemeine Faehigkeit (konnte immer schwimmen). Zweite Luecke: bestimmter Erfolg (zum ersten Mal ueber den See).
items.e: 'Could' = general ability (could always swim). 'Was able to' = specific achievement (succeeded in swimming across the lake).
items.ed: 'Could' = allgemeine Faehigkeit (konnte immer schwimmen). 'Was able to' = bestimmter Erfolg (es geschafft, ueber den See zu schwimmen).

items.p: I ___ (not) hear what the teacher said because the class was so noisy, but I ___ read the instructions on the board.
a: couldn't ... was able to
could not ... was able to
couldn't...was able to
items.h: First gap: failed to hear (negative). Second gap: managed to read (specific success despite difficulty).
items.hd: Erste Luecke: konnte nicht hoeren (Verneinung). Zweite Luecke: hat es geschafft zu lesen (bestimmter Erfolg trotz Schwierigkeit).
items.e: 'Couldn't' for inability (negative — both 'couldn't' and 'wasn't able to' work). 'Was able to' for specific success.
items.ed: 'Couldn't' fuer Unfaehigkeit (Verneinung). 'Was able to' fuer bestimmten Erfolg trotz Schwierigkeit.

items.p: Which sentence correctly describes a general past ability?
items.c: She could play the piano when she was six.
a: She could play the piano when she was six.
ds: She was able to play the piano when she was six.
She could to play the piano when she was six.
She can play the piano when she was six.
items.h: General ability (she always had this skill) → which word is the most natural choice?
items.hd: Allgemeine Faehigkeit (sie hatte diese Faehigkeit immer) → welches Wort ist am natuerlichsten?
items.e: 'Could' is the most natural choice for general abilities in the past. No 'to' after 'could'.
items.ed: 'Could' ist die natuerlichste Wahl fuer allgemeine Faehigkeiten in der Vergangenheit. Kein 'to' nach 'could'.

items.p: The fire alarm went off. Which sentence is correct?
items.c: Everyone was able to get out safely.
a: Everyone was able to get out safely.
ds: Everyone was able get out safely.
Everyone could to get out safely.
items.h: This was a specific event (one fire, one escape). Which form is for specific achievements?
items.hd: Das war ein bestimmtes Ereignis (ein Brand, eine Flucht). Welche Form ist fuer bestimmte Erfolge?
items.e: 'Was able to' is used because this was a specific achievement — everyone managed to escape from that particular fire.
items.ed: 'Was able to' wird verwendet, weil dies ein bestimmter Erfolg war — alle haben es geschafft, bei diesem Brand zu entkommen.

items.p: Which pair of sentences uses 'could' and 'was able to' correctly?
items.c: He could run fast as a child. / He was able to win the race last Saturday.
a: He could run fast as a child. / He was able to win the race last Saturday.
ds: He was able to run fast as a child. / He could win the race last Saturday.
He could run fast as a child. / He could win the race last Saturday.
He was able to run fast as a child. / He was able to win the race last Saturday.
items.h: General ability (always fast) → 'could'. Specific achievement (won one race) → 'was able to'.
items.hd: Allgemeine Faehigkeit (immer schnell) → 'could'. Bestimmter Erfolg (ein Rennen gewonnen) → 'was able to'.
items.e: 'Could' = general ability (he was always fast). 'Was able to' = specific achievement (won that particular race).
items.ed: 'Could' = allgemeine Faehigkeit (er war immer schnell). 'Was able to' = bestimmter Erfolg (dieses bestimmte Rennen gewonnen).

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She could to swim very well as a child.
a: could swim
She could swim very well as a child.
She could swim very well as a child
items.h: 'Could' is a modal verb. Do modal verbs need 'to' before the next verb?
items.hd: 'Could' ist ein Modalverb. Brauchen Modalverben 'to' vor dem naechsten Verb?
items.e: Modal verbs (can, could, must, etc.) are followed directly by the base form — no 'to'.
items.ed: Modalverben (can, could, must usw.) stehen direkt vor der Grundform — ohne 'to'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: The test was very difficult, but I could pass it.
a: was able to pass
I was able to pass it.
I was able to pass it
managed to pass
items.h: Is this a general ability or a specific achievement (passing one particular test)?
items.hd: Ist das eine allgemeine Faehigkeit oder ein bestimmter Erfolg (eine bestimmte Pruefung bestehen)?
items.e: Passing a specific difficult test is a specific achievement → 'was able to', not 'could'.
items.ed: Eine bestimmte schwierige Pruefung bestehen ist ein bestimmter Erfolg → 'was able to', nicht 'could'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: The door was locked, but my brother could open it with a hairpin.
a: was able to open
My brother was able to open it with a hairpin.
managed to open
items.h: Opening a locked door with a hairpin is a specific success, not a general ability.
items.hd: Eine verschlossene Tuer mit einer Haarnadel zu oeffnen ist ein bestimmter Erfolg, keine allgemeine Faehigkeit.
items.e: This was a specific achievement (one locked door, one success) → 'was able to open', not 'could open'.
items.ed: Das war ein bestimmter Erfolg (eine verschlossene Tuer, ein Erfolg) → 'was able to open', nicht 'could open'.

items.p: You're describing your childhood to a friend. Complete: 'When I was eight, I ________ (ride) a horse, but I ________ (not / ski).'
a: could ride ... couldn't ski
could ride...couldn't ski
could ride ... could not ski
items.h: Both are general abilities you had (or didn't have) as a child.
items.hd: Beides sind allgemeine Faehigkeiten, die du als Kind hattest (oder nicht hattest).
items.e: General past abilities (things you always could or couldn't do as a child) → 'could'/'couldn't'.
items.ed: Allgemeine vergangene Faehigkeiten (Dinge, die man als Kind immer konnte oder nicht konnte) → 'could'/'couldn't'.

items.p: You're telling a story about last week. Rewrite using 'was/were able to': 'We managed to finish the project on time.' → 'We ________ the project on time.'
a: were able to finish
items.h: 'Managed to' = 'was/were able to' (specific achievement). Which form of 'be' for 'we'?
items.hd: 'Managed to' = 'was/were able to' (bestimmter Erfolg). Welche Form von 'be' fuer 'we'?
items.e: 'Managed to finish' = 'were able to finish'. 'We' takes 'were'. Both mean: succeeded in doing something specific.
items.ed: 'Managed to finish' = 'were able to finish'. 'We' braucht 'were'. Beides bedeutet: etwas Bestimmtes geschafft.

items.p: Translate: Als Kind konnte ich gut zeichnen.
items.c: When I was a child, I could draw well.
a: When I was a child, I could draw well.
When I was a child, I could draw well
As a child, I could draw well.
As a child I could draw well.
I could draw well when I was a child.
items.h: 'Konnte' for a general ability (always could do it) = 'could' in English.
items.hd: 'Konnte' fuer eine allgemeine Faehigkeit = 'could'.
items.e: German 'konnte' for general ability = English 'could'. Drawing well was a permanent ability.
items.ed: Deutsch 'konnte' fuer allgemeine Faehigkeit = Englisch 'could'. Gut zeichnen war eine dauerhafte Faehigkeit.

items.p: Translate: Trotz des Regens konnten wir das Fussballspiel gewinnen.
items.c: Despite the rain, we were able to win the football match.
a: Despite the rain, we were able to win the football match.
Despite the rain, we were able to win the football match
Despite the rain we were able to win the football match.
Despite the rain, we managed to win the football match.
Despite the rain, we were able to win the football game.
items.h: Winning a match is a specific achievement (one match, one success). German 'konnten' here = 'were able to'.
items.hd: Ein Spiel gewinnen ist ein bestimmter Erfolg. 'Konnten' hier = 'were able to'.
items.e: German 'konnten' can mean both 'could' (general) and 'were able to' (specific). Winning one match = specific → 'were able to'.
items.ed: Deutsch 'konnten' kann sowohl 'could' (allgemein) als auch 'were able to' (bestimmt) bedeuten. Ein Spiel gewinnen = bestimmt → 'were able to'.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: play / my / could / when / chess / grandfather / was / he / young
items.c: My grandfather could play chess when he was young.
a: My grandfather could play chess when he was young.
My grandfather could play chess when he was young
When he was young, my grandfather could play chess.
When he was young my grandfather could play chess.
items.h: Subject + could + base form + when-clause.
items.hd: Subjekt + could + Grundform + when-Satz.
items.e: Word order: My grandfather + could + play chess + when he was young.
items.ed: Wortstellung: My grandfather + could + play chess + when he was young.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: to / able / was / finish / the / she / race
items.c: She was able to finish the race.
a: She was able to finish the race.
She was able to finish the race
items.h: Subject + was + able + to + base form + object.
items.hd: Subjekt + was + able + to + Grundform + Objekt.
items.e: Word order: She + was + able to + finish + the race.
items.ed: Wortstellung: She + was + able to + finish + the race.

items.p: Match each sentence with 'could' or 'was/were able to'. 1: She ___ read when she was four. 2: He ___ escape from the burning building. 3: As a child, I ___ run very fast. 4: After two hours, we ___ find the lost dog. 5: My mum ___ speak French fluently when she lived in Paris.
items.h: General ability (always) = could. Specific achievement (one occasion) = was/were able to.
items.hd: Allgemeine Faehigkeit (immer) = could. Bestimmter Erfolg (eine Gelegenheit) = was/were able to.
items.e: 1: General (always could read) → could. 2: Specific escape → was able to. 3: General (always fast) → could. 4: Specific find → were able to. 5: General (fluent skill) → could.
items.ed: 1: Allgemein (konnte immer lesen) → could. 2: Bestimmte Flucht → was able to. 3: Allgemein (immer schnell) → could. 4: Bestimmte Suche → were able to. 5: Allgemein (fliessend) → could.

items.p: Your friend is talking about their childhood. Ask if they could play an instrument. Start with 'Could...'
items.c: Could you play an instrument when you were a child?
a: Could you play an instrument when you were a child?
Could you play an instrument when you were a child
Could you play an instrument as a child?
Could you play a musical instrument when you were young?
ds: Could you to play an instrument when you were a child?
Did you could play an instrument when you were a child?
Were you able play an instrument when you were a child?
items.h: 'Could' questions: Could + subject + base form? No 'to', no 'did'.
items.hd: 'Could'-Fragen: Could + Subjekt + Grundform? Kein 'to', kein 'did'.
items.e: Questions with 'could': Could + you + play? No extra auxiliary needed. General ability question.
items.ed: Fragen mit 'could': Could + you + play? Kein zusaetzliches Hilfsverb noetig. Frage nach allgemeiner Faehigkeit.

items.p: When I was little, I ___ (not allowed to / stay) up late.
a: wasn't allowed to stay
was not allowed to stay
items.h: Use was/were + (not) + allowed to + base verb. Which form of 'be' matches 'I'?
items.hd: Verwende was/were + (not) + allowed to + Grundform. Welche Form von 'be' passt zu 'I'?
items.e: 'I' takes 'was' in the past. Negative: wasn't allowed to + base verb. 'I wasn't allowed to stay up late.'
items.ed: 'I' braucht 'was' in der Vergangenheit. Verneinung: wasn't allowed to + Grundform. 'I wasn't allowed to stay up late.'

items.p: We ___ (allowed to / use) our phones in class last year.
a: were allowed to use
items.h: 'We' needs a plural past form of 'be'. Look at the time signal 'last year'.
items.hd: 'We' braucht eine Mehrzahl-Vergangenheitsform von 'be'. Schau auf das Zeitsignal 'last year'.
items.e: 'We' takes 'were' in the past. 'Were allowed to use' = had permission to use phones.
items.ed: 'We' braucht 'were' in der Vergangenheit. 'Were allowed to use' = hatten die Erlaubnis, Handys zu benutzen.

items.p: Next year, students ___ (be allowed to / choose) their own topics.
a: will be allowed to choose
items.h: For future permission, use will + be + allowed to + base verb.
items.hd: Für zukünftige Erlaubnis verwende will + be + allowed to + Grundform.
items.e: Future permission: will be allowed to + base verb. 'Students will be allowed to choose.'
items.ed: Zukünftige Erlaubnis: will be allowed to + Grundform. 'Students will be allowed to choose.'

items.p: She ___ (not allowed to / go) to the party because she was too young.
a: wasn't allowed to go
was not allowed to go
items.h: The second part of the sentence is in the past. 'She' needs a singular past form.
items.hd: Der zweite Teil des Satzes ist in der Vergangenheit. 'She' braucht eine Einzahl-Vergangenheitsform.
items.e: 'She' takes 'was' in the past. Negative: wasn't allowed to go. The reason ('she was too young') confirms the past tense.
items.ed: 'She' braucht 'was' in der Vergangenheit. Verneinung: wasn't allowed to go. Der Grund ('she was too young') bestätigt die Vergangenheit.

items.p: In the old school, they ___ (not allowed to / wear) trainers, but in the new school they ___ (be allowed to / wear) whatever shoes they like.
items.c: weren't allowed to wear ... will be allowed to wear
a: weren't allowed to wear ... will be allowed to wear
were not allowed to wear ... will be allowed to wear
ds: wasn't allowed to wear ... will be allowed to wear
weren't allowed to wear ... are allowed to wear
didn't allowed to wear ... will allowed to wear
items.h: First gap: past negative permission for 'they'. Second gap: future permission with 'will'.
items.hd: Erste Lücke: vergangene verneinte Erlaubnis für 'they'. Zweite Lücke: zukünftige Erlaubnis mit 'will'.
items.e: 'They weren't allowed to wear' (past, no permission) contrasts with 'will be allowed to wear' (future permission).
items.ed: 'They weren't allowed to wear' (Vergangenheit, keine Erlaubnis) steht im Gegensatz zu 'will be allowed to wear' (zukünftige Erlaubnis).

items.p: ___ you ___ (allowed to / bring) pets to school when you were in primary school?
a: Were ... allowed to bring
Were you allowed to bring
items.h: For past permission questions, put the past form of 'be' before the subject.
items.hd: Für Fragen zur vergangenen Erlaubnis stelle die Vergangenheitsform von 'be' vor das Subjekt.
items.e: Question form: Were + subject + allowed to + base verb? 'Were you allowed to bring pets?'
items.ed: Frageform: Were + Subjekt + allowed to + Grundform? 'Were you allowed to bring pets?'

items.p: Which sentence correctly expresses that Tom had permission to leave early yesterday?
items.c: Tom was allowed to leave early yesterday.
a: Tom was allowed to leave early yesterday.
ds: Tom were allowed to leave early yesterday.
Tom allowed to leave early yesterday.
Tom did allowed to leave early yesterday.
items.h: Singular subjects in the past need 'was'. The structure is: was/were + allowed to + verb.
items.hd: Einzahl-Subjekte brauchen 'was' in der Vergangenheit. Die Struktur ist: was/were + allowed to + Verb.
items.e: 'Tom' is singular, so 'was allowed to leave' is correct. This expresses past permission.
items.ed: 'Tom' ist Einzahl, also ist 'was allowed to leave' richtig. Das drückt vergangene Erlaubnis aus.

items.p: Choose the correct sentence about future permission.
items.c: We will be allowed to stay up late on New Year's Eve.
a: We will be allowed to stay up late on New Year's Eve.
ds: We will allowed to stay up late on New Year's Eve.
We will be allow to stay up late on New Year's Eve.
We are allowed to stay up late on New Year's Eve.
items.h: Future permission = will + be + allowed to + base verb.
items.hd: Zukünftige Erlaubnis = will + be + allowed to + Grundform.
items.e: The full future permission form is: will be allowed to + base verb. All three parts are needed.
items.ed: Die vollständige Form für zukünftige Erlaubnis ist: will be allowed to + Grundform. Alle drei Teile werden gebraucht.

items.p: Which sentence correctly shows that the children did NOT have permission to swim in the lake?
items.c: The children weren't allowed to swim in the lake.
a: The children weren't allowed to swim in the lake.
ds: The children wasn't allowed to swim in the lake.
The children didn't allowed to swim in the lake.
The children weren't allowed swimming in the lake.
items.h: 'The children' is plural. After 'allowed to', use the base form of the verb.
items.hd: 'The children' ist Mehrzahl. Nach 'allowed to' verwende die Grundform des Verbs.
items.e: Plural subject 'children' takes 'weren't'. After 'allowed to', always use the base form: 'to swim'.
items.ed: Mehrzahl-Subjekt 'children' braucht 'weren't'. Nach 'allowed to' immer die Grundform: 'to swim'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: We was allowed to leave school early on Friday.
a: were allowed
We were allowed to leave school early on Friday.
We were allowed to leave school early on Friday
items.h: Look at the subject 'We'. Which form of 'be' goes with it in the past?
items.hd: Schau dir das Subjekt 'We' an. Welche Form von 'be' passt in der Vergangenheit?
items.e: 'We' takes 'were' in the past, not 'was'. Correct: 'We were allowed to leave.'
items.ed: 'We' braucht 'were' in der Vergangenheit, nicht 'was'. Richtig: 'We were allowed to leave.'

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: He didn't allowed to eat sweets before dinner.
a: wasn't allowed
was not allowed
He wasn't allowed to eat sweets before dinner.
He was not allowed to eat sweets before dinner.
items.h: Past permission uses be + allowed to, not did + allowed to.
items.hd: Vergangene Erlaubnis verwendet be + allowed to, nicht did + allowed to.
items.e: For past permission, use was/were (not) allowed to, NOT did/didn't. Correct: 'He wasn't allowed to eat.'
items.ed: Für vergangene Erlaubnis verwende was/were (not) allowed to, NICHT did/didn't. Richtig: 'He wasn't allowed to eat.'

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: Next summer, we will be allowed going camping on our own.
a: allowed to go
will be allowed to go
Next summer, we will be allowed to go camping on our own.
Next summer, we will be allowed to go camping on our own
items.h: What comes after 'allowed'? Is it 'going' or 'to go'?
items.hd: Was kommt nach 'allowed'? Ist es 'going' oder 'to go'?
items.e: The structure is 'be allowed TO + base verb'. Use 'to go', not 'going'. Correct: 'will be allowed to go.'
items.ed: Die Struktur ist 'be allowed TO + Grundform'. Verwende 'to go', nicht 'going'. Richtig: 'will be allowed to go.'

items.p: Your teacher is explaining the old school rules. Rewrite using 'allowed to': 'Students could not run in the corridors.' → Students ___.
items.c: weren't allowed to run in the corridors
a: weren't allowed to run in the corridors
were not allowed to run in the corridors
items.h: 'Could not' = did not have permission = were not allowed to.
items.hd: 'Could not' = hatten keine Erlaubnis = were not allowed to.
items.e: 'Students could not run' means they didn't have permission. Rewrite: 'Students weren't allowed to run in the corridors.'
items.ed: 'Students could not run' bedeutet, sie hatten keine Erlaubnis. Umformung: 'Students weren't allowed to run in the corridors.'

items.p: A parent is talking about future changes at school. Rewrite: 'At the moment, children can't use the new playground. But from September, children ___.'
items.c: will be allowed to use the new playground
a: will be allowed to use the new playground
will be allowed to use it
items.h: Change 'can' (present) to 'will be allowed to' (future).
items.hd: Ändere 'can' (Gegenwart) zu 'will be allowed to' (Zukunft).
items.e: Future permission: will be allowed to + base verb. 'Children will be allowed to use the new playground.'
items.ed: Zukünftige Erlaubnis: will be allowed to + Grundform. 'Children will be allowed to use the new playground.'

items.p: Translate into English: 'Wir durften im Park Fußball spielen.'
items.c: We were allowed to play football in the park.
a: We were allowed to play football in the park.
We were allowed to play football in the park
items.h: 'Durften' = had permission = were allowed to.
items.hd: 'Durften' = were allowed to.
items.e: 'Durften' (past permission) = were allowed to. 'We were allowed to play football in the park.'
items.ed: 'Durften' (vergangene Erlaubnis) = were allowed to. 'We were allowed to play football in the park.'

items.p: Translate into English: 'Er wird nächstes Jahr allein reisen dürfen.'
items.c: He will be allowed to travel alone next year.
a: He will be allowed to travel alone next year.
He will be allowed to travel on his own next year.
Next year, he will be allowed to travel alone.
items.h: 'Wird ... dürfen' = will be allowed to.
items.hd: 'Wird ... dürfen' = will be allowed to.
items.e: 'Wird dürfen' (future permission) = will be allowed to. 'He will be allowed to travel alone next year.'
items.ed: 'Wird dürfen' (zukünftige Erlaubnis) = will be allowed to. 'He will be allowed to travel alone next year.'

items.p: Put the words in order: allowed / wasn't / she / to / stay / out / late
items.c: She wasn't allowed to stay out late.
a: She wasn't allowed to stay out late.
She wasn't allowed to stay out late
items.h: Start with the subject, then wasn't allowed to, then the rest.
items.hd: Beginne mit dem Subjekt, dann wasn't allowed to, dann der Rest.
items.e: The word order is: Subject + wasn't + allowed to + base verb + rest. 'She wasn't allowed to stay out late.'
items.ed: Die Wortstellung ist: Subjekt + wasn't + allowed to + Grundform + Rest. 'She wasn't allowed to stay out late.'

items.p: Put the words in order: will / be / you / allowed / to / drive / when / you / are / 18
items.c: You will be allowed to drive when you are 18.
a: You will be allowed to drive when you are 18.
You will be allowed to drive when you are 18
When you are 18, you will be allowed to drive.
items.h: Start with 'You will be allowed to...' then add the time clause.
items.hd: Beginne mit 'You will be allowed to...' dann füge den Zeitsatz hinzu.
items.e: Future permission: Subject + will be allowed to + base verb. 'You will be allowed to drive when you are 18.'
items.ed: Zukünftige Erlaubnis: Subjekt + will be allowed to + Grundform. 'You will be allowed to drive when you are 18.'

items.p: Match the sentence halves. 1: When I was five, I 2: Last summer, we 3: Next year, students 4: My little sister 5: In the future, everyone
ds: a: were allowed to camp by the river.
b: wasn't allowed to watch the scary film.
c: wasn't allowed to cross the street alone.
d: will be allowed to choose their own careers.
e: will be allowed to bring tablets to class.
items.h: Match the time expression with the correct tense: past = was/were allowed to, future = will be allowed to.
items.hd: Ordne den Zeitausdruck der richtigen Zeitform zu: Vergangenheit = was/were allowed to, Zukunft = will be allowed to.
items.e: 1→c (past, singular I), 2→a (past, plural we), 3→e (future), 4→b (past, singular she), 5→d (future).
items.ed: 1→c (Vergangenheit, Einzahl I), 2→a (Vergangenheit, Mehrzahl we), 3→e (Zukunft), 4→b (Vergangenheit, Einzahl she), 5→d (Zukunft).

items.p: Your friend is talking about the rules at her old school. Which sentence fits?
items.c: We weren't allowed to eat in the classroom.
a: We weren't allowed to eat in the classroom.
ds: We aren't allowed to eat in the classroom.
We won't be allowed to eat in the classroom.
We don't allowed to eat in the classroom.
items.h: She's talking about her OLD school. Which tense do you need?
items.hd: Sie spricht über ihre ALTE Schule. Welche Zeitform brauchst du?
items.e: The context is past (old school), so we need 'weren't allowed to'. Present or future forms don't fit.
items.ed: Der Kontext ist Vergangenheit (alte Schule), also brauchen wir 'weren't allowed to'. Gegenwart oder Zukunft passen nicht.

items.p: After the operation, he ___ (be able to / walk) again.
a: will be able to walk
items.h: You cannot say 'will can'. The alternative for 'can' in the future is 'will be able to'.
items.hd: Man kann nicht 'will can' sagen. Die Alternative für 'can' in der Zukunft ist 'will be able to'.
items.e: 'Can' has no future form. Use 'will be able to + base verb'. 'He will be able to walk again.'
items.ed: 'Can' hat keine Zukunftsform. Verwende 'will be able to + Grundform'. 'He will be able to walk again.'

items.p: I ___ (be able to / speak) three languages since I was a child.
a: have been able to speak
've been able to speak
items.h: 'Since' signals the present perfect. Use have/has + been able to.
items.hd: 'Since' signalisiert das Present Perfect. Verwende have/has + been able to.
items.e: 'Since I was a child' needs present perfect: have been able to + base verb. 'I' takes 'have'.
items.ed: 'Since I was a child' braucht Present Perfect: have been able to + Grundform. 'I' braucht 'have'.

items.p: She ___ (not / be able to / find) her keys for two hours.
a: hasn't been able to find
has not been able to find
items.h: 'For two hours' with an ongoing situation needs present perfect. 'She' takes 'has'.
items.hd: 'For two hours' bei einer andauernden Situation braucht Present Perfect. 'She' braucht 'has'.
items.e: 'For two hours' + still happening = present perfect. 'She hasn't been able to find her keys.'
items.ed: 'For two hours' + dauert noch an = Present Perfect. 'She hasn't been able to find her keys.'

items.p: With this new app, you ___ (be able to / learn) vocabulary faster.
a: will be able to learn
'll be able to learn
items.h: This is about a future ability. 'Will can' is not possible in English.
items.hd: Es geht um eine zukünftige Fähigkeit. 'Will can' ist im Englischen nicht möglich.
items.e: Future ability: will be able to + base verb. You cannot combine two modal verbs.
items.ed: Zukünftige Fähigkeit: will be able to + Grundform. Man kann nicht zwei Modalverben kombinieren.

items.p: They ___ (not / be able to / finish) the project yet, but they ___ (be able to / present) it next Monday.
items.c: haven't been able to finish ... will be able to present
a: haven't been able to finish ... will be able to present
ds: hasn't been able to finish ... will be able to present
haven't been able to finish ... can present
weren't able to finish ... will be able to present
items.h: 'Yet' needs present perfect. 'Next Monday' needs future.
items.hd: 'Yet' braucht Present Perfect. 'Next Monday' braucht die Zukunft.
items.e: 'Yet' signals present perfect (haven't been able to), 'next Monday' signals future (will be able to).
items.ed: 'Yet' signalisiert Present Perfect (haven't been able to), 'next Monday' signalisiert Zukunft (will be able to).

items.p: ___ you ___ (be able to / solve) the maths problem? You've been working on it for ages!
a: Have ... been able to solve
Have you been able to solve
items.h: The situation is still ongoing ('for ages'). Form a present perfect question.
items.hd: Die Situation dauert noch an ('for ages'). Bilde eine Present-Perfect-Frage.
items.e: Present perfect question: Have + subject + been able to + base verb? 'Have you been able to solve it?'
items.ed: Present-Perfect-Frage: Have + Subjekt + been able to + Grundform? 'Have you been able to solve it?'

items.p: Which sentence correctly talks about a future ability?
items.c: Robots will be able to cook meals for us.
a: Robots will be able to cook meals for us.
ds: Robots will can cook meals for us.
Robots will be able cook meals for us.
Robots are able to cook meals for us.
items.h: For future ability, replace 'can' with 'will be able to'.
items.hd: Für zukünftige Fähigkeit ersetze 'can' durch 'will be able to'.
items.e: 'Will be able to + base verb' is the future form of 'can'. You cannot say 'will can'.
items.ed: 'Will be able to + Grundform' ist die Zukunftsform von 'can'. Man kann nicht 'will can' sagen.

items.p: Maria started learning guitar two years ago. Which sentence is correct?
items.c: She has been able to play simple songs since last year.
a: She has been able to play simple songs since last year.
ds: She have been able to play simple songs since last year.
She has been able play simple songs since last year.
She could play simple songs since last year.
items.h: 'Since last year' tells you the ability started in the past and continues. Which tense shows that?
items.hd: 'Since last year' zeigt, dass die Fähigkeit in der Vergangenheit begann und andauert. Welche Zeitform zeigt das?
items.e: 'Since' + present perfect: has been able to play. The ability started last year and continues.
items.ed: 'Since' + Present Perfect: has been able to play. Die Fähigkeit begann letztes Jahr und dauert an.

items.p: Which sentence is grammatically WRONG?
items.c: I will can swim after the lessons.
a: I will can swim after the lessons.
ds: I will be able to swim after the lessons.
I have been able to swim since I was six.
She hasn't been able to come to practice for weeks.
items.h: Look for a double modal error. Can you put two modal verbs together?
items.hd: Suche nach einem Doppel-Modal-Fehler. Kann man zwei Modalverben zusammen verwenden?
items.e: 'Will can' is wrong — you cannot combine two modal verbs. The correct form is 'will be able to swim'.
items.ed: 'Will can' ist falsch — man kann nicht zwei Modalverben kombinieren. Die richtige Form ist 'will be able to swim'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She will can drive a car when she is 18.
a: will be able to drive
She will be able to drive a car when she is 18.
She will be able to drive a car when she is 18
items.h: You cannot use 'will' and 'can' together. What is the alternative?
items.hd: Man kann 'will' und 'can' nicht zusammen verwenden. Was ist die Alternative?
items.e: Two modal verbs cannot be used together. Replace 'will can' with 'will be able to'.
items.ed: Zwei Modalverben können nicht zusammen verwendet werden. Ersetze 'will can' durch 'will be able to'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: He have been able to read since he was five.
a: has been able
He has been able to read since he was five.
He has been able to read since he was five
items.h: Look at the subject 'He'. Which auxiliary does it need in the present perfect?
items.hd: Schau dir das Subjekt 'He' an. Welches Hilfsverb braucht es im Present Perfect?
items.e: 'He' takes 'has' in the present perfect, not 'have'. Correct: 'He has been able to read.'
items.ed: 'He' braucht 'has' im Present Perfect, nicht 'have'. Richtig: 'He has been able to read.'

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: I haven't been able finishing my homework yet.
a: able to finish
I haven't been able to finish my homework yet.
I haven't been able to finish my homework yet
items.h: After 'able', what small word do you need before the verb?
items.hd: Nach 'able', welches kleine Wort brauchst du vor dem Verb?
items.e: The structure is 'be able TO + base verb'. Use 'to finish', not 'finishing'.
items.ed: Die Struktur ist 'be able TO + Grundform'. Verwende 'to finish', nicht 'finishing'.

items.p: You are explaining that your baby brother learned to walk recently. Rewrite using 'be able to': 'My brother can walk now. He learned last month.' → My brother ___.
items.c: has been able to walk since last month
a: has been able to walk since last month
items.h: An ability that started in the past and continues now = have/has been able to + since.
items.hd: Eine Fähigkeit, die in der Vergangenheit begann und andauert = have/has been able to + since.
items.e: He learned last month → the ability started then and continues. Use present perfect: 'has been able to walk since last month'.
items.ed: Er lernte letzten Monat → die Fähigkeit begann damals und dauert an. Verwende Present Perfect: 'has been able to walk since last month'.

items.p: Your friend asks about your plans after school. Rewrite: 'I can't travel now because I have exams, but after the exams, I ___.'
a: will be able to travel
items.h: Change 'can't' to a future ability form.
items.hd: Ändere 'can't' zu einer zukünftigen Fähigkeitsform.
items.e: Future ability: will be able to + base verb. After the exams = future time.
items.ed: Zukünftige Fähigkeit: will be able to + Grundform. Nach den Prüfungen = Zukunft.

items.p: Translate into English: 'Sie kann seit drei Jahren Klavier spielen.'
items.c: She has been able to play the piano for three years.
a: She has been able to play the piano for three years.
She has been able to play piano for three years.
items.h: 'Seit drei Jahren' + ongoing ability = present perfect with 'for'.
items.hd: 'Seit drei Jahren' + andauernde Fähigkeit = Present Perfect mit 'for'.
items.e: 'Seit' + duration = 'for' with present perfect. 'She has been able to play the piano for three years.'
items.ed: 'Seit' + Dauer = 'for' mit Present Perfect. 'She has been able to play the piano for three years.'

items.p: Translate into English: 'Nächstes Jahr werde ich allein mit dem Zug fahren können.'
items.c: Next year, I will be able to travel by train alone.
a: Next year, I will be able to travel by train alone.
Next year I will be able to travel by train alone.
I will be able to travel by train alone next year.
Next year, I will be able to take the train alone.
items.h: 'Werde ... können' = will be able to.
items.hd: 'Werde ... können' = will be able to.
items.e: 'Werde können' (future ability) = will be able to. 'I will be able to travel by train alone.'
items.ed: 'Werde können' (zukünftige Fähigkeit) = will be able to. 'I will be able to travel by train alone.'

items.p: Put the words in order: will / able / be / she / to / speak / French / fluently
items.c: She will be able to speak French fluently.
a: She will be able to speak French fluently.
She will be able to speak French fluently
items.h: Subject first, then will + be + able + to + verb.
items.hd: Subjekt zuerst, dann will + be + able + to + Verb.
items.e: Word order: Subject + will be able to + base verb + object. 'She will be able to speak French fluently.'
items.ed: Wortstellung: Subjekt + will be able to + Grundform + Objekt. 'She will be able to speak French fluently.'

items.p: Put the words in order: has / he / swim / been / to / able / since / July
items.c: He has been able to swim since July.
a: He has been able to swim since July.
He has been able to swim since July
items.h: Subject + has + been + able + to + verb + since + time.
items.hd: Subjekt + has + been + able + to + Verb + since + Zeit.
items.e: Word order: He has been able to swim since July. Present perfect ability with 'since'.
items.ed: Wortstellung: He has been able to swim since July. Present-Perfect-Fähigkeit mit 'since'.

items.p: Match each sentence beginning with the correct ending. 1: With more practice, you 2: She has been able to 3: In ten years, people 4: I haven't been able to 5: After the course, we
ds: a: ride a bike since she was seven.
b: will be able to speak Italian.
c: will be able to fly to Mars.
d: will be able to play the song.
e: sleep well for a week.
items.h: Match future situations with 'will be able to' and ongoing abilities with 'has/have been able to'.
items.hd: Ordne zukünftige Situationen 'will be able to' und andauernde Fähigkeiten 'has/have been able to' zu.
items.e: 1→d (future), 2→a (present perfect, since), 3→c (future), 4→e (present perfect negative), 5→b (future, after course).
items.ed: 1→d (Zukunft), 2→a (Present Perfect, since), 3→c (Zukunft), 4→e (Present Perfect negativ), 5→b (Zukunft, nach dem Kurs).

items.p: Your friend broke her leg last month. She just told you she walked without crutches today for the first time. Which response fits?
items.c: That's great! So you've been able to walk without crutches since today!
a: That's great! So you've been able to walk without crutches since today!
ds: That's great! So you will can walk without crutches!
That's great! So you will be able to walking without crutches!
That's great! So you could walk without crutches since today!
items.h: A new ability that started today and continues = present perfect with 'since'.
items.hd: Eine neue Fähigkeit, die heute begann und andauert = Present Perfect mit 'since'.
items.e: The ability started today and continues, so use present perfect: 'have been able to walk since today'.
items.ed: Die Fähigkeit begann heute und dauert an, also verwende Present Perfect: 'have been able to walk since today'.

items.p: I ___ (wait) for the bus for twenty minutes.
a: have been waiting
've been waiting
items.h: 'For twenty minutes' shows duration. The waiting is still happening. Use have/has + been + -ing.
items.hd: 'For twenty minutes' zeigt Dauer. Das Warten geht noch weiter. Verwende have/has + been + -ing.
items.e: 'For' + duration + still happening = present perfect continuous. 'I have been waiting for twenty minutes.'
items.ed: 'For' + Dauer + dauert noch an = Present Perfect Continuous. 'I have been waiting for twenty minutes.'

items.p: She ___ (learn) French since September.
a: has been learning
's been learning
items.h: 'Since September' = a starting point. The learning is still continuing. 'She' takes 'has'.
items.hd: 'Since September' = ein Startpunkt. Das Lernen dauert noch an. 'She' braucht 'has'.
items.e: 'Since' + point in time + still happening = present perfect continuous. 'She' takes 'has been learning'.
items.ed: 'Since' + Zeitpunkt + dauert noch an = Present Perfect Continuous. 'She' braucht 'has been learning'.

items.p: They ___ (play) football for two hours. They must be tired!
a: have been playing
've been playing
items.h: 'For two hours' shows duration of an activity up to now. Use present perfect continuous.
items.hd: 'For two hours' zeigt die Dauer einer Aktivität bis jetzt. Verwende Present Perfect Continuous.
items.e: 'For two hours' + result visible now (tiredness) = present perfect continuous. 'They have been playing.'
items.ed: 'For two hours' + Ergebnis jetzt sichtbar (Müdigkeit) = Present Perfect Continuous. 'They have been playing.'

items.p: How long ___ you ___ (study) for the test?
a: have ... been studying
have you been studying
items.h: 'How long' asks about duration of something still happening. Use present perfect continuous.
items.hd: 'How long' fragt nach der Dauer von etwas, das noch andauert. Verwende Present Perfect Continuous.
items.e: 'How long have you been studying?' asks about the duration of an ongoing activity.
items.ed: 'How long have you been studying?' fragt nach der Dauer einer andauernden Aktivität.

items.p: My dad ___ (paint) the house since last weekend. He still hasn't finished!
a: has been painting
's been painting
items.h: 'Since last weekend' + 'still hasn't finished' = the action continues. 'My dad' is 3rd person singular.
items.hd: 'Since last weekend' + 'still hasn't finished' = die Handlung dauert an. 'My dad' ist 3. Person Einzahl.
items.e: 'Since last weekend' + still ongoing = present perfect continuous. 'My dad' (he) takes 'has been painting'.
items.ed: 'Since last weekend' + dauert noch an = Present Perfect Continuous. 'My dad' (er) braucht 'has been painting'.

items.p: It ___ (rain) all day. The streets are completely flooded.
a: has been raining
's been raining
items.h: 'All day' shows duration. The result (flooded streets) is visible now. 'It' takes 'has'.
items.hd: 'All day' zeigt Dauer. Das Ergebnis (überschwemmte Straßen) ist jetzt sichtbar. 'It' braucht 'has'.
items.e: Duration ('all day') with visible result now = present perfect continuous. 'It has been raining all day.'
items.ed: Dauer ('all day') mit jetzt sichtbarem Ergebnis = Present Perfect Continuous. 'It has been raining all day.'

items.p: Your friend looks exhausted. Which sentence explains why?
items.c: She has been running for an hour.
a: She has been running for an hour.
ds: She has run for an hour.
She is running for an hour.
items.h: Which tense shows an activity that has been in progress and explains a current result?
items.hd: Welche Zeitform zeigt eine Aktivität, die andauerte und ein aktuelles Ergebnis erklärt?
items.e: Present perfect continuous links the ongoing activity (running) to the current result (exhaustion).
items.ed: Present Perfect Continuous verbindet die andauernde Aktivität (Laufen) mit dem aktuellen Ergebnis (Erschöpfung).

items.p: Choose the correct question to ask about the duration of an ongoing activity.
items.c: How long have you been learning English?
a: How long have you been learning English?
ds: How long do you learn English?
How long are you learning English?
How long did you learn English?
items.h: You want to know about an activity that started in the past and is still going on.
items.hd: Du willst etwas über eine Aktivität wissen, die in der Vergangenheit begann und noch andauert.
items.e: 'How long have you been learning?' is the present perfect continuous question for ongoing duration.
items.ed: 'How long have you been learning?' ist die Present-Perfect-Continuous-Frage für andauernde Dauer.

items.p: Which word completes the sentence correctly? 'I've been reading this book ___ two weeks.'
a: for
items.h: 'Two weeks' is a duration (period of time). Which preposition goes with durations?
items.hd: 'Two weeks' ist eine Dauer (Zeitspanne). Welche Präposition passt zu Zeitspannen?
items.e: Use 'for' with durations (for two weeks, for three hours) and 'since' with points in time (since Monday).
items.ed: Verwende 'for' mit Zeitspannen (for two weeks, for three hours) und 'since' mit Zeitpunkten (since Monday).

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She have been working here since 2020.
a: has been working
She has been working here since 2020.
She has been working here since 2020
items.h: 'She' is 3rd person singular. Which auxiliary does it need?
items.hd: 'She' ist 3. Person Einzahl. Welches Hilfsverb braucht sie?
items.e: 'She' takes 'has', not 'have'. Correct: 'She has been working here since 2020.'
items.ed: 'She' braucht 'has', nicht 'have'. Richtig: 'She has been working here since 2020.'

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: We have been waiting since three hours.
a: for three hours
We have been waiting for three hours.
We have been waiting for three hours
items.h: 'Three hours' is a duration, not a point in time. Which preposition goes with durations?
items.hd: 'Three hours' ist eine Zeitspanne, kein Zeitpunkt. Welche Präposition passt zu Zeitspannen?
items.e: Use 'for' with durations (three hours) and 'since' with points in time (since 3 o'clock). Austrian German 'seit' maps to both!
items.ed: Verwende 'for' mit Zeitspannen (three hours) und 'since' mit Zeitpunkten (since 3 o'clock). Deutsch 'seit' wird zu beidem!

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: He has been play tennis for two hours.
a: been playing
has been playing
He has been playing tennis for two hours.
He has been playing tennis for two hours
items.h: What form does the main verb need in the present perfect continuous?
items.hd: Welche Form braucht das Hauptverb im Present Perfect Continuous?
items.e: Present perfect continuous: have/has + been + verb-ING. 'Play' must become 'playing'.
items.ed: Present Perfect Continuous: have/has + been + Verb-ING. 'Play' muss zu 'playing' werden.

items.p: You notice your friend's eyes are red. Ask how long they've been crying: 'How long ___?'
a: have you been crying
items.h: Ask about duration of an ongoing action. Use present perfect continuous question form.
items.hd: Frage nach der Dauer einer andauernden Handlung. Verwende die Present-Perfect-Continuous-Frageform.
items.e: How long + have/has + subject + been + -ing? 'How long have you been crying?'
items.ed: How long + have/has + Subjekt + been + -ing? 'How long have you been crying?'

items.p: Your brother started cooking at 4 pm. It's now 6 pm. He's still cooking. Complete: 'My brother ___ for two hours.'
items.c: has been cooking for two hours
a: has been cooking for two hours
has been cooking
items.h: The cooking started at 4 pm and is still going on. Use present perfect continuous + for.
items.hd: Das Kochen begann um 16 Uhr und dauert noch an. Verwende Present Perfect Continuous + for.
items.e: Started in the past, still going on, emphasis on duration = present perfect continuous with 'for'.
items.ed: In der Vergangenheit begonnen, dauert noch an, Betonung auf Dauer = Present Perfect Continuous mit 'for'.

items.p: Translate into English: 'Wir warten seit einer Stunde auf den Bus.'
items.c: We have been waiting for the bus for an hour.
a: We have been waiting for the bus for an hour.
We've been waiting for the bus for an hour.
items.h: 'Seit einer Stunde' = a duration → use 'for' (not 'since'). The waiting is still happening.
items.hd: 'Seit einer Stunde' = eine Zeitspanne → verwende 'for' (nicht 'since'). Das Warten dauert noch an.
items.e: German 'seit' can be 'for' (duration) or 'since' (point in time). 'Eine Stunde' is a duration → 'for an hour'.
items.ed: Deutsch 'seit' kann 'for' (Dauer) oder 'since' (Zeitpunkt) sein. 'Eine Stunde' ist eine Dauer → 'for an hour'.

items.p: Translate into English: 'Er lernt seit September Gitarre.'
items.c: He has been learning guitar since September.
a: He has been learning guitar since September.
He has been learning the guitar since September.
He's been learning guitar since September.
items.h: 'Seit September' = a point in time → use 'since'. The learning continues.
items.hd: 'Seit September' = ein Zeitpunkt → verwende 'since'. Das Lernen dauert an.
items.e: 'September' is a point in time → use 'since'. Ongoing action = present perfect continuous.
items.ed: 'September' ist ein Zeitpunkt → verwende 'since'. Andauernde Handlung = Present Perfect Continuous.

items.p: Put the words in order: been / has / she / reading / since / morning / the
items.c: She has been reading since the morning.
a: She has been reading since the morning.
She has been reading since the morning
items.h: Subject + has + been + verb-ing + since + time.
items.hd: Subjekt + has + been + Verb-ing + since + Zeit.
items.e: Word order: She has been reading since the morning. Present perfect continuous with 'since'.
items.ed: Wortstellung: She has been reading since the morning. Present Perfect Continuous mit 'since'.

items.p: Put the words in order: long / how / have / been / they / for / waiting / the / concert
items.c: How long have they been waiting for the concert?
a: How long have they been waiting for the concert?
How long have they been waiting for the concert
items.h: Start with 'How long', then have + subject + been + verb-ing.
items.hd: Beginne mit 'How long', dann have + Subjekt + been + Verb-ing.
items.e: Question: How long + have + subject + been + -ing? 'How long have they been waiting for the concert?'
items.ed: Frage: How long + have + Subjekt + been + -ing? 'How long have they been waiting for the concert?'

items.p: Match the sentence with the correct time expression. 1: I've been studying 2: She's been dancing 3: We've been living here 4: He's been sleeping 5: They've been travelling
items.h: Use 'for' with durations (hours, weeks) and 'since' with points in time (Monday, 2018).
items.hd: Verwende 'for' mit Zeitspannen (Stunden, Wochen) und 'since' mit Zeitpunkten (Montag, 2018).
items.e: 1→c (for three hours), 2→b (since she was four), 3→e (since 2018), 4→a (for ten hours), 5→d (since last Monday).
items.ed: 1→c (for three hours), 2→b (since she was four), 3→e (since 2018), 4→a (for ten hours), 5→d (since last Monday).

items.p: Your hands are covered in paint. Someone asks what you've been doing. Which answer fits?
items.c: I've been painting my room all afternoon.
a: I've been painting my room all afternoon.
ds: I painted my room all afternoon.
I paint my room all afternoon.
I'm painting my room all afternoon.
items.h: The paint on your hands is evidence of what you've been doing. Which tense links activity to current evidence?
items.hd: Die Farbe an deinen Händen ist Beweis für das, was du getan hast. Welche Zeitform verbindet Aktivität mit aktuellem Beweis?
items.e: Present perfect continuous explains why your hands are dirty now — the painting has been going on.
items.ed: Present Perfect Continuous erklärt, warum deine Hände jetzt schmutzig sind — das Malen hat andauert.

items.p: Pizza ___ (eat) all over the world.
a: is eaten
items.h: 'Pizza' is singular. For a general fact, use present simple passive: is/are + past participle.
items.hd: 'Pizza' ist Einzahl. Für eine allgemeine Tatsache verwende Present Simple Passive: is/are + Past Participle.
items.e: Present simple passive for a general fact: 'Pizza is eaten all over the world.' Singular = is + past participle.
items.ed: Present Simple Passive für eine allgemeine Tatsache: 'Pizza is eaten all over the world.' Einzahl = is + Past Participle.

items.p: These cars ___ (make) in Germany.
a: are made
items.h: 'These cars' is plural. Use the plural form of 'be' + past participle.
items.hd: 'These cars' ist Mehrzahl. Verwende die Mehrzahlform von 'be' + Past Participle.
items.e: Present simple passive, plural subject: 'These cars are made in Germany.' Plural = are + past participle.
items.ed: Present Simple Passive, Mehrzahl-Subjekt: 'These cars are made in Germany.' Mehrzahl = are + Past Participle.

items.p: The school ___ (build) in 1965.
a: was built
items.h: 'In 1965' tells you this is past tense. 'The school' is singular.
items.hd: 'In 1965' zeigt, dass es Vergangenheit ist. 'The school' ist Einzahl.
items.e: Past simple passive: was/were + past participle. 'The school was built in 1965.' Singular = was.
items.ed: Past Simple Passive: was/were + Past Participle. 'The school was built in 1965.' Einzahl = was.

items.p: Millions of emails ___ (send) every day.
a: are sent
items.h: 'Millions of emails' is plural. 'Every day' tells you it's a general fact.
items.hd: 'Millions of emails' ist Mehrzahl. 'Every day' zeigt, dass es eine allgemeine Tatsache ist.
items.e: Present simple passive for a regular fact: 'Millions of emails are sent every day.' Plural = are + sent.
items.ed: Present Simple Passive für eine regelmäßige Tatsache: 'Millions of emails are sent every day.' Mehrzahl = are + sent.

items.p: The Olympic Games ___ (hold) every four years. The last ones ___ (hold) in Paris.
a: are held ... were held
items.h: First gap: general fact (present passive). Second gap: completed event in the past (past passive).
items.hd: Erste Lücke: allgemeine Tatsache (Present Passive). Zweite Lücke: abgeschlossenes Ereignis (Past Passive).
items.e: 'Every four years' = present passive (are held). 'The last ones' = past passive (were held).
items.ed: 'Every four years' = Present Passive (are held). 'The last ones' = Past Passive (were held).

items.p: Thousands of trees ___ (plant) in the park last spring. Now new flowers ___ (plant) there every year.
a: were planted ... are planted
items.h: First gap: 'last spring' = past. Second gap: 'every year' = general fact.
items.hd: Erste Lücke: 'last spring' = Vergangenheit. Zweite Lücke: 'every year' = allgemeine Tatsache.
items.e: 'Last spring' = past passive (were planted). 'Every year' = present passive (are planted). Both subjects are plural.
items.ed: 'Last spring' = Past Passive (were planted). 'Every year' = Present Passive (are planted). Beide Subjekte sind Mehrzahl.

items.p: Which sentence is in the passive voice?
items.c: Cheese is produced in many countries.
a: Cheese is produced in many countries.
ds: People make cheese in many countries.
They eat a lot of cheese in France.
items.h: In the passive, the subject receives the action. Look for is/are + past participle.
items.hd: Im Passiv empfängt das Subjekt die Handlung. Suche nach is/are + Past Participle.
items.e: 'Cheese is produced' — cheese receives the action. The other sentences are active (subject does the action).
items.ed: 'Cheese is produced' — Käse empfängt die Handlung. Die anderen Sätze sind aktiv (Subjekt führt die Handlung aus).

items.p: Which sentence correctly uses the past simple passive?
items.c: The Eiffel Tower was built in 1889.
a: The Eiffel Tower was built in 1889.
ds: The Eiffel Tower is built in 1889.
The Eiffel Tower were built in 1889.
The Eiffel Tower was build in 1889.
items.h: Past passive = was/were + past participle. The Tower is one thing (singular).
items.hd: Past Passive = was/were + Past Participle. Der Turm ist ein Ding (Einzahl).
items.e: Past simple passive, singular: was + past participle. 'The Eiffel Tower was built in 1889.'
items.ed: Past Simple Passive, Einzahl: was + Past Participle. 'The Eiffel Tower was built in 1889.'

items.p: Choose the correct passive sentence: 'Millions of litres of water ___ every day.'
a: are used
items.h: The subject is plural. 'Every day' means it's a general fact.
items.hd: Das Subjekt ist Mehrzahl. 'Every day' bedeutet, es ist eine allgemeine Tatsache.
items.e: Present passive, plural: are + past participle. 'Millions of litres are used every day.'
items.ed: Present Passive, Mehrzahl: are + Past Participle. 'Millions of litres are used every day.'

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: Rice are grown in Asia.
a: is grown
Rice is grown in Asia.
Rice is grown in Asia
items.h: 'Rice' is uncountable and singular. Which form of 'be' matches?
items.hd: 'Rice' ist unzählbar und Einzahl. Welche Form von 'be' passt?
items.e: 'Rice' is uncountable (singular). Use 'is', not 'are'. 'Rice is grown in Asia.'
items.ed: 'Rice' ist unzählbar (Einzahl). Verwende 'is', nicht 'are'. 'Rice is grown in Asia.'

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: The first computer was invent in the 1940s.
a: was invented
The first computer was invented in the 1940s.
The first computer was invented in the 1940s
items.h: The passive needs was/were + PAST PARTICIPLE. What is the past participle of 'invent'?
items.hd: Das Passiv braucht was/were + PAST PARTICIPLE. Was ist das Past Participle von 'invent'?
items.e: The passive needs the past participle: 'invented', not the base form 'invent'. Correct: 'was invented'.
items.ed: Das Passiv braucht das Past Participle: 'invented', nicht die Grundform 'invent'. Richtig: 'was invented'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: These songs is listened to by millions of people.
a: are listened
These songs are listened to by millions of people.
These songs are listened to by millions of people
items.h: 'These songs' is plural. Which form of 'be' do you need?
items.hd: 'These songs' ist Mehrzahl. Welche Form von 'be' brauchst du?
items.e: 'These songs' is plural, so use 'are', not 'is'. 'These songs are listened to by millions.'
items.ed: 'These songs' ist Mehrzahl, also verwende 'are', nicht 'is'. 'These songs are listened to by millions.'

items.p: You are writing a report about your school. Rewrite in the passive: 'They clean the classrooms every evening.' → The classrooms ___.
a: are cleaned every evening
items.h: Move the object ('the classrooms') to subject position. Use are + past participle.
items.hd: Setze das Objekt ('the classrooms') an die Subjektposition. Verwende are + Past Participle.
items.e: Active: They clean the classrooms. Passive: The classrooms are cleaned. The 'doer' (they) is removed because it's not important.
items.ed: Aktiv: They clean the classrooms. Passiv: The classrooms are cleaned. Der 'Täter' (they) wird entfernt, weil er unwichtig ist.

items.p: You are telling someone about old buildings. Rewrite in the passive: 'They built this castle 500 years ago.' → This castle ___.
a: was built 500 years ago
items.h: 'Built' is both past simple and past participle. Move 'this castle' to subject position.
items.hd: 'Built' ist sowohl Past Simple als auch Past Participle. Setze 'this castle' an die Subjektposition.
items.e: Active: They built this castle. Passive: This castle was built. Singular subject = was + past participle.
items.ed: Aktiv: They built this castle. Passiv: This castle was built. Einzahl-Subjekt = was + Past Participle.

items.p: Translate into English: 'In Österreich wird viel Schokolade gegessen.'
items.c: A lot of chocolate is eaten in Austria.
a: A lot of chocolate is eaten in Austria.
Lots of chocolate is eaten in Austria.
In Austria, a lot of chocolate is eaten.
items.h: 'Wird ... gegessen' = is eaten (passive). 'Schokolade' is uncountable → singular 'is'.
items.hd: 'Wird ... gegessen' = is eaten (Passiv). 'Schokolade' ist unzählbar → Einzahl 'is'.
items.e: German passive 'wird gegessen' = present passive 'is eaten'. Chocolate is uncountable = singular.
items.ed: Deutsches Passiv 'wird gegessen' = Present Passive 'is eaten'. Schokolade ist unzählbar = Einzahl.

items.p: Translate into English: 'Dieses Lied wurde 2020 geschrieben.'
a: This song was written in 2020.
This song was written in 2020
items.h: 'Wurde geschrieben' = was written (past passive). 'Write' has an irregular past participle.
items.hd: 'Wurde geschrieben' = was written (Past Passive). 'Write' hat ein unregelmäßiges Past Participle.
items.e: 'Wurde geschrieben' = past passive 'was written'. Write → wrote → written (irregular).
items.ed: 'Wurde geschrieben' = Past Passive 'was written'. Write → wrote → written (unregelmäßig).

items.p: Put the words in order: spoken / English / is / in / many / countries
items.c: English is spoken in many countries.
a: English is spoken in many countries.
English is spoken in many countries
items.h: Subject + is + past participle + rest.
items.hd: Subjekt + is + Past Participle + Rest.
items.e: Passive word order: English is spoken in many countries. Subject + is + past participle.
items.ed: Passiv-Wortstellung: English is spoken in many countries. Subjekt + is + Past Participle.

items.p: Put the words in order: were / the / letters / sent / yesterday / all
items.c: All the letters were sent yesterday.
a: All the letters were sent yesterday.
All the letters were sent yesterday
items.h: Subject first, then were + past participle + time.
items.hd: Subjekt zuerst, dann were + Past Participle + Zeit.
items.e: Past passive word order: All the letters were sent yesterday. Subject + were + past participle.
items.ed: Past Passive Wortstellung: All the letters were sent yesterday. Subjekt + were + Past Participle.

items.p: Match each active sentence with its passive form. 1: They grow coffee in Brazil. 2: Someone stole my bike yesterday. 3: People speak Spanish in Mexico. 4: They invented the telephone in 1876. 5: They recycle paper at our school.
ds: a: The telephone was invented in 1876.
b: Coffee is grown in Brazil.
c: Paper is recycled at our school.
d: My bike was stolen yesterday.
e: Spanish is spoken in Mexico.
items.h: Move the object to subject position. Use is/are for present facts, was/were for past events.
items.hd: Setze das Objekt an die Subjektposition. Verwende is/are für gegenwärtige Fakten, was/were für vergangene Ereignisse.
items.e: 1→b (present), 2→d (past), 3→e (present), 4→a (past), 5→c (present). Object becomes subject in passive.
items.ed: 1→b (Gegenwart), 2→d (Vergangenheit), 3→e (Gegenwart), 4→a (Vergangenheit), 5→c (Gegenwart). Objekt wird Subjekt im Passiv.

items.p: You are reading a factsheet about your town. Which sentence fits a factsheet style?
items.c: The town hall was built in 1820.
a: The town hall was built in 1820.
ds: Someone built the town hall in 1820.
They built the town hall in 1820.
A builder built the town hall in 1820.
items.h: Factsheets focus on what happened, not who did it. Which voice does that?
items.hd: Factsheets konzentrieren sich darauf, was passiert ist, nicht wer es getan hat. Welche Stimme macht das?
items.e: The passive is used when the 'doer' is unknown or unimportant. Factsheets prefer passive style.
items.ed: Das Passiv wird verwendet, wenn der 'Täter' unbekannt oder unwichtig ist. Factsheets bevorzugen den Passivstil.

items.p: If I ___ (have) a million euros, I would buy a huge house.
a: had
items.h: In the second conditional, the if-clause uses past simple, even though it's about an imaginary present.
items.hd: Im Second Conditional verwendet der if-Satz Past Simple, auch wenn es um eine vorgestellte Gegenwart geht.
items.e: Second conditional: If + past simple, would + base verb. 'If I had a million euros, I would buy...'
items.ed: Second Conditional: If + Past Simple, would + Grundform. 'If I had a million euros, I would buy...'

items.p: If she ___ (be) taller, she would play basketball.
a: were
was
items.h: In formal English, 'were' is used for all persons in the if-clause of the second conditional.
items.hd: Im formellen Englisch wird 'were' für alle Personen im if-Satz des Second Conditional verwendet.
items.e: Second conditional with 'be': If + were (for all persons). 'If she were taller' is the formal form. 'Was' is also accepted informally.
items.ed: Second Conditional mit 'be': If + were (für alle Personen). 'If she were taller' ist die formelle Form. 'Was' wird informell auch akzeptiert.

items.p: If I were you, I ___ (talk) to the teacher about it.
a: would talk
'd talk
items.h: 'If I were you' is advice using the second conditional. The main clause needs 'would'.
items.hd: 'If I were you' ist ein Ratschlag mit dem Second Conditional. Der Hauptsatz braucht 'would'.
items.e: 'If I were you, I would talk...' is a common advice pattern. Main clause: would + base verb.
items.ed: 'If I were you, I would talk...' ist ein häufiges Ratschlagmuster. Hauptsatz: would + Grundform.

items.p: If we ___ (not / live) so far from school, we ___ (walk) every day.
a: didn't live ... would walk
did not live ... would walk
items.h: Negative if-clause: If + didn't + base verb. Main clause: would + base verb.
items.hd: Verneinter if-Satz: If + didn't + Grundform. Hauptsatz: would + Grundform.
items.e: Negative second conditional: If + didn't + base verb, would + base verb. 'If we didn't live so far, we would walk.'
items.ed: Verneintes Second Conditional: If + didn't + Grundform, would + Grundform. 'If we didn't live so far, we would walk.'

items.p: What ___ you ___ (do) if you ___ (find) a wallet on the street?
a: would ... do ... found
would you do ... found
items.h: Second conditional question: What would + subject + do if + subject + past simple?
items.hd: Second Conditional Frage: What would + Subjekt + do if + Subjekt + Past Simple?
items.e: Question: 'What would you do if you found...?' Would in main clause, past simple in if-clause.
items.ed: Frage: 'What would you do if you found...?' Would im Hauptsatz, Past Simple im if-Satz.

items.p: If I ___ (can / fly), I ___ (not / need) a car.
a: could fly ... wouldn't need
could fly ... would not need
items.h: 'Can' in imaginary situations becomes 'could'. The main clause is negative with 'would'.
items.hd: 'Can' in vorgestellten Situationen wird zu 'could'. Der Hauptsatz ist verneint mit 'would'.
items.e: If + could (past of can), wouldn't + base verb. 'If I could fly, I wouldn't need a car.'
items.ed: If + could (Vergangenheit von can), wouldn't + Grundform. 'If I could fly, I wouldn't need a car.'

items.p: Which sentence is a correct second conditional?
items.c: If I had a dog, I would take it for walks every day.
a: If I had a dog, I would take it for walks every day.
ds: If I have a dog, I will take it for walks every day.
If I had a dog, I will take it for walks every day.
If I would have a dog, I would take it for walks every day.
items.h: Second conditional: If + past simple, would + base verb. No 'would' in the if-clause!
items.hd: Second Conditional: If + Past Simple, would + Grundform. Kein 'would' im if-Satz!
items.e: Correct: 'If I had (past simple), I would take (would + base).' Never use 'would' in the if-clause.
items.ed: Richtig: 'If I had (Past Simple), I would take (would + Grundform).' Verwende nie 'would' im if-Satz.

items.p: Your friend can't decide which subject to choose. What advice would you give?
items.c: If I were you, I would choose music.
a: If I were you, I would choose music.
ds: If I am you, I will choose music.
If I were you, I will choose music.
If I would be you, I would choose music.
items.h: The advice pattern is: If I were you, I would + base verb.
items.hd: Das Ratschlagmuster ist: If I were you, I would + Grundform.
items.e: 'If I were you' is a fixed advice pattern. Main clause always uses 'would'.
items.ed: 'If I were you' ist ein festes Ratschlagmuster. Der Hauptsatz verwendet immer 'would'.

items.p: Is this sentence first or second conditional? 'If I won the lottery, I would travel the world.'
items.c: Second conditional — it's an imaginary/unlikely situation.
a: Second conditional — it's an imaginary/unlikely situation.
ds: First conditional — it could really happen.
Second conditional — it happened in the past.
First conditional — it uses past tense.
items.h: The second conditional is for unreal or unlikely situations, even though it uses past tense forms.
items.hd: Das Second Conditional ist für unwirkliche oder unwahrscheinliche Situationen, auch wenn es Vergangenheitsformen verwendet.
items.e: Second conditional: imaginary/unlikely situation. The past tense signals unreality, not past time.
items.ed: Second Conditional: vorgestellte/unwahrscheinliche Situation. Die Vergangenheitsform signalisiert Unwirklichkeit, nicht Vergangenheit.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: If I would be rich, I would help poor people.
a: If I were rich
If I was rich
If I were rich, I would help poor people.
If I was rich, I would help poor people.
items.h: 'Would' cannot go in the if-clause. Use past simple or 'were' instead.
items.hd: 'Would' kann nicht im if-Satz stehen. Verwende Past Simple oder 'were' stattdessen.
items.e: Never use 'would' in the if-clause. Correct: 'If I were rich' or 'If I was rich'. This is a common L1 German error.
items.ed: Verwende nie 'would' im if-Satz. Richtig: 'If I were rich' oder 'If I was rich'. Dies ist ein häufiger Fehler durch deutsches 'wenn ich wäre'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: If she studied harder, she will pass the test.
a: would pass
she would pass
If she studied harder, she would pass the test.
If she studied harder, she would pass the test
items.h: The if-clause uses past simple (studied). The main clause needs 'would', not 'will'.
items.hd: Der if-Satz verwendet Past Simple (studied). Der Hauptsatz braucht 'would', nicht 'will'.
items.e: Past simple in the if-clause signals second conditional. The main clause must use 'would', not 'will'.
items.ed: Past Simple im if-Satz signalisiert Second Conditional. Der Hauptsatz muss 'would' verwenden, nicht 'will'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: If I were you, I would told him the truth.
a: would tell
I would tell
If I were you, I would tell him the truth.
If I were you, I would tell him the truth
items.h: After 'would', what form of the verb do you need?
items.hd: Nach 'would', welche Form des Verbs brauchst du?
items.e: After 'would', always use the BASE form of the verb: 'would tell', not 'would told'.
items.ed: Nach 'would' immer die GRUNDFORM des Verbs verwenden: 'would tell', nicht 'would told'.

items.p: Your friend is afraid of water and can't swim. Give advice: 'If I were you, I ___.'
a: would take swimming lessons
would learn to swim
would go to swimming classes
items.h: Use 'If I were you, I would + base verb' to give advice.
items.hd: Verwende 'If I were you, I would + Grundform' um Ratschläge zu geben.
items.e: 'If I were you, I would take swimming lessons.' The advice pattern uses would + base verb.
items.ed: 'If I were you, I would take swimming lessons.' Das Ratschlagmuster verwendet would + Grundform.

items.p: Rewrite as a second conditional: 'I don't have a car, so I take the bus.' → If I ___.
items.c: had a car, I wouldn't take the bus
a: had a car, I wouldn't take the bus
had a car, I would not take the bus
items.h: Change the real situation to an imaginary one. Negative facts become positive in the if-clause.
items.hd: Ändere die reale Situation zu einer vorgestellten. Verneinte Fakten werden im if-Satz positiv.
items.e: Real: I don't have a car. Imaginary: If I had a car, I wouldn't take the bus. Reverse the reality.
items.ed: Real: I don't have a car. Vorgestellt: If I had a car, I wouldn't take the bus. Drehe die Realität um.

items.p: Translate into English: 'Wenn ich ein Tier wäre, wäre ich ein Delfin.'
items.c: If I were an animal, I would be a dolphin.
a: If I were an animal, I would be a dolphin.
If I was an animal, I would be a dolphin.
items.h: 'Wenn ich wäre' = If I were (second conditional). 'Wäre ich' = I would be.
items.hd: 'Wenn ich wäre' = If I were. 'Wäre ich' = I would be.
items.e: 'Wenn ich wäre' = If I were (second conditional). Don't use 'If I would be' — that's a German interference error.
items.ed: 'Wenn ich wäre' = If I were (Second Conditional). Verwende nicht 'If I would be' — das ist ein deutscher Interferenzfehler.

items.p: Translate into English: 'An deiner Stelle würde ich mir keine Sorgen machen.'
items.c: If I were you, I wouldn't worry.
a: If I were you, I wouldn't worry.
If I were you, I would not worry.
If I was you, I wouldn't worry.
items.h: 'An deiner Stelle' = If I were you. 'Würde nicht' = wouldn't.
items.hd: 'An deiner Stelle' = If I were you. 'Würde nicht' = wouldn't.
items.e: 'An deiner Stelle' is a fixed expression meaning 'If I were you'. Use wouldn't for the negative.
items.ed: 'An deiner Stelle' ist ein fester Ausdruck, der 'If I were you' bedeutet. Verwende wouldn't für die Verneinung.

items.p: Put the words in order: were / if / you / I / would / I / the / choose / blue / one
items.c: If I were you, I would choose the blue one.
a: If I were you, I would choose the blue one.
If I were you, I would choose the blue one
items.h: Start with 'If I were you', then 'I would + verb'.
items.hd: Beginne mit 'If I were you', dann 'I would + Verb'.
items.e: 'If I were you, I would choose the blue one.' Advice pattern word order.
items.ed: 'If I were you, I would choose the blue one.' Ratschlagmuster-Wortstellung.

items.p: Put the words in order: could / if / I / fly / would / I / around / travel / the / world
items.c: If I could fly, I would travel around the world.
a: If I could fly, I would travel around the world.
If I could fly, I would travel around the world
items.h: If-clause first (If I could fly), then main clause (I would travel...).
items.hd: If-Satz zuerst (If I could fly), dann Hauptsatz (I would travel...).
items.e: 'If I could fly, I would travel around the world.' If + could, would + base verb.
items.ed: 'If I could fly, I would travel around the world.' If + could, would + Grundform.

items.p: Match the if-clause with the correct main clause. 1: If I had more time, 2: If she spoke Chinese, 3: If we lived near the sea, 4: If he weren't so lazy, 5: If I were you,
ds: a: she would get a job in Beijing.
b: he would get better marks.
c: I would learn to play the guitar.
d: I would apologise to her.
e: we would go surfing every day.
items.h: Match the imaginary condition with the logical result. All use would + base verb.
items.hd: Ordne die vorgestellte Bedingung dem logischen Ergebnis zu. Alle verwenden would + Grundform.
items.e: 1→c (time→guitar), 2→a (Chinese→Beijing), 3→e (sea→surfing), 4→b (not lazy→marks), 5→d (advice→apologise).
items.ed: 1→c (Zeit→Gitarre), 2→a (Chinesisch→Beijing), 3→e (Meer→Surfen), 4→b (nicht faul→Noten), 5→d (Ratschlag→entschuldigen).

items.p: Your friend says: 'I'm so bored at home!' Which response uses the second conditional correctly?
items.c: If I were you, I would join a sports club.
a: If I were you, I would join a sports club.
ds: If I am you, I will join a sports club.
If I would be you, I would join a sports club.
If I were you, I will join a sports club.
items.h: The advice pattern is always: If I were you, I would + base verb.
items.hd: Das Ratschlagmuster ist immer: If I were you, I would + Grundform.
items.e: 'If I were you, I would join a sports club.' Correct second conditional advice pattern.
items.ed: 'If I were you, I would join a sports club.' Richtiges Second Conditional Ratschlagmuster.

items.p: Look at those dark clouds! It ___ (rain).
a: is going to rain
