's going to rain
items.h: You can SEE the dark clouds. When there is visible evidence, use 'going to'.
items.hd: Du kannst die dunklen Wolken SEHEN. Wenn es sichtbare Beweise gibt, verwende 'going to'.
items.e: 'Going to' is used for predictions based on present evidence. We can see the clouds → it's going to rain.
items.ed: 'Going to' wird für Vorhersagen basierend auf sichtbaren Beweisen verwendet. Wir können die Wolken sehen → it's going to rain.

items.p: Watch out! That vase ___ (fall)!
a: is going to fall
's going to fall
items.h: You can see the vase is about to fall. Use 'going to' for predictions based on what you see.
items.hd: Du siehst, dass die Vase gleich fallen wird. Verwende 'going to' für Vorhersagen basierend auf dem, was du siehst.
items.e: 'Watch out!' + visible danger = 'going to'. The speaker can see the vase is about to fall.
items.ed: 'Watch out!' + sichtbare Gefahr = 'going to'. Der Sprecher kann sehen, dass die Vase gleich fällt.

items.p: She looks really tired. She ___ (fall) asleep in class.
a: is going to fall
's going to fall
items.h: You can SEE she looks tired. This visible evidence tells you what's going to happen next.
items.hd: Du SIEHST, dass sie müde aussieht. Dieser sichtbare Beweis sagt dir, was als nächstes passieren wird.
items.e: We can see she's tired (evidence) → she is going to fall asleep (prediction based on evidence).
items.ed: Wir können sehen, dass sie müde ist (Beweis) → she is going to fall asleep (Vorhersage basierend auf Beweis).

items.p: The team has scored three goals already. They ___ (win) the match!
a: are going to win
're going to win
items.h: Three goals is strong evidence. Use 'going to' when you can see signs of what will happen.
items.hd: Drei Tore sind ein starker Beweis. Verwende 'going to' wenn du Anzeichen siehst, was passieren wird.
items.e: The score (3 goals) is evidence that the team is going to win. Plural subject 'they' → are going to.
items.ed: Der Spielstand (3 Tore) ist Beweis, dass die Mannschaft gewinnen wird. Mehrzahl-Subjekt 'they' → are going to.

items.p: Oh no! The little boy is running towards the road. He ___ (get) hit by a car if nobody stops him!
a: is going to get
's going to get
items.h: You can see the boy running towards the road. This present evidence makes a prediction urgent.
items.hd: Du siehst den Jungen zur Straße laufen. Dieser gegenwärtige Beweis macht eine Vorhersage dringend.
items.e: We can see the danger (boy running toward road) = 'going to' for evidence-based prediction.
items.ed: Wir können die Gefahr sehen (Junge läuft zur Straße) = 'going to' für beweisbasierte Vorhersage.

items.p: Look at the traffic! We ___ (not / be) there on time. We ___ (be) late again.
items.c: aren't going to be ... are going to be
a: aren't going to be ... are going to be
are not going to be ... are going to be
're not going to be ... 're going to be
ds: aren't going to be ... will be
isn't going to be ... is going to be
items.h: You can see the traffic jam. Both predictions are based on this evidence. 'We' takes 'are'.
items.hd: Du siehst den Stau. Beide Vorhersagen basieren auf diesem Beweis. 'We' braucht 'are'.
items.e: Both predictions are evidence-based (traffic jam visible). Negative: aren't going to be. Positive: are going to be.
items.ed: Beide Vorhersagen basieren auf Beweisen (Stau sichtbar). Verneinung: aren't going to be. Positiv: are going to be.

items.p: The sky is getting very dark. Which prediction is best?
a: It's going to storm.
items.h: You can SEE the dark sky. Which form do we use for predictions based on what we see?
items.hd: Du SIEHST den dunklen Himmel. Welche Form verwenden wir für Vorhersagen basierend auf dem, was wir sehen?
items.e: 'Going to' is the best choice for predictions based on present evidence (the dark sky we can see).
items.ed: 'Going to' ist die beste Wahl für Vorhersagen basierend auf sichtbaren Beweisen (den dunklen Himmel).

items.p: When do we use 'going to' instead of 'will' for predictions?
items.c: When there is visible evidence for what will happen.
a: When there is visible evidence for what will happen.
ds: When we make a spontaneous decision.
When we are not sure about the future.
When we talk about scheduled events.
items.h: Think about what makes 'going to' special for predictions.
items.hd: Überlege, was 'going to' bei Vorhersagen besonders macht.
items.e: 'Going to' for predictions = we can see evidence NOW of what is about to happen (clouds → rain, tired → fall asleep).
items.ed: 'Going to' für Vorhersagen = wir können JETZT Beweise sehen, was gleich passieren wird (Wolken → Regen, müde → einschlafen).

items.p: Which sentence uses 'going to' correctly for an evidence-based prediction?
items.c: Look! The cat is climbing the curtains. It's going to rip them!
a: Look! The cat is climbing the curtains. It's going to rip them!
ds: I'm going to be a doctor when I grow up.
We're going to visit Grandma next weekend.
She's going to have a birthday party on Saturday.
items.h: Only one sentence has visible evidence in the present that leads to the prediction.
items.hd: Nur ein Satz hat sichtbare Beweise in der Gegenwart, die zur Vorhersage führen.
items.e: Only 'The cat is climbing' gives visible evidence for the prediction. The others use 'going to' for plans/intentions.
items.ed: Nur 'The cat is climbing' gibt sichtbare Beweise für die Vorhersage. Die anderen verwenden 'going to' für Pläne/Absichten.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: Look at those clouds! It going to rain.
a: It's going to rain
It is going to rain
Look at those clouds! It's going to rain.
Look at those clouds! It is going to rain.
items.h: 'Going to' needs a form of 'be' before it. What form goes with 'it'?
items.hd: 'Going to' braucht eine Form von 'be' davor. Welche Form passt zu 'it'?
items.e: 'Going to' always needs be: am/is/are + going to. 'It IS going to rain.'
items.ed: 'Going to' braucht immer 'be': am/is/are + going to. 'It IS going to rain.'

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: The baby is crying. She is going to wants her mum.
a: is going to want
She is going to want her mum.
The baby is crying. She is going to want her mum.
items.h: After 'going to', what form does the verb need?
items.hd: Nach 'going to', welche Form braucht das Verb?
items.e: After 'going to', use the BASE form of the verb: 'want', not 'wants'. No -s ending!
items.ed: Nach 'going to' verwende die GRUNDFORM des Verbs: 'want', nicht 'wants'. Keine -s-Endung!

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: Look at the score! Our team are going to loses the game.
a: is going to lose
Our team is going to lose the game.
Look at the score! Our team is going to lose the game.
items.h: Two errors: 'our team' is singular (it), and the verb after 'to' needs the base form.
items.hd: Zwei Fehler: 'our team' ist Einzahl (es), und das Verb nach 'to' braucht die Grundform.
items.e: 'Our team' is singular → 'is going to'. After 'to', use base form: 'lose', not 'loses'.
items.ed: 'Our team' ist Einzahl → 'is going to'. Nach 'to' verwende die Grundform: 'lose', nicht 'loses'.

items.p: You see your friend carrying too many books. Make a prediction: 'She ___ (drop) them!'
a: is going to drop
's going to drop
items.h: You can see the evidence (too many books). Use 'going to' for your prediction.
items.hd: Du siehst den Beweis (zu viele Bücher). Verwende 'going to' für deine Vorhersage.
items.e: Visible evidence (carrying too many books) → evidence-based prediction with 'going to': 'She is going to drop them!'
items.ed: Sichtbarer Beweis (trägt zu viele Bücher) → beweisbasierte Vorhersage mit 'going to': 'She is going to drop them!'

items.p: You're at a restaurant. Your little brother is leaning back on his chair. Warn your parents: 'Watch out! He ___ (fall) off his chair!'
a: is going to fall
's going to fall
items.h: You can see the boy leaning dangerously. Use 'going to' for an evidence-based warning.
items.hd: Du siehst den Jungen gefährlich lehnen. Verwende 'going to' für eine beweisbasierte Warnung.
items.e: The leaning is visible evidence → 'He is going to fall off his chair!' Evidence-based prediction/warning.
items.ed: Das Lehnen ist sichtbarer Beweis → 'He is going to fall off his chair!' Beweisbasierte Vorhersage/Warnung.

items.p: Translate into English: 'Schau dir die Wolken an! Es wird regnen.' (You can see the evidence.)
items.c: Look at the clouds! It's going to rain.
a: Look at the clouds! It's going to rain.
Look at the clouds! It is going to rain.
Look at those clouds! It's going to rain.
items.h: 'Es wird regnen' with visible evidence = it's going to rain (not 'it will rain').
items.hd: 'Es wird regnen' mit sichtbarem Beweis = it's going to rain (nicht 'it will rain').
items.e: When you can see evidence (clouds), use 'going to': 'It's going to rain.'
items.ed: Wenn du Beweise sehen kannst (Wolken), verwende 'going to': 'It's going to rain.'

items.p: Translate into English: 'Pass auf! Die Tasse wird vom Tisch fallen!' (You can see it happening.)
items.c: Watch out! The cup is going to fall off the table!
a: Watch out! The cup is going to fall off the table!
Careful! The cup is going to fall off the table!
Look out! The cup is going to fall off the table!
items.h: 'Wird fallen' with visible evidence = is going to fall.
items.hd: 'Wird fallen' mit sichtbarem Beweis = is going to fall.
items.e: Visible evidence (cup about to fall) = 'going to'. 'The cup is going to fall off the table!'
items.ed: Sichtbarer Beweis (Tasse fällt gleich) = 'going to'. 'The cup is going to fall off the table!'

items.p: Put the words in order: is / to / it / rain / going
a: It is going to rain.
It is going to rain
items.h: Subject + is + going to + base verb.
items.hd: Subjekt + is + going to + Grundform.
items.e: Word order: It + is + going to + rain. Subject + be + going to + base verb.
items.ed: Wortstellung: It + is + going to + rain. Subjekt + be + going to + Grundform.

items.p: Put the words in order: going / they / to / aren't / win / the / game
items.c: They aren't going to win the game.
a: They aren't going to win the game.
They aren't going to win the game
They're not going to win the game.
items.h: Negative: Subject + aren't + going to + base verb + rest.
items.hd: Verneinung: Subjekt + aren't + going to + Grundform + Rest.
items.e: Negative word order: They aren't going to win the game. Subject + be + not + going to + verb.
items.ed: Verneinte Wortstellung: They aren't going to win the game. Subjekt + be + not + going to + Verb.

items.p: Match each situation (evidence) with the correct prediction. 1: The sky is full of dark clouds. 2: The dog is running towards the cat. 3: She hasn't studied at all. 4: He's eaten three burgers already. 5: The baby's eyes are closing.
ds: a: It's going to chase it!
b: She's going to fall asleep.
c: She's going to fail the test.
e: He's going to feel sick.
items.h: Match the visible evidence with the most logical prediction using 'going to'.
items.hd: Ordne den sichtbaren Beweis der logischsten Vorhersage mit 'going to' zu.
items.e: 1→d (clouds→rain), 2→a (dog→chase), 3→c (no study→fail), 4→e (overeating→sick), 5→b (closing eyes→sleep).
items.ed: 1→d (Wolken→Regen), 2→a (Hund→jagen), 3→c (nicht gelernt→durchfallen), 4→e (zu viel essen→übel), 5→b (Augen schließen→schlafen).

items.p: Your friend is carrying a huge pile of plates. Ask a worried question: ___ ?
items.c: Are you going to drop those?
a: Are you going to drop those?
Are you going to drop them?
Is he going to drop those?
Is she going to drop those?
items.h: Form a yes/no question with 'going to': Be + subject + going to + base verb?
items.hd: Bilde eine Ja/Nein-Frage mit 'going to': Be + Subjekt + going to + Grundform?
items.e: Question: Are + you + going to + drop those? Evidence (huge pile) triggers the worried question.
items.ed: Frage: Are + you + going to + drop those? Beweis (riesiger Stapel) löst die besorgte Frage aus.
